006168-000-A.title: Red Wedding
006168-000-A.desc: Pierre, a local councillor, cheats on his wife with Lucienne, the wife of the small town's rich deputy mayor. To be true to each other, they decide to go to extreme lengths… Based on a true story, Claude Chabrol paints a grim portrait of the provincial petty bourgeoisie. With Stéphane Audran, Michel Piccoli and Claude Piéplu.

017931-000-A.title: Bad Girls
017931-000-A.desc: A rich girl, a poor girl, and a handsome architect: In off-season Saint Tropez, Claude Chabrol creates a poisonous love triangle, fraught with class antagonism. With Stéphane Audran and Jean-Louis Trintignant.

043246-000-A.title: The Man Must Die
043246-000-A.desc: When Charles Thénier's nine-year-old son is run over by a reckless driver, he swears blood vengeance and sets out to find the guilty party. A dense psychological thriller which is one of Claude Chabrol's most famous films.

044397-001-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - The Abyss (1/8)
044397-001-A.desc: We follow 14-year-old Marina Yurlova who joins her father, Cossack Colonel Yurlov, on a train to the front. Artist Käthe Kollwitz and her husband must accept their 17-year-old son’s decision to enlist. The Austrian Karl Kasser is sent to the front despite an injury and ten-year-old Yves Congar from Sedan expects France to win quickly. He feels safe in his family until German troops arrive on the doorstep.

044397-002-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - The Attack (2/8)
044397-002-A.desc: The European conflict has turned into a world war: Greece and Bulgaria, Japan and the Ottoman Empire have entered the fighting. Battles are fought in Europe, Africa, off the Falkland Islands and in the Pacific. More than 20 million men are under arms.

044397-003-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - The Wound (3/8)
044397-003-A.desc: The number of injured people is increasing day by day. There are too few doctors and nurses. Auxiliary nurses are hastily called in: 20,000 of them in the first year of the war in Germany alone. They work in extremely difficult conditions. Thousands and thousands of young men are physically injured and completely traumatised.

044397-004-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - Desire (4/8)
044397-004-A.desc: Millions of mothers and wives must endure years of separation from their sons or husbands. They are tormented by the uncertainty of whether their loved ones are still alive. The men in the trenches are told to concentrate on killing and not worry about their wives back home. The war destroys the lives and health of countless soldiers.

044397-005-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - Destruction (5/8)
044397-005-A.desc: 1916 becomes the year of horrific battles. A new kind of death machine is set in motion with submarines, aeroplanes, tanks and poison gas. On the Somme, the Western powers aim to achieve a breakthrough against the German troops, while Russia tries to put Austria-Hungary under pressure on the Eastern front. The number of victims exceeds all predictions.

044397-006-A.title: 14 Diaries of the Great War - The Homeland (6/8)
044397-006-A.desc: The soldiers' prospect of returning home is an important motive to continue fighting. In the first total war in history, a new phrase is coined: The Home Front. "Fighting" takes place not only in the trenches, but also in the factories, in fields and in schools. The burden of war is borne by soldiers as well as women, workers, children and the elderly.

044397-007-A.title: 14: Diaries of the First World War - Insurrection (7/8)
044397-007-A.desc: In 1917, unrest breaks out, affecting all sides. People across Europe revolt against living conditions and the never-ending bloodshed. There are strikes in armaments factories in England and France, industrial workers take to the streets in Germany, sailors refuse to obey orders and in Russia hunger drives hundreds of thousands towards revolution.

044397-008-A.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - In Ruins (8/8)
044397-008-A.desc: While his father is sent to Germany for forced labour, Yves Congar lives through the end of the war in his cellar. Ernst Jünger returns to Germany when revolution breaks out in his country. Too old for active service, Charles Edward Montague was asked to organise photo shoots of German prisoners for the American press. Marina Yurlova spent her 18th birthday in a Red Army prison as the civil war raged. On 5 October 1918, Elfriede Kuhr hears for the first time at school about the possibility of defeat...

044639-001-A.title: Twin Peaks - Season 1 (1/8) - Pilot
044639-001-A.desc: David Lynch's masterpiece of television is a unique drama that takes us beneath the surface of a small American town. In this feature-length pilot episode: The community of Twin Peaks is shocked by the discovery of the body of Laura Palmer, a popular high school student.

044639-009-A.title: Twin Peaks - Season 2 (1/22) - May the Giant Be with You
044639-009-A.desc: FBI agent Dale Cooper continues his investigation into the murder of Laura Palmer and uncovers the secrets of the inhabitants of Twin Peaks. He soon has to face a new threat. Episode one of season two: While Audrey is out of her depth at One-Eyed-Jack’s, Agent Cooper receives three clues from a giant.

054796-000-A.title: Diego Velázquez - Raw Realism
054796-000-A.desc: Like a detective story using the famous “Rokeby Venus” as a starting point, this documentary sets out to discover the singularity of Diego Velázquez (1599-1660), the "painter of painters", and the artistic and aesthetic revolution he provoked.

058411-000-A.title: Jerry Lewis - A Clumsy Heart Winner
058411-000-A.desc: ARTE looks back at the extraordinary career of Jerry Lewis, whose centenary we celebrate on 16 March 2026. The comedian became famous with his burlesque, often satirical gags and brilliant physical comedy. He then entered the film business where he wrote, produced and directed films in which he usually played the leading role. While much admired in his native America, he was never really taken seriously there whereas in Europe many hailed him as a genius.

062250-000-A.title: Henri Rousseau - A Modern Visionary
062250-000-A.desc: This documentary follows in the footsteps of the self-taught Parisian painter Henri Rousseau (1844-1910). His paintings, strangely realist are inspired by dreams, fantasy and childhood and have had a large influence on modern artists, including Picasso.

064565-009-A.title: Elephant Hospital in Thailand - 360° Geo
064565-009-A.desc: Considered lucky in Thailand, elephants are an integral part of Thai culture. In the north of the country, the only elephant hospital in the world has existed for over twenty years.

064565-024-A.title: Escape to a Hut in the Pyrenees - 360° GEO
064565-024-A.desc: For the past fourteen years, Brice has taken his herd of cows to graze in vast pastures in the mountains, five months out of the year. He loves to run to the point of exhaustion in the Pyrenees, across the steep slopes, ridges and rocks at 3000 metres above sea level. It's a spartan, lonely existence, but for Brice, it is the definition of happiness.

065347-000-A.title: Zubin Mehta: Conductor and World Citizen
065347-000-A.desc: Zubin Mehta has always been considered an ambassador of peace. He has given concerts in conflict zones such as in Kashmir, to try to bring India and Pakistan together, and has had a long career at the head of the Israel Philharmonic orhcestra. This documentary accompanies the music of renowned Indian conductor in his constant travels around the world.

069092-000-A.title: Mont Saint Michel - The Enigmatic Labyrinth
069092-000-A.desc: Over the centuries this famous island, floating between sea and sky, has been a sanctuary, an abbey, a fortress and a prison. But how did this architectural wonder come to be built?

070745-000-A.title: Ship of the Dead
070745-000-A.desc: Philip Gale, a young American sailor, signs onto the Yorikke, a decrepit freighter crewed by outcasts. What begins as a quest for freedom becomes a brutal ordeal of hard labour, inhumane treatment, and grim revelations – especially when a disturbing secret is found in the ship’s cargo. A gritty seafaring tale that launched Horst Buchholz’s path to Hollywood.

071420-000-A.title: David Hockney: Beyond the Pool
071420-000-A.desc: The life and work of British painter David Hockney. Since the 1960s, his paintings have shaped the contemporary vision of Californian landscapes, their specific light, and their swimming pools.

071548-000-A.title: Andrzej Wajda, a Lesson in Cinema
071548-000-A.desc: Andrzej Wajda died in 2016. A few months before he passed away, the Polish filmmaker revisited his work in an assembly room of the Wajda school, founded in Warsaw in 2002. He gives us a rich and deep self-assessment of his body of work in the twilight of his life.

073439-000-A.title: Ludivine's Demons
073439-000-A.desc: 15-year-old Ludivine does whatever she wants. She drinks, runs away from home, and smokes joints. But she goes too far when she beats up a classmate and ends up in juvenile court. She's offered two choices: either she can go into foster care, or she can go on a three-month hike through Spain to reflect.

077259-000-A.title: The Unfaithful Wife
077259-000-A.desc: Charles Desvallées lives happily with his wife Hélène and his son Michel in a villa near Versailles. After eleven years of marriage, he discovers that his wife has a lover. Although Charles wants to be a modern husband who accepts his wife's affair, he is seized by an impulsive and dangerous jealousy ...French classic by Claude Chabrol (1968).

077260-000-A.title: The Butcher
077260-000-A.desc: Hélène, a schoolteacher, meets Paul, a butcher and veteran of the French colonial wars. In their rural community, young women are turning up dead. A thriller by Claude Chabrol, starring Jean Yanne and Stéphane Audran.

077297-000-A.title: Just Before Nightfall
077297-000-A.desc: Haunted by his mistress’s death, a man finally confesses his crime to his wife... With Stéphane Audran and Michel Bouquet, Claude Chabrol weaves a moral drama with Dostoevskian overtones.

078749-000-A.title: Sex Slaves Of The Catholic Church
078749-000-A.desc: Church and sexual abuse of minors has long been an issue. But in addition to the paedophilia scandals, other revelations are shaking the Catholic Church: allegations from across the world of nuns being sexually abused by priests. In February 2019, Pope Francis acknowledged the abuse for the first time. This documentary gives an insight into an issue in the Catholic Church that has remained largely unreported.

081858-005-A.title: Apparat - Passengers: Palais de la Découverte
081858-005-A.desc: After a long collaboration with Modeselektor as Moderat, Apparat returns to making music alone. The result is ‘LP5’, set to be an ambient classic, performed live in the majestic surroundings of the Palais de la Découverte in Paris.

081858-006-A.title: Circle of Live: Neel, Sebastian Mullaert and Vril - Passengers: Melun Villaroche Aerodrome
081858-006-A.desc: Is Circle of Live a collective or a record label? Headed by Sebastian Mullaert, this Swedish project invites the cream of electro music to its concerts and places improvisation at the heart of live performances. Filmed at the Melun Villaroche aerodrome.

081858-009-A.title: Kid Francescoli - Passengers: La Seine
081858-009-A.desc: Electro artist Kid Francescoli performs his latest album Lovers, from a boat cruising down the Seine. Recorded 6 July 2020 in Paris.

083147-000-A.title: Europe: Divorce Italian Style - Square Salon
083147-000-A.desc: The anti-establishment Five Star movement and the far-right Northern League won the last election in Italy and now form a coalition government. This choice seems to reveal a deep resentment towards European institutions. Why does a founding member of the European Union appear to be distancing itself from common European ideals? A debate chaired by Annette Gerlach.

083940-000-A.title: 50 Years After Stonewall
083940-000-A.desc: June 28, 1969: A violent protest erupts when the police raid the Stonewall Inn, a gay bar in New York. The Stonewall riot was the start of the gay liberation movement. 50 years later, what does Stonewall mean for the diverse LGBTQ+ community?

089127-000-A.title: The Mysterious First Lady - Melania Trump
089127-000-A.desc: Melania Trump was born in Slovenia when the country was still Yugoslavia. Daughter of a communist mechanic from the small town of Sevnica, she had big ambitions from her early years. A portrait of a mysterious first lady and icon of conservative America produced in 2020.

089134-001-A.title: The Long War - Iran - Israel - USA - The Origins of the Conflict
089134-001-A.desc: How did Iran become the sworn enemy of Israel and the United States, despite being allies of Iran’s Shah in the 1970s? A look at shifting geopolitics and war in a troubled region.

089134-002-A.title: The Long War - Iran - Israel - USA - Dialogue or War
089134-002-A.desc: For more than forty years, Iran, Israel and the United States have been fighting a covert war. A look back at this long war which has brought hardship and choas to the Middle East. This second episode begins with the Madrid conference and the United States' wish to improve relations between Israel and its neighbours. One notable absentee: Iran, which was not invited...

090624-000-A.title: John Eliot Gardiner - The Art of Conducting
090624-000-A.desc: Founder of the English Baroque Soloists, the Monteverdi Choir and the Orchestre révolutionnaire et romantique, John Eliot Gardiner is not only one of the greatest conductors of his time, he is also a gentleman farmer. When he is not conducting, he looks after the cows and sheep on his organic farm in Dorset, some 200 kilometres from London. Portrait of a revered musician, a pioneer of historically informed performance, as he celebrates his eightieth birthday.

091179-000-A.title: Margerethe von Trotta - A Women’s Director
091179-000-A.desc: A tribute to the German filmmaker who celebrates her 80th birthday this year. Margarethe von Trotta’s work is highly respected both at home and abroad in films such as Hannah Arendt and Rosenstrasse, where female characters take the lead.

091885-000-A.title: Cigarettes After Sex in Private Paris Concert
091885-000-A.desc: In a special private concert in Paris, Greg Gonzales and his band perform from their newly released second album, ‘Cry’.

093698-000-A.title: This Is My Body
093698-000-A.desc: Jérôme was sexually abused as a child by a priest. In a deeply personal film, he tries to search for clues in his memories and come to terms with the complicity of his former social environment.

094417-000-A.title: Azzedine Alaïa - The French Couturier
094417-000-A.desc: From a Tunisian farmer's son to a fashion designer in demand in fashionable Paris: How Azzedine Alaïa (1935-2017) became France's last great couturier. He learned his profession as an assistant in the shadow of well-known names, made his breakthrough in the 1980s with his figure-hugging robes and finally achieved world fame himself. Portrait of a discreet and frugal man who proved that his craft is art.

095070-000-A.title: Pesticides: European Hypocrisy
095070-000-A.desc: Why does the European Union allow deadly pesticides banned at home to be exported to developing countries? Brazil, especially, uses large quantities of harmful pesticides imported from Europe with terrible effects on the population’s health.

095164-000-A.title: Beth Hart at the Paris Olympia
095164-000-A.desc: In an extraordinary concert, Californian singer-songwriter Beth Hart brings the house down with her charm, vocal force and energetic performance; a tour de force from the Paris Olympia.

098068-000-A.title: The Secrets of Bogs
098068-000-A.desc:
Peatlands contain more carbon than all the ground vegetation combined.
Melting permafrost peatlands can become a major source of greenhouse gas emissions,
and damaged bogs will speed up global warming. If the carbon in the peatlands is released into the atmosphere, it would be an ecological disaster. But if we harness the power of these carbon sinks, we could slow down climate change.

098789-000-A.title: Edgar Morin - Journal of a Life
098789-000-A.desc: A philosopher of complexity, a sociologist of cultural practices and one of the first to address ecological issues, Edgar Morin has been a major intellectual figure in France from the post-war period up to the present day. Through extensive archive footage, this documentary tells the story of a fruitful life dedicated to humanist thought. Morin passed away on 29 May 2026.

098815-000-A.title: Libya: Migrant Camps Scandal - ARTE Reportage
098815-000-A.desc: Tortured, murdered, or kidnapped, migrants held in Libya go through hell. Shot on location, this poignant documentary reveals a system ruled by impunity and collusion between smugglers, militias and authorities, to which Europe turns a blind eye.

100835-000-A.title: Too Hot to Work
100835-000-A.desc: Can we continue to work productively and safely in a world that is heating up? This documentary sheds light on the impact of rising temperatures on the workplace and on worker’s health.

101309-010-A.title: Christine and the Queens - Passengers: Saint Peter's church Thaon
101309-010-A.desc: A small church in Normandy turns out to be a heavenly setting for this session of Passengers, with Christine and the Queens performing tracks from last album Paranoïa, Angels, True Love.

101309-011-A.title: Worakls - Passengers: Fondation Vasarely
101309-011-A.desc: Passengers gives Worakls the keys to the Vasarely Foundation. Accompanied by an orchestra, the electro artist explores the various spaces of the Aix-en-Provence institution, revisiting some of the finest tracks in his repertoire.

101309-012-A.title: Archive - Passengers: Grandes Serres de Pantin
101309-012-A.desc: Archive unleash all the nuances of their album Call to Arms & Angels on this post-apocalyptic edition of Passengers. An epic session, filmed in an equally impressive location: The Grandes Serres de Pantin, lurking on the industrial eastern edges of Paris.

101309-013-A.title: Fever Ray - Passengers: Les Hauts Fourneaux d'Uckange
101309-013-A.desc: Beautifully grotesque, politically confrontational and utterly unique: Karin Dreijer's Fever Ray project has come a long way since the immaculate icebox of their eponymous 2009 debut. It's been a thrilling journey. Mournful explorations of parental paranoia and Scandinavian solitude crumbled under the force of a newly emerging identity straining to devour societal interdictions and accepted gender frameworks with 2017's Plunge. Splintered facets of a stridently non-binary personality were explored with 2023's Radical Romantics along with a very human need for intimacy, its most vulnerable moments still as steely and unsettling as a shark circling its prey. Experience the idiosyncratic Fever Ray vision for this exclusive Passengers session beamed in from an abandoned factory in northern France.

101395-000-A.title: Joni Mitchell - Folk, Melancholy and Rage
101395-000-A.desc: At the end of the 1960s, Joni Mitchell's intimate and fervent folk music conquered an America full of freedom. Portrait of a great woman without concession, whose insouciant voice marked her epoch.

101890-002-A.title: The Berlin Quadriga - A Franco-German Affair
101890-002-A.desc: On the Brandenburg Gate in Berlin, a statue representing the goddess of peace on a chariot carries with it the turbulent history between France and Germany. The Quadriga was sculpted by Johann Gottfried Schadow in 1793, at the request of the Prussian king. Its story mirrors the history of France, Germany, and Europe.

102300-000-A.title: Toyen: The Baroness of Surrealism
102300-000-A.desc: Long considered a marginal figure, it was not until her death in 1980, when her estate was auctioned off, that Toyen’s masterpieces finally saw the light of day. Marie Cerminova, known as Toyen, was born in 1902 in a working-class neighbourhood of Prague. She left her family at the age of 16 guided by her one true wish: she wanted the freedom to create. A portrait of an important figure of the European artistic avant-garde in the 20th century.

102805-000-A.title: Modeselektor Presents Work - A Film by Corey Scott-Gilbert, Krsn Brasko and Tobias Staab
102805-000-A.desc: Arriving in April 2021, Extended is the fifth album from Modeselektor. The German duo are accompanying this release with a ‘visual album’ in film: 'Work’ stars American dancer Corey Scott-Gilbert who transposes the energy of Modeselektor’s music into a radical performance.

103008-000-A.title: The Power of the German Constitutional Court
103008-000-A.desc: The Federal Constitutional Court was founded 70 years ago. It is one of the most powerful institutions in Germany, along with the federal government, parliament and the Federal President. Often hostile to politics and respected by Germans, it has shaped the Federal Republic. But do these judges have too much power given they don't have a democratic mandate?

103432-001-A.title: The Split (1/6) - Season 1
103432-001-A.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of ‘The Hour’. Episode 1: Hannah resists the advances of a former lover and, together with her sisters, faces her father’s return.

103432-002-A.title: The Split (2/6) - Season 1
103432-002-A.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of 'The Hour'. Episode 2: Hannah and her mother find themselves on opposite sides of a divorce case. Her father reveals the reason for his return.

103432-003-A.title: The Split (3/6) - Season 1
103432-003-A.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of ‘The Hour’. Episode 3: Oscar tries to reconnect with his daughters. Tensions begin to surface in Hannah’s relationship.

103432-004-A.title: The Split (4/6) - Season 1
103432-004-A.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of ‘The Hour’. Episode 4: Working with Christie unsettles Hannah. A new case throws her marriage into turmoil.

103432-005-A.title: The Split (5/6) - Season 1
103432-005-A.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of 'The Hour'. Episode 5: Hannah loses faith in Nathan. Rose has doubts about her wedding plans.

103432-007-A.title: The Split (1/6) - Season 2
103432-007-A.desc: The life of Hannah Stern, a divorce solicitor in London, remains turbulent. She is now in a relationship with Christie, but a new case demands her full attention. Season two premiere: The Defoes are still mourning Oscar’s death when Hannah takes on the defence of a new client. Rose and Nina are faced with an unexpected turn of events.

103432-008-A.title: The Split (2/6) - Season 2
103432-008-A.desc: The life of Hannah Stern, a divorce solicitor in London, remains turbulent. She is now in a relationship with Christie, but a new case demands her full attention. Episode 2: Hannah’s double life is taking its toll. Her sisters are going through a difficult time.

103432-009-A.title: The Split (3/6) - Season 3
103432-009-A.desc: The life of Hannah Stern, a divorce solicitor in London, remains turbulent. She is now in a relationship with Christie, but a new case demands her full attention. Episode 3: Hannah is coping with the media attention surrounding the Hansens’ divorce and rejects Christie.

103432-010-A.title: The Split (4/6) - Season 2
103432-010-A.desc: The life of Hannah Stern, a divorce solicitor in London, remains turbulent. She is now in a relationship with Christie, but a new case demands her full attention. Episode 4: The Hansen case is getting complicated. Hannah decides to be honest with Nathan, and Nina takes back control of her life.

103432-011-A.title: The Split (5/6) - Season 2
103432-011-A.desc: Between her secret relationship with Christie and a new case, the legal and family life of Hannah Stern, a London-based divorce lawyer, is as eventful as ever. Episode five: Hannah takes her revelations a step further, risking losing Nathan, whilst the pressure mounts in the Hansen case. Rose has an important conversation with James.

103432-012-A.title: The Split (6/6) - Season 2
103432-012-A.desc: Between her secret relationship with Christie and a new case, the legal and family life of Hannah Stern, a London-based divorce solicitor, is as eventful as ever. In the final episode of series 2, the Hansen case comes to a close. Christie, Nathan and Nina each make crucial decisions.

103549-000-A.title: The Red Princess
103549-000-A.desc: Who is Kim Yo-jong? As tension are high between North Korea and the United States, a focus on Kim Jong-un's little sister, Kim Yo-jong, a savvy political operator whose influence in Pyongyang is growing stronger by the day.

103554-002-A.title: In Conversation with Mia Hansen-Løve
103554-002-A.desc: Mia Hansen-Løve describes her love for the cathartic and emancipatory dimension of cinema. From her early films, the intimate and the personal unfold with sincerity and a minimalistic aesthetic. "Bergman Island" is in competition this year at Cannes.

103780-001-A.title: Live From the Halle am Berghain: Day One
103780-001-A.desc: Berlin-based label Ostgut Ton has made techno, house and experimental club music history. As the label turns 16, ARTE Concert will be streaming three gigs exclusively recorded at the Halle am Berghain. The party will begin in the large hall of the former thermal power plant with collaborative live performances, featuring the ambient and the experimental sounds the Berlin club is so renowned for.

103780-002-A.title: Live From the Halle am Berghain: Day Two
103780-002-A.desc: As techno label Ostgut Ton turns 16, ARTE Concert is livestreaming three gigs exclusively from the Halle am Berghain. On this second night, the party continues in the old boiler room. Six DJ sets will blend a variety of rhythms and styles.

103780-003-A.title: Live From the Halle am Berghain: Day Three
103780-003-A.desc: Berlin-based label Ostgut Ton is a powerhouse of techno and electro. As the label turns 16, ARTE celebrates with an exclusive livestream recorded at the Halle am Berghain. On the third and final evening of live music, enjoy seven back-to-back DJ sets that blend house, techno, electro and breakbeat.

103780-010-A.title: JASSS X Hyperaktivist - Ostgut Ton from the Halle am Berghain
103780-010-A.desc: Spanish JASSS (Silvia Jiménez Álvarez) and Venezuelan Hyperaktivist celebrate in Berlin the 16th birthday of the Ostgut Ton label.

103780-013-A.title: Marcel Dettmann X DJ Stingray 313 - Ostgut Ton from the Halle am Berghain
103780-013-A.desc: Two techno legends come together to perform a B2B set built on a deep common understanding: Berghain resident Marcel Dettmann brings together old and new musical forms and Detroit born DJ Stingray is an authority on futuristic sounds, which he calls cyborg beats.

103993-000-A.title: Kathryn Bigelow: Hollywood Adrenaline
103993-000-A.desc: For more than 40 years Kathryn Bigelow has been making films that explore male violence. With movies like Blue Steel, Point Break, The Hurt Locker and Zero Dark Thirty, the Oscar winning American filmmaker has impressed with hard-hitting moviemaking that holds a mirror up to contemporary America and the world.

104001-000-A.title: Clint Eastwood - The Last Legend
104001-000-A.desc: 70 years of career in front of and behind the camera and still active at 90, Clint Eastwood is a Hollywood legend. A look back at his unique career through a portrait that explores the complexity of the Eastwood myth.

104006-000-F.title: Cap Corse - The Mystery of the Rings
104006-000-F.desc: Off the coast of Corsica, at a depth of one hundred metres, strange circular forms were picked up by an oceanographic survey in 2011. What are these mysterious rings? How were they formed? And could they be a living organism? A team of scientists led by celebrated diver and biologist Laurent Ballesta, tried to answer these questions.

104308-002-A.title: Colin Benders - Stone Techno X Zollverein
104308-002-A.desc: Better known by his stage name Kyteman, Dutchman Colin Benders has had an impressive career on the hip-hop scene. More recently, his talent has also been noted on the techno scene.

104308-004-A.title: Vril & Rødhåd: "Out Of Place Artefacts" - Stone Techno X Zollverein
104308-004-A.desc:
From the late 1990s, Rødhåd laid the foundations for his DJ career. His perfectionist mixes of dub techno and percussive grooves earned him widespread recognition and since 2010, he has been a resident of Berlin's Berghain.
At the same time, Vril took the techno scene by storm, almost out of nowhere. As a producer, he pursues a timeless sound aesthetic that serves no trend in the genre. With his energetic live sets, he has impressed audiences across the world.
The techno giants perform from the post-industrial space of the Ruhr Museum.

104308-006-A.title: Oscar Mulero - Stone Techno X Zollverein
104308-006-A.desc: A leading proponent of underground techno since the early 2000s and a major influence in electronic music in his native Spain since the early 1990s, Oscar Mulero performs in Stone Techno at the cavernous post-industrial space of the Zollverein near Essen.

104465-000-A.title: Johnny Depp: The Love of the Bizarre
104465-000-A.desc: Johnny Depp started off as a punk band guitarist, turning to acting to help pay the bills. But his dabbling catapulted him to global fame and there was no going back. Throughout his career, Depp has had fame and infamy in equal measure. His roles are often that of the isolated anti-hero: Edward Scissorhands, Donnie Brasco, Raoul Duke in Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas, and Captain Jack Sparrow in Pirates of the Caribbean. His poetic outsider characters reveal a critical view of American society.

104533-011-A.title: John Eliot Gardiner Conducts Chabrier, Stravinsky and Debussy - With Isabelle Faust
104533-011-A.desc:
The Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France performs a programme of French and Russian works with celebrated British conductor John Eliot Gardiner and acclaimed German violinist Isabelle Faust.
Programme:
Emmanuel Chabrier - Suite pastorale
 Igor Stravinsky – Violin Concerto in D
 Emmanuel Chabrier - España
 Claude Debussy – Images

104533-016-A.title: Lalo Schifrin, Mission: Impossible - Conducted by Victor Jacob
104533-016-A.desc: Argentine pianist and composer Lazlo Schifrin has penned many famous American film and TV scores. But it is the theme to Mission Impossible that he is perhaps most famous for. The Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France perform the catchy and irregular piece, as well as many other of Schifrin’s works, conducted by Victor Jacob.

104533-017-A.title: Presences Festival 2023 - Bach / Lacôte / Chin / Robin
104533-017-A.desc: Every year, Radio France celebrates contemporary music with Présences Festival. The Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra, conducted by Kent Nagano takes over the Philharmonie de Paris to perform works by Thomas Lacôte, Unsuk Chin and Yann Robin.

104769-000-A.title: Antonio Banderas and Pedro Almodovar - The Master and his Muse
104769-000-A.desc: Antonio Banderas' raw talent caught the eye of director Pedro Almodóvar. The inspiration the director has drawn from the actor fuelled a close partnership that has produced eight films. While Almodóvar is undeniably a woman's director, he found a muse and inspiration in Banderas, who best embodies the masculine roles in his films. This documentary tells the story of their decades long collaboration.

104770-000-A.title: Brad Pitt - The Revenge of a Sex Symbol
104770-000-A.desc: Over the course of his thirty-year career, Brad Pitt has never stopped challenging his image as a sex symbol. This documentary is a look at the unexpected career of a film-loving, committed actor who has managed to break free from the shackles of movie-star glamour.

105291-000-A.title: ARTE Reportage - Rwanda: The Silence of Words
105291-000-A.desc:
"They called us: 'Tutsi! Tutsi!' They took you out of the tent and did what they wanted with you." 

Prisca, Marie-Jeanne and Concessa say they survived the Rwandan genocide and sought safety in refugee camps, only to be repeatedly raped and abused by the French soldiers sent there to protect them. All describe similar horrors: abduction from their tent, the gang rapes, and the photos of the assaults taken by the soldiers. “We naively thought that the white man was a saviour, that he would bring peace,” sighs one of the women. While French army denies all accusations of rape, the three women filed a complaint with the French courts in 2004 and 2012. The investigation is now at a standstill.

105607-000-A.title: Jungle - Passengers: Paris Metro
105607-000-A.desc: The British band Jungle represents neo-soul with broken rhythms that stay extremely danceable. They copied the sound and style from legendary artists such as Daft Punk, Chet Baker and Thom Yorke. The result is a rich, sun-drenched sound. Jungle performs for ARTE in the Paris Metro.

106170-000-A.title: Qatar - Conquering the World
106170-000-A.desc: The host of the World Cup this year is a tiny state with huge ambitions: Qatar, in little over a generation, has become one of the richest countries in the world. A focus on how the gulf state plans to reach new heights.

106265-001-F.title: 360° Reportage - Buffaloes in Laos
106265-001-F.desc: In Laos, water buffaloes are regarded as a ‘savings accounts for a rainy day’". If someone has to go to hospital, if the rice runs out or if a wedding icoming up, a buffalo is sold. But most of them are weak and malnourished. But innovative feed blocks could improve their health.

106265-012-F.title: 360° Reportage - Amazonia - The Floating Courthouse
106265-012-F.desc: Rural regions of the Amazon basin in Brazil are very hard to reach and lack infrastructure and public services. To bring law and justice to remote areas, a floating courthouse takes to the waterways for six weeks a year.

106660-000-A.title: Are Women Ill Differently?
106660-000-A.desc: Incorrect diagnoses, inappropriate treatments and persistent shortcomings in care. The medical field, whose benchmark remains the male body, seems to neglect the physiological particularities of women. Focus on the alarming gender gap in healthcare.

107200-000-A.title: Jakub Józef Orliński - Music for a While
107200-000-A.desc: Jakub Józef Orliński is an opera singer, break-dancer and model with a lot of fans. Between New York, Warsaw and Barcelona, the countertenor reveals three facets of his life as a musician.

108497-000-A.title: Fanny Ardant: A Passionate Life
108497-000-A.desc: Over her long and varied career, French actress Fanny Ardant has appeared in around 100 films with Alain Resnais, Antonioni, Costa-Gavras, Agnès Varda, François Ozon and many more. Documentary filmmaker William Karel paints a portrait of the intense and widely celebrated actress.

108558-001-A.title: Love in the 21st Century - The Child of Freedom (1/3)
108558-001-A.desc: A look at how we approach love and relationships in the modern world. In this first episode, how can we reconcile love, sexuality and freedom.

108558-002-A.title: Love in the 21st Century - Till Death Do Us Part (2/3)
108558-002-A.desc: Are we capable of living in monogamous relationships? And is the matrimonial promise of fidelity realistic? This episode explores a range of romantic relationships with people who yearn to spend their whole lives together.

108558-003-A.title: Love in the 21st Century - A Complicated Era (3/3)
108558-003-A.desc: Our era seems to hold limitless possibility for love as people explore their sexuality, their desires and their gender identity. Part three of our series exploring love in the modern world.

109374-000-A.title: Dopamine - How Smartphone Apps Get Us Addicted
109374-000-A.desc: Developed in the utmost secrecy by the tech industry, social media apps are eating up more and more of our time. Why are the apps so terribly addictive? And how can we learn better smartphone habits?

109393-001-A.title: Ploughing Tilling Fighting: A History of Peasants - The Beginning (1/4)
109393-001-A.desc: Many Europeans are probably descended from medieval peasants, but we know remarkably little about the lives of the peasantry. Often derided and misunderstood, this four-part documentary tells the story of these people at the bottom of the social scale from the early Middle Ages onwards.

109393-002-A.title: Ploughing Tilling Fighting: A History of Peasants - Rebellion and War (2/4)
109393-002-A.desc: The turbulent history of the people that work the land, from the fall of the Roman Empire to the present day. Episode 2: Around the year 1000 an economic boom comes to Europe as peasants enter the market economy. But in the 14th century famine, war, and plague put an end to economic growth and it is not long before the great peasant uprisings begin.

109393-003-A.title: Ploughing Tilling Fighting: A History of Peasants - Towards Freedom (3/4)
109393-003-A.desc: The turbulent history of the people that work the land, from the fall of the Roman Empire to the present day. Episode 3: In the 17th century, peasants began to learn how to read and write, much to the displeasure of their masters.

109393-004-A.title: Ploughing Tilling Fighting: A History of Peasants - Against the Rest of the World (4/4)
109393-004-A.desc: The turbulent history of the people that work the land, from the fall of the Roman Empire to the present day. Final episode: In the 19th century, the industrial revolution turned the peasant world upside down. The old village communities were dispersed and the agrarian population plummeted with the rural exodus.

109807-015-A.title: Dominik Eulberg - Nature One 2022
109807-015-A.desc: As well as being a top DJ, Dominik Eulberg is also a scientist and an environmentalist. He likes to include sounds of nature into his techno music.

109820-000-A.title: Grandbrothers from Cologne Cathedral
109820-000-A.desc: Piano/electric duo Grandbrothers perform at Cologne Cathedral to celebrate the 700th anniversary of its consecration, in a spectacular show featuring their ‘augmented’ piano and light show.

109825-000-A.title: Ode to the Sun - An Art History
109825-000-A.desc: The sun has captured the imagination of human beings since the dawn of time with its representation contrasting in different civilisations. A focus on the star at the centre of our solar system and its depiction in art throughout history.

110119-005-A.title: Ellen Allien - Stone Techno 2022
110119-005-A.desc: The DJ, musician, producer and label manager is one of the cult figures of her generation. Her characteristic sounds, syncopated beats and vocal components make Ellen Allien a giant of the Berlin techno scene.

110119-006-A.title: KiNK live - Stone Techno 2022
110119-006-A.desc: Bulgarian DJ and techno producer KiNK is adored for his dynamic and spontaneous sets. Both playful and experimental, his sets brilliantly alternate house, techno and acid, peppered with jazzy improvisations. Electro sounds that evoke the characteristic frenzy of a rave.

110119-009-A.title: Ben Sims B2B Oscar Mulero - Stone Techno 2022
110119-009-A.desc:
Ben Sims is one of the most talented DJs and producers on the techno scene with a 25-year career. Steeped in funk, groove and electro, he is always keen to evolve. The British artist has collaborated on remixes with Jeff Mills, Robert Hood and Kevin Saunderson. He is also famous for his electro sets where he mixes simultaneously with three or even five decks.
Oscar Mulero is a prolific Spanish DJ. In the early 90s, his influence on dance music in Spain was decisive. Since the 2000s, his productions have inspired the scene at home and abroad.

110119-012-A.title: Dax J - Stone Techno 2022
110119-012-A.desc: Dax J grew up in the UK an avid fan of jungle, drum 'n' bass and garage. This London DJ has made it from pirate radio to magazine covers and from backroom parties to headlining at every major techno institution around the world. His technical ability has made him a unique presence in modern dance music.

110190-002-A.title: Deep Thought - In Conversation with Lea Ypi
110190-002-A.desc: Swiss journalist Yves Kugelmann travels with philosopher Lea Ypi to her home country, Albania. She grew up in the coastal city of Durres. In this place laden with memory, they reflect on "freedom" in philosophy, history, and politics.

110190-004-A.title: Deep Thought - In Conversation with Philipp Blom
110190-004-A.desc: "We are currently learning that we are not the rulers of this universe. We are not the masters of creation. We cannot subjugate the Earth," says Vienna-based philosopher Philipp Blom. And he immediately asks, "Then who are we?" In conversation with Yves Kugelmann, Philipp Blom engages in a deep, thought-provoking exploration.

110190-005-A.title: Deep Thought - An Interview with Lech Wałęsa
110190-005-A.desc: Lech Wałęsa, former Polish trade unionist and co-founder of the Solidarność movement became President of Poland after the fall of communism. He talks to Yves Kugelmann about the current state of Europe.

110192-001-A.title: European Emigration: Forgotten Stories (1/3) - Into A New World
110192-001-A.desc: Between 1618 and 1748, thousands of German-speaking people fled to North America and Eastern Europe to escape famine, disease and religious persecution. A huge refugee camp was established in London. European rulers began to compete for new settlers, making refugees a political weapon.

110192-002-A.title: European Emigration: Forgotten Stories (2/3) - Promised Lands - 1750-1848
110192-002-A.desc: By 1750, the Promised Land in North America had already attracted almost 100,000 German-speaking emigrants. Without money for the long crossing, new arrivals had to work as indentured servants, ending up in the Brazilian jungle or in the French colony of Algiers. A few decades later, German-speaking immigrants contributed to the industrialisation of the USA.

110192-003-A.title: European Emigration: Forgotten Stories (3/3) - Between Two Worlds 1848 - 1933
110192-003-A.desc: After the revolutions of 1848, one million Germans emigrated to the USA, but came under suspicion of espionage during the First World War. Brazil remained open to German immigration and the largest national group of the Nazi Part was formed there. Germany’s crimes during the Nazi period destroyed the relationship between host countries and their German-speaking population.

110229-000-A.title: Do You Know Kafka?
110229-000-A.desc: A mysterious, strange, and reclusive individual, a humourless writer, but also a 20th century visionary. Do these stereotypes reflect the real Franz Kafka? This documentary tries to find out who Kafka really was and reinterprets the life and work of perhaps the greatest writer in the German language.

110284-000-A.title: Edelweiss Pirates - Teenagers Against Hitler
110284-000-A.desc: Cologne, Autumn 1944: In the bombed-out city, a small group of young people put up armed resistance against the Nazi regime. Bartholomäus Schink, Franz Rheinberger and Fritz Theilen belong to the so-called "Edelweiss Pirates", an originally apolitical youth movement that emerged in the late 1930s and was persecuted by the Nazis.

110288-000-A.title: Sunken Warships in the North Sea - Danger on the Seabed
110288-000-A.desc: Around 10,000 wrecks lie at the bottom of the North Sea, many of them warships from the twentieth century’s world wars. They represent a clear danger to human life and the environment that Europe is only beginning to face up to. Corrosion releases poisonous substances which harms marine life and unexploded munition poses an ever-increasing threat.

110335-002-A.title: The Book of My Life - With Ece Temelkuran
110335-002-A.desc: Jagoda Marinić meets exiled Turkish journalist Ece Temelkuran. After publishing articles critical of the government, she had to leave her country overnight. What books have influenced her and stayed with her? What can books do against autocracy, state repression and the absence of freedom?

110335-003-A.title: The Book of My Life - With Neven Subotic
110335-003-A.desc: Ex-footballer turned anti-poverty activist Neven Subotić had the world at his feet. But it wasn't enough. He knew something was missing and turned to books to help him find meaning in his life. What he read changed his outlook, his way of thinking, and his future.

110335-005-A.title: The Book of My Life - With Diane Kruger
110335-005-A.desc: Actress, model and now children’s author, Diane Kruger tells the story of her life and how books have accompanied her most important moments.

110598-000-A.title: Lviv - A Hospital in Wartime
110598-000-A.desc: In March 2022, the war between Ukraine and Russia is raging. While Russian troops are at the gates of Kyiv, this documentary films events in a paediatric hospital in Lviv treating the victims of the conflict. A powerful portrait of a doctor’s daily life in wartime.

110654-000-A.title: Antonov - The World's Largest Aircraft
110654-000-A.desc: The Antonov AN-225 Mriya, the world's largest cargo plane and the pride of Ukraine, was destroyed in a Russian attack on Hostomel airport near Kyiv at the beginning of the invasion in 2022. With exclusive images and never-before-seen film footage, test pilots and engineers of the aviatio giant tell its legendary story.

110989-000-A.title: Steven Spielberg - Hollywood's Eternal Wunderkind
110989-000-A.desc: Over the last 50 years, Steven Spielberg has proved himself to be an exceptionally diverse and gifted filmmaker who has had unrivalled commercial success, critical acclaim and influence as a Hollywood director and produce. But his films are much more personal than many viewers may realise, expressing his childhood fear and trauma.

110991-000-A.title: Taiwan - Fear of a Chinese Invasion
110991-000-A.desc: Beijing is increasingly flexing its muscles over Taiwan, which they view as a rebel province, and want the Taiwanese to rejoin China.How do experts understand the situation in Taiwan? Is a de-escalation of the conflict still possible? And how real is the danger of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan?

111661-000-A.title: Cologne Cathedral - The French Cathedral on the Rhine
111661-000-A.desc: An exploration of Cologne Cathedral, an emblematic monument and world heritage site. The towering place of worship took over 600 years to complete. Once the tallest building in the world, its ornate facade remains a masterpiece of Gothic architecture - and a reflection of the evolution of Franco-German relations.

111664-001-A.title: How Our Brain Tricks Us (1/2) - Me and My Brain
111664-001-A.desc: Our brain uses mental shortcuts so that we can filter the countless stimuli in our environment and react to them. However, this strength of the brain is also its weakness. Using optical illusions and magic tricks, Albert Moukheiber demonstrates in the first episode of the two-part documentary that what we think is real is not always real.

111664-002-A.title: How Our Brain Tricks Us (2/2) - My Brain and Others
111664-002-A.desc: Humans are highly social beings and the influence of those around us on our perception, decisions, and general behaviour is far greater than we realise. Whether it's social stress, our tendency to conform or the so-called "wisdom of crowds" - in this episode, neuroscientist Albert Moukheiber looks at the social brain.

111671-000-A.title: Canary Islands: Addicted to Desalination
111671-000-A.desc: Sixty years ago, the Canary Islands were the first in Europe to adopt desalination of sea water to produce water suitable for drinking and irrigation. Often considered a miracle solution, is the desalination process compatible with sustainable living?

111684-017-F.title: 360° Reportage - Sea Cucumbers: Treasures of the Sea
111684-017-F.desc: For centuries, Icelanders have tirelessly fished for cod. But for some years now, another marine creature is now sought after by Icelandic fishermen: The sea cucumber, highy prized in the Asian market.

111687-001-A.title: Iron Curtain: Tales of Soviet Occupation (1/3) - Satellite States
111687-001-A.desc: How did the Soviet Union impose its communist ideology on central and eastern Europe after the Second World War? From 1945 until the building of the Berlin Wall in 1961, citizens of Poland, Hungary, East Germany and Czechoslovakia tell their stories of how their countries were occupied and transformed into communist states.

111687-002-A.title: Iron Curtain: Tales of Soviet Occupation (2/3) - Stalin's Reign
111687-002-A.desc: How did the Soviet Union impose its communist ideology on central and eastern Europe after the Second World War? Episode Two: The whole communist world celebrates Stalin’s 70th birthday in December 1949 including the ‘brother nations’ of eastern Europe. Stalinist cities rise from the ashes of World War 2 with great buildings and avenues.

111687-003-A.title: Iron Curtain: Tales of Soviet Occupation (3/3) - Uprisings
111687-003-A.desc: How did the Soviet Union impose its communist ideology on central and eastern Europe after the Second World War? Episode three: After Stalin’s death in 1953 many in the Eastern Bloc hope for a better future with mass protests emerging in East Germany.

111741-000-A.title: Pakistan: A Nuclear Power under Pressure
111741-000-A.desc: Pakistan is a highly populous and very young country, with 240 million people, one third of which are under the age of 15. The majority Muslim nation faces immense challenges: A long-running feud with neighbouring India, Islamists destabilising the country, unstable politics and severe poverty. This documentary takes stock of Pakistan past and present, to better understand this nuclear power.

111756-000-A.title: Culture War: Art as a Weapon
111756-000-A.desc: Faced with the invasion of their country, artists in Ukraine are fighting for their country through their artworks and at the frontline.

112248-000-A.title: The Art of Mark Rothko
112248-000-A.desc: Filled with colour, sensuality, drama and emotion, the hypnotic paintings of Mark Rothko (1903-1970) are vividly revealed in this portrait of the American master of abstract expressionism.

112297-000-A.title: How Ikea Plunders the Planet
112297-000-A.desc: Behind its wholesome and eco-friendly image, the Swedish furniture giant - the world's biggest consumer of wood - has some highly unscrupulous practices. An investigation into the Scandinavian multinational with an insatiable appetite for raw materials, or how Ikea plunders the planet.

112300-000-A.title: Northern Ireland: A Fragile Peace
112300-000-A.desc:
Brexit has caused old tensions to flare up in Northern Ireland and threatens a fragile peace. The introduction of controls on goods between Great Britain and Northern Ireland have angered the loyalist community, whilst some in the Nationalist community see a united Ireland, inside the EU, within their grasp.
This documentary looks at the impact of Brexit on Northern Ireland, where a violent past, a conflict that claimed 3500 lives, still haunt the present.

112302-000-A.title: Italo Calvino
112302-000-A.desc: Italo Calvino is one of the most significant Italian writers of the 20th century. He achieved international fame with his novels, including *The Baron in the Trees*. 103 years after his birth, this documentary uses partly unpublished archive material and numerous text excerpts to recount the artistic career and eventful life of this cosmopolitan author.

112331-000-A.title: Deep Thought: A Conversation with Wolf Biermann
112331-000-A.desc: The new ARTE talk format visits exceptional thinkers throughout Europe and invites them for in-depth conversations. This time, with Wolf Biermann. In Hamburg, he reflects on life in two different Germanys, how democracy is unfinished, and an unexpected turn that may lie ahead.

112561-000-A.title: The Rapper Politician - Myanmar's Zayar Thaw
112561-000-A.desc: A look back at the brief and tragic life of Zayar Thaw, wunderkind Burmese rapper who became a member of parliament alongside Aung San Suu Kyi, before being executed by the Burmese junta in 2022.

112849-000-A.title: Artists of Prehistory - The Chauvet Cave
112849-000-A.desc: The Chauvet-Pont-d’Arc Cave in southeastern France is a jewel of prehistoric art. When it was discovered in December 1994, it amazed the world with its vivid cave paintings, believed to be over 30,000 years old, amongst the oldest in Europe. Using spectacular 3D reconstructions, this documentary film provides fascinating insights into the cave, which can only be entered once a year by a team of researchers.

113217-006-A.title: Compagnie Hervé Koubi - Sur Mesure
113217-006-A.desc: Dancers from the Compagnie Hervé Koubi take over the Château d'Ormesson for Sur Mesure. This performance, a blend of dance and acrobatics, is brought to life by Quatuor Voce and Jasser Haj Youssef.

113576-000-A.title: The Hand - A Transplant Story
113576-000-A.desc: In Finland, Arja Ahtaanluoma, who has lost both arms and both legs, hopes to regain some independence with a donor hand. But will the risky transplant work?

113617-007-A.title: NDR Bigband & Omar Sosa - Elbjazz Festival 2023
113617-007-A.desc: The NDR Bigband play on home turf here at Elbjazz festival, marking their ongoing collaboration with Cuban pianist-composer Omar Sosa. 17 players in total demonstrate an uncommon versitility as they leap across a variety of styles underlined by the cosmopolitan compositions of Sosa's restless creative spirit.

113626-000-A.title: Japan: The End of Pacifism
113626-000-A.desc: Faced with rising threats from Russia, North Korea and most of all China, Japan is re-arming at an incredible rate. This documentary sheds light on the geopolitical upheavals in the Pacific region and on Japan’s history of pacifism since the second world war.

113627-000-A.title: Hunting Down Paedophiles
113627-000-A.desc: 88 million images of child abuse circulated worldwide in 2022 with at least two images of child rape being shared online every second. Social media and online video games have made it particularly easy for sexual offenders to find new victims. This documentary is the result of four years’ research in Germany, France, the UK, North America and the Philippines, amongst other countries, focusing on the international fight to track down child abusers.

113639-006-A.title: The Book of My Life - With Daniel Richter
113639-006-A.desc: Daniel Richter is one of the best-known artists of our time. He speaks to Jagoda Marinić about the books that have inspired him and accompanied him throughout his life.

113646-001-A.title: Draw for Change - India: Cartoonist Rachita Taneja
113646-001-A.desc: Freedom of expression is under threat in the world’s largest democracy. India cartoonist Rachita Taneja has used her successful feminist comic, Sanitary Panels, to criticise India’s institutions. A lawsuit against her means she could go to prison.

113646-002-A.title: Draw for Change - Mexico: Illustrator Mar Maremoto
113646-002-A.desc: Mar Maremoto is a queer artist with a punk aesthetic and boundless energy. Her work is vivid and emotionally charged, her drawings and murals striking. She explores the multiple repercussions of machismo in her native Mexico, the anguish of fat-shaming and the difficulty of not conforming to societal norms.

113760-000-A.title: Silence of the Earth - Environmental Campaigners Assassinated
113760-000-A.desc: Berta Cáceres, Aldo Zamora, and Paulino Guajajara: These three environmental activists bravely fought for the protection of forests, rivers and indigenous communities - and were murdered for it. Who were the perpetrators? And why have they gone unpunished? This documentary investigates the involvement of large corporations and governments in these crimes.

114115-007-A.title: Klaudia Gawlas - Nature One 2023
114115-007-A.desc: Polish DJ and producer Klaudia Gawlas started making her own beats at the age of 13. In 2008, she got her break after winning a Raveline mixtape competition and played at Ruhr-in-Love. International success swiftly followed from there. In 2013, she brought the techno scene to its knees with her track 'Papillon' and is now one of the best-known DJs in the world. For Klaudia Gawlas, techno is quite simply a way of life.

114115-011-A.title: Paul Van Dyk - Nature One 2023
114115-011-A.desc: Internationally-renowned DJ Paul von Dyk grew up in East Berlin. He made a name for himself on the scene with his track "For an Angel" in 1998. Since then his success has grown exponentially, with a strong collection of studio albums and remixes for the likes of U2 and Depeche Mode.

114189-000-A.title: Plastic and Marine Life - An Uneasy Coexistence
114189-000-A.desc: Plastic in our oceans is a major ecological disaster of our time. But their presence is also an opportunity for certain lifeforms: Researchers have found a dizzying array of microorganisms colonising plastics in the seas. How will their presence affect ecosystems? And could they prove a danger to humans? this documentary looks at the latest research.

114219-000-A.title: Citizens of the Reich - Inside the German Far Right Movement
114219-000-A.desc: The German Reichsbürgerbewegung (Reich citizens movement) rejects the post-war democratic order and wants to rip up the German constitution. Suspected of involvement in a 2022 coup d’état plot, the movement is growing. Who are its members? And why do they want to do away with democracy?

114585-000-A.title: Three Women from Mariupol
114585-000-A.desc: Like her mother and daughter, director Svitlana Lishchynska grew up in Mariupol, a city in eastern Ukraine that has had cultural links with Russia for centuries. Three generations of women are confronted with the upheaval of war in a film about shifting identities in Ukraine.

114586-000-A.title: The Basement
114586-000-A.desc: In March 2022, the residents of Jahidne, a village in northern Ukraine, were held captive in a cellar by Russian soldiers for almost a month. How can local people deal with this trauma? Their memories are pieced together in this film.

114590-000-A.title: Intercepted
114590-000-A.desc: Overlaying images of a devastated Ukraine with telephone conversations between Russian soldiers and their families, intercepted by Kyiv, this film contrasts ordinary Russians' view of the war and the dehumanisation engendered by conflict.

114592-000-A.title: Queens of Joy - A Queer Fight for Freedom
114592-000-A.desc: Marlen Scandal, Monroe and Aura are three Ukrainian drag queens. When Putin invaded their country, they stayed put. Aura joined the army, while Monroe and Marlen got involved on the home front. Together they organise a charity show to support the soldiers and embark on a moving journey that is both personal and political.

114617-000-A.title: 100 Metres - The Fastest Legs on Earth
114617-000-A.desc: On 4 August 2024, at least three billion people around the world will watch the 100-metre final of the Olympic Games in Paris. The highly popular discipline captures our imagination as we watch athletes go beyond what seems humanly possible. But how do sprinters keep getting faster? And will we eventually reach our limit? A deep dive into the 100 metres from the beginning of the modern Olympics in 1896 to the iconic sprinter Usain Bolt.

114626-000-A.title: The Strangest Dream
114626-000-A.desc: Polish physicist Joseph Rotblat left the prestigious team of scientists working on the Manhattan Project and devoted his life to the fight against nuclear weapons, organising the Pugwash conferences to encourage scientists to meet and reflect on the role of science in society. Portrait of a man who swam against the tide of history and was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1995.

114638-000-A.title: Denzel Curry - splash! Festival 2023
114638-000-A.desc: In the summer of 2023, the German festival Splash! celebrated its quarter-century. A ‘special edition’ with, among others, Denzel Curry, a hip-hop figure from Florida, whose underground influence has left its mark on dark rap.

114660-000-A.title: A Shepherd Family
114660-000-A.desc: Damien, his wife Claire, and their three children, look after the family flock of four hundred sheep and eighty goats, in the breathtaking landscape of the Swiss Alps. But it's not always easy to combine family life and shepherding. This film is a touching portrait immersed in the daily life of an atypical family.

114662-000-A.title: Until Memory Separates Us
114662-000-A.desc: As her grandmother suffers from Alzheimer's, the filmmaker asks her grandfather about the darker side of their family history in this very personal story.

114745-019-A.title: Carpenter Brut - Hellfest 2023
114745-019-A.desc: Carpenter Brut unleashes a thunderous mix of synthwave and metal at Hellfest 2023! The tracks on Leather Terror, Carpenter's second album, make for a shocking encounter.

114745-026-A.title: The Hu - Hellfest 2023
114745-026-A.desc: Mongolian folk metal megastars the Hu pull together an unlikely combination of throat singing, traditional instruments and ancient battle cries with propulsive grooves and danceable riffs. The international excitement and acclaim that has greeted them since their beginnings in 2016 show that it works. Catch them here at Hellfest 2023.

114745-027-A.title: Faun - Hellfest 2023
114745-027-A.desc: The title of their last album says it all really: Pagan. Think hurdy-gurdies, stone circles and pixie-led minstrels as renowned German pagan folk band Faun take the stage at Hellfest.

114775-000-A.title: Heavy Metal Kingdom - The New wave of British Heavy Metal
114775-000-A.desc: Amid the social strife of 1970s Britain emerged NWOBHM, the New Wave of British Heavy Metal. New bands from ordinary British towns and suburbs like Iron Maiden, Def Leppard; Saxon, and Girlschool changed the face of amplified music and created a musical and cultural revolution. This is the story of Heavy Metal Kingdom.

114846-001-A.title: Howard Shore Weekend - The Lord of the Rings
114846-001-A.desc: A concert dedicated to Canadian film composer Howard Shore, famous for his scores to Peter Jackson’s The Lord of the Rings and David Cronenberg’s The Fly. With the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France.

114846-002-A.title: Howard Shore Weekend - Eastern Promises
114846-002-A.desc: A second concert dedicated to the music of Howard Shore, composer of famous film scores such as David Cronenberg’s Eastern Promises.  Bastien Stil conducts the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France.

114846-007-A.title: Mikko Franck conducts Webern and Mendelssohn - With María Dueñas
114846-007-A.desc: Mikko Franck, the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France and violinist María Dueñas join forces for a concert dedicated to Anton Webern and Felix Mendelssohn. An encounter with Mendelssohn's magnificent Violin Concerto at its centre.

114846-008-A.title: New Year Concert with the Orchestre National de France - Conducted by Stéphane Denève
114846-008-A.desc: Conducted by Stéphane Denève with the talent of pianist Lise de la Salle, the Orchestre National de France celebrates the New Year through Bernstein, Gershwin, Bizet and Offenbach.

114846-013-A.title: Rufus Wainwright: Dream Requiem - Featuring Meryl Streep
114846-013-A.desc: Rufus Wainwright has composed a requiem in memory of those lost to the Covid-19 pandemic. This ode to life uses the full power of the Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra and features actress Meryl Streep.

114846-015-A.title: Mikhaïl Pletnev with the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France - Rachmaninov's concertos: No.1 and No.2
114846-015-A.desc: Mikhail Pletnev and the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France embark on an ambitious musical adventure: performing all of Rachmaninov's piano concertos. They begin with concertos Nos. 1 and 2, conducted by Dima Slobodeniouk.

114846-016-A.title: Mikhaïl Pletnev and the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France - Rachmaninov's concertos: Nos.3 and 4
114846-016-A.desc: Continuation and conclusion of Rachmaninov's complete piano concertos: Conducted by Dima Slobodeniouk, Mikhail Pletnev and the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France perform concertos Nos. 3 and 4.

114846-017-A.title: Barbara Hannigan - Ligeti and Stravinsky
114846-017-A.desc: Barbara Hannigan conducts the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France performing works by György Ligeti and Igor Stravinski.

114846-019-A.title: Mikko Franck - With Marie-Nicole Lemieux
114846-019-A.desc: Mikko Franck invites counter-alto Marie-Nicole Lemieux, the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France and the Maîtrise de Radio France to perform works by Dutilleux, Mahler and Strauss.

114846-020-A.title: Josman - Hip Hop Symphony
114846-020-A.desc: At the invitation of French radio station Mouv', heavyweight rapper Josman joins forces with the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France to revisit the most emblematic tracks in his repertoire. Josman as you've never heard him before!

115003-001-A.title: Mysterious Treasures of Nigeria - Nok Terracotta Sculptures
115003-001-A.desc: Archaeologists in Nigeria study the remains of little-understood civilisations. In this episode: the Nok civilisation produced fascinating terracotta figurines, rarely found in their original archaeological setting.

115003-002-A.title: Mysterious Treasures of Nigeria - Ile-Ife Bronzes
115003-002-A.desc: Archaeologists in Nigeria study the remains of little-understood civilisations. In the south-west of Nigeria the ancient Yoruba city of Ilé-Ifẹ̀ holds many treasures from the Middle Ages when the metropolis was a centre for artistic production.

115016-000-A.title: A Year Amongst Wolves
115016-000-A.desc: A photographer and a painter hole up in a tiny cabin in the no man’s land between Russia and Finland. This wilderness, where humans seldom tread, is wolf territory. They want to observe these magnificent beasts in their natural habitat over a year.

115018-000-A.title: Ice Grave
115018-000-A.desc: Three men, a hydrogen balloon and the dream of the North Pole: In 1897, the Andrée expedition disappeared without a trace. It was not until 1930 that their preserved bodies, diaries and photographs were discovered on the remote Norwegian island of Kvitøya. Ice Grave delves into the still unsolved mystery of the mortal failure of a dangerous expedition.

115029-000-A.title: Living Among the Living
115029-000-A.desc: In times of environmental crises and human beings’ growing alienation from nature, some thinkers are questioning the prevailing anthropocentric view: What space for animals in our very human world? This documentary features human philosophers Viciane Despret and Baptiste Morizot as well as the dog Alba and the wild horse Stipa.

115065-000-A.title: Israel: Extremists in Power
115065-000-A.desc: The current Israeli government is the most right-wing in the nation’s history. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is in coalition with far-right parties whose racist and violent discourses are poisoning Israeli politics. A focus on two extremist ministers: Minister of National Security Itamar ben-Gvir and Minister of Finance Bezalel Smotrich.

115485-000-A.title: Far-Right Terrorism (1/2)
115485-000-A.desc: Often underestimated, far-right terrorism is a real and present threat in Europe and the world. This investigation goes deep into the world of extreme groups that could be organising major attacks.

115633-000-A.title: The Power of Microbes
115633-000-A.desc: In the last 50 years, the proportion of asthma and allergy sufferers in industrialised countries has risen from five to 30 per cent. This is likely due to the lack of diversity of microbes in our increasingly germ-free environment. This documentary look at how microbes affect our health and how we can plan urban space to renew our contact with the natural world.

115836-001-A.title: Héctor Oaks B2B Helena Hauff - Stone Techno 2023
115836-001-A.desc: Helena Hauff's strictly analog and raw style is a great choice to pair with Héctor Oaks for a special B2B hour of power. The German DJ's acid-industrial sound perfectly complements Oaks' intriguing selections that have made him one of the most sought-after names in the Spanish underground.

115836-002-A.title: Freddy K - Stone Techno 2023
115836-002-A.desc: Berlin-based DJ and producer Freddy K has been putting ravers into trance with his dynamic, fluid sets since the early 90s. His style of techno is fully immersive: A master of building from soft ecstatic sounds to hard pounding bacchanals tailor-made for the dancefloor.

115836-003-A.title: Anetha - Stone Techno 2023
115836-003-A.desc: Anetha has been leading French techno from the front for a few years now, part of a new generation of DJs in thrall to the old school. Her generous approach encompasses EBM, trance and electro, always focussed on delivering generous sets brimming with groove and rhythm.

115836-004-A.title: David Vunk - Stone Techno 2023
115836-004-A.desc: David Vunk has been on the scene for over 25 years, picking up enormous respect for his skills and experience. Also his vast range: The so-called "beast of Rotterdam" can move effortlessly from funky house to soaring techno, surfing through acid house and Italo disco in between.

115836-005-A.title: Marcel Dettmann - Stone Techno 2023
115836-005-A.desc: Berghain resident Marcel Dettmann is a heavyweight name in modern techno, a scene he has ruled since the 90s. His sound oscillates between contemporary compositions and pre-techno forms, a raw and singular aesthetic that is as powerful as it is unpredictable.

115836-006-A.title: Rene Wise - Stone Techno 2023
115836-006-A.desc: Before discovering electronic music, Rene Wise played drums just like his father, a professional Iranian drummer. These roots have had a huge influence on his compositions, where intricate polyrhythms lurk inside techno soundscapes - an endless and irresistible invitation to get moving.

115836-007-A.title: Rødhåd - Stone Techno 2023
115836-007-A.desc: An established figure in contemporary techno, DJ and producer Rødhåd's sets reflect the brutalism of his Berlin hometown and an expansive imagination. Dark beats meet science fiction soundscapes in his finely calibrated sets that perpetually push onward and upward past the outer limits of the dancefloor.

115836-008-A.title: Narciss - Stone Techno 2023
115836-008-A.desc: They might have chosen their name after the ultimate mythical egomaniac, but Narciss approaches techno without a shred of conceit. Their generous, bonkers sets draw from influences as far flung as 1950s samurai films, white-label techno records, old-skool hip hop and 90s anime.

115836-012-A.title: Dasha Rush - Stone Techno 2023
115836-012-A.desc: Russian DJ, producer and experimental sound artist Dasha Rush has made a name for herself with raw, merciless sets that get ravers sweating as they drift off into other dimensions. The punishing brutality of her sound is offset by her ability to decorate the intensity with a delicate, soaring touch.

115993-001-A.title: The Split (1/6) - Season 3
115993-001-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous production and flawless performances, the final season of this bittersweet British series reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. First episode: Hannah has filed for divorce from Nathan. But just as she is about to sign the papers, she is overcome by doubts.

115993-002-A.title: The Split (2/6) - Season 3
115993-002-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous production and flawless performances, the final season of this bittersweet British series reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. Episode two: Nathan makes a revelation to Hannah that leaves her stunned.

115993-003-A.title: The Split (3/6) - Season 3
115993-003-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous production and flawless performances, the final season of this bittersweet British series reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. Episode three: increasingly affected by the circumstances of her divorce, Hannah has an encounter that leaves her reeling.

115993-004-A.title: The Split (4/6) - Season 3
115993-004-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous production and flawless performances, the final series of this bittersweet British drama reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. Episode four: Hannah finds herself dreaming of a life in New York with Christie, whilst Nathan has doubts about his relationship with Kate.

115993-005-A.title: The Split (5/6) - Season 3
115993-005-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous direction and flawless performances, the final season of this bittersweet British series reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. Episode five: whilst the holidays are far from relaxing, they might bring Hannah and Nathan closer together.

115993-006-A.title: The Split (6/6) - Season 3
115993-006-A.desc: How can you live without each other after years of living together? With its meticulous production and flawless performances, the final season of this bittersweet British series reveals a new side to Hannah Stern. Final episode: just as he is about to embark on a new life, Nathan begins to have second thoughts.

115994-000-A.title: Julian Assange: Silenced
115994-000-A.desc:
For some he is a hero, for others he is public enemy number one: Julian Assange's revelationsthrough WikiLeaks have angered governments across the world.
With the publication of thousands of secret documents from the US military and US diplomacy in 2010, WikiLeaks led the world into a new information age. Suddenly, state secrets were out in the open and under scrutiny.
For many journalists and activists, Assange became a pioneer who used new technologies to escape censorship and reprisals. For others, he symbolised an openness that terrifies nation states and multinational corporations.
A documentary about the extraordinary story of the WikiLeaks founder.

116000-000-A.title: The Battle of Chile (2/3) - The Coup d'Etat
116000-000-A.desc: Between March and September 1973, the left and right clashed on all fronts: In the streets, the factories, the courts and the universities, Parliament and the media. The US government continued to interfere by financing those fermenting chaos. As the military conspired in Valparaiso, the middle classes encouraged the unrest.

116001-000-A.title: The Battle of Chile (3/3) - People Power
116001-000-A.desc: In the months leading up to the military coup in Chile, supporters of the socialist president Allende try to create a " state within a state " with political and charitable actions - as a counterweight to the anti-reform forces in the country, supported by the Nixon administration in the USA.

116002-000-A.title: The Battle of Chile (1/3) - The Insurrection of the Bourgeoisie
116002-000-A.desc:
Chilean president Salvador Allende wanted to modernise his country and fight poverty. But conservative groups organised a series of wildcat strikes and the White House put pressure on Chile's economy. When they realised that they could not eliminate the president by legal means, they prepared a coup d'état. 

This bold three-part work of cinema vérité captures the many voices of Chilean society in a riveting account of the fall of the Allende government in 1973.

116051-001-A.title: Mogli - Musicverse
116051-001-A.desc: Filmed at the Zeiss planetarium in Berlin, Musicverse features concerts accompanied by AI-generated projections in real time. Mogli, a versatile artist from Berlin, plunges the audience into a surprising psychedelic world.

116051-004-A.title: Ásgeir - Musicverse
116051-004-A.desc: The Zeiss Planetarium in Berlin is the host for Musicverse, a series of concerts enhanced by AI-generated visual elements. Ásgeir, an Icelandic musician with a penchant for dream pop, rains down nebulous particles in waves reminiscent of the northern lights.

116062-001-A.title: Laufey: Sounds Like Art - Monet | Munch | Signac
116062-001-A.desc: Icelandic prodigy Laufey invites you into her mesmerising world for this edition of Sounds Like Art, where music meets the visual arts. A colourful outing to the Barberini Musuem in Potsdam, home to many masterpieces from Impressionist greats like Monet, Munch and Signac.

116072-000-A.title: Ireland’s Coast
116072-000-A.desc: We journey along Ireland’s coast, revealing its rich biodiversity and magnetic charm. From Mesolithic sites to forgotten shipwrecks, the hidden secrets of the Irish coast are revealed.

116080-000-A.title: Gregory Peck - Filmstar and Gentleman
116080-000-A.desc: A legend of the golden age of Hollywood, Gregory Peck (1916-2003) led a glittering career in film working with great directors: Alfred Hitchcock, Elia Kazan, Raoul Walsh, Vincente Minnelli and many more. This documentary follows the life and career of an actor of rare charm and strong political convictions.

116675-000-A.title: The First Year - A film by Patricio Guzmán
116675-000-A.desc: A focus on the first year of the Popular Unity government in Chile, where Salvador Allende was elected as President in September 1970.

116709-000-A.title: Million Dollar Babies - The Big Business Behind Eel Trafficking
116709-000-A.desc: Across rivers, national borders and black markets, this documentary delves into the billion-dollar world of glass eel smuggling. With exclusive access to key players and rare footage, the film reveals how the global appetite for eels is fuelling an ecological catastrophe.

116733-000-A.title: Movies by Machines - AI and Cinema
116733-000-A.desc: As artificial intelligence becomes ever more sophisticated, the film industry is split between enthusiasm at what the technology can achieve and concern over the future for human workers in the industry. Will actors and actresses be replaced by machines? An overview on the coming wave of AI in cinema.

116820-000-A.title: Félix Vallotton - Colours of Desire
116820-000-A.desc: Even 100 years after his death, the art of Félix Vallotton (1865-1925) has lost none of its power and modernity. The painter confronted the viewer with his personal fears and desires. This documentary attempts to understand an enigmatic artist and his work.

116857-000-A.title: The Colours of Antiquity
116857-000-A.desc: For centuries, it was assumed that Greek art and architecture was created in pure white marble. But tiny traces of colour can be found on temples and statues from ancient Greece. Using the latest technology and experimental reconstructions, researchers are bringing back the bright pigments of the Parthenon, Delphi and Knossos and revealing a colourful antiquity full of life, expressiveness and surprising colourations.

116876-000-A.title: Mia Farrow: Between Fiction and Reality
