116876-000-A.desc: From the embodiment of female innocence in her early years to her humanitarian commitment in recent decades: Mia Farrow's life story is that of a passionate actress who was thrust into a particular role of femininity by the great directors of her time, somewhere between innocence and madness.

117159-000-A.title: Egypt: The Mysterious Tomb of the Vizier
117159-000-A.desc: Why did Ouni, the vizier of King Pepi I who ruled Egypt more than 4,200 years ago, have two tombs, at Abydos and Saqqara? This archaeological investigation looks for the answers with spectacular discoveries in excavations of ancient Egyptian mausoleums.

117219-000-A.title: Pedro Soler & Gaspar Claus: Entrañas
117219-000-A.desc: Pedro Soler and Gaspar Claus take you on a road trip: A musical film shot during a tour of the Albi area, revealing moving father/son relationships and superb musical moments.

117221-000-A.title: Hommage to the Beatles - The Analogues at Paris' Salle Pleyel
117221-000-A.desc: For one night, Dutch band The Analogues are bringing the Beatles back to Paris for this special tribute concert. Lucky concertgoers can once again return to the unique world created by the Fab Four at the French capital's famed venue Salle Pleyel.

117304-000-A.title: Charlie Winston - Meisenthal's Halle Verrière
117304-000-A.desc: Charlie Winston first came to public attention back in the 2000s with his hit "Like a Hobo." He's now performing in one of Europe's leading cultural heritage hotspots: the Halle Verrière in Meisenthal, a small village nestled in the Vosges du Nord nature park in northeastern France.

117538-000-A.title: ARTE Reportage - Hamas: Creating a Monster
117538-000-A.desc:
What exactly is Hamas? Is it the Muslim Brotherhood's instrument to keep people in Gaza under Islamic law? Has it been manipulated by Israel to discredit the Palestinian Authority and sabotage the two-state solution? Or is it a pawn of Iran to attack Israel?
In this documentary, journalist Sofia Amara gained access to key figures in order to understand Hamas and its aims, as well as to paint a picture of the current state of the Israel-Palestine conflict.

117728-004-F.title: 360° Reportage - India: Perfume Country
117728-004-F.desc: A precious material is produced in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh: attar, a fragrant essential oil. In the perfume capital of India, Kannauj, rose and jasmine petals are distilled together with sandalwood oil to create fragrant essences, in a centuries-old tradition.

117772-000-A.title: Tracks - A Life Without Work
117772-000-A.desc: Working wears us out as human beings and wears out the Earth too. To avoid the big burn-out, Tracks has a few solutions at hand: a universal basic income, a deliberate reduction in growth , or a radical exit from the labour market. Ready to roll up your sleeves and do nothing?

117773-000-A.title: Tracks - Living Stateless
117773-000-A.desc: Dreamers and utopians, awaken from your slumber and build the perfect world today! Tracks leaves the modern nation state behind and goes in search of alternative ways of living. Can we reinvent democracy to create perfect world without fixed structures or hierarchies?

117774-005-A.title: Klangphonics - Fairground Festival 2023
117774-005-A.desc:
Ben Kopfnagel, Maxl Walmsley-Pledl and Markus Zunic make up Klangphonics. A trio creating a natural, almost organic sound, yet one that retains the energy inherent in deep house and melodic techno.
Filmed on 1 December 2023 at the Fairground Festival in Hanover, Germany.

117790-000-A.title: Armenia: Caught in the Middle
117790-000-A.desc: Caught between the regional powers of Turkey, Russia, and Iran, Armenia is constantly fighting for survival in the powder keg that is the Caucasus. A deep insight into the recent history of Armenia including its independence from the Soviet Union and the struggle to hold onto Nagorno-Karabakh.

117792-000-A.title: Jews of Poland - The Struggle for Truth
117792-000-A.desc: Historian Jan T Gross caused a scandal in Poland with the publication of his book ‘Neighbours’ in 2000 which described how ordinary Poles, rather than German soldiers, had murdered their Jewish neighbours in the village of Jedwabne in World War II. The bitter dispute that followed on the Polish role in the Holocaust has divided society as academics struggle to tell the truth on the eradication of Poland’s jews.

118152-002-A.title: In Conversation with Robert Guédiguian
118152-002-A.desc: Before becoming a filmmaker, Robert Guédiguian always thought he would be an activist who lived intensely. His cinema is like him: From Marseille, expansive and joyful, poetic and above all, human.

118152-038-A.title: In Conversation with François Ozon
118152-038-A.desc: François Ozon navigates diverse genres, from tragedies to popular comedies and enigmatic tales. With curiosity and a playful approach, he deciphers the unknowns of life through his films, crafting stories that reveal his unique cinematic voice.

118152-043-A.title: In Conversation with Walter Salles
118152-043-A.desc: Walter Salles, a former documentary filmmaker, is deeply inspired by Brazil’s history. His films vividly depict the country’s social and political realities, intertwining past and present. Central to his storytelling are the perspectives of people in a society shaped by the absence of fathers, both figuratively and literally – offering a poignant view of Brazilian identity.

118152-044-A.title: In Conversation with Lucile Hadzihalilovic
118152-044-A.desc: Filmmaker Lucile Hadzilalilovic, winner of the Silver Bear at the 2025 Berlinale for “The Tower of Ice”, delves into her world of hauntingly beautiful tales. From “Innocence” to her latest film, she crafts stories rich in mystery, where powerful women and enigmatic youth evoke the legacy of Argento-style genre cinema.

118161-001-A.title: Marilyn Monroe: Fame at any Price (1/3)
118161-001-A.desc: Norma Jeane Baker grew up in difficult circumstances. Her mother and grandmother suffer from depression; her father only existed as a photograph. When an early marriage does not bring the security she craves, her only consolation is the dream of a career in the movies…

118161-002-A.title: Marilyn Monroe: Fame at any Price (2/3)
118161-002-A.desc: The story of the rise of the ambitious Marilyn Monroe. Episode 2: In 1947 Fox does not renew her contract. The young actress is determined to succeed: She changes her name and gets stuck into learning the craft of dancing, singing, and performing on stage and screen.

118161-003-A.title: Marilyn Monroe: Fame at any Price (3/3)
118161-003-A.desc: The story of the meteoric rise of Marilyn Monroe, an icon of cinema. Final episode: In 1955, at the height of her fame, Marilyn Monroe starts her own production company in New York; She wants to take control of her career and choose more complicated and satisfying roles.

118179-000-A.title: GDR Photo Shoot - Propaganda and Daily Life
118179-000-A.desc: In recent years, works by photographers who lived in the GDR before the fall of the Berlin Wall have been rediscovered and reappraised.  An exclusive look at a world that no longer exists.

118182-000-A.title: Terra Incognita
118182-000-A.desc: German couple Anne and Gerd, live in the Alps, far from the modern world. After the Chernobyl disaster in 1986, they fled with their children from the radioactive fallout to a life without electricity and running water. Nearby is a nuclear fusion reactor that is supposed to generate clean electricity... A poetic documentary which contrasts two visions of our relationship with technology.

118200-001-A.title: Suffocation - Cérémonie
118200-001-A.desc: Suffocation's death metal takes the Cérémonie audience by the throat. A performance of rare intensity, and a fitting introduction to the album Hymns from the Apocrypha.

118212-001-A.title: Irène Drésel - Passengers
118212-001-A.desc: Irène Drésel brings her electro-floral machines to the Roseraie du Val de Marne for Passengers. The perfect setting to immerse yourself in her album Rose Fluo, released in 2024.

118212-003-A.title: Agoria - Passengers: Le Radôme of Pleumeur-Bodou
118212-003-A.desc: Electro DJ and producer Agoria takes over the mighty Radôme. A retro-futuristic Passengers to discover Unshadow, an album at the genre crossroads.

118231-001-A.title: The Fabulous History of Money (1/4) - A Revolutionary Idea
118231-001-A.desc: Gold, silver, bronze or paper: money tells the story of 2,700 years of human history. An essential tool for trade and a symbol of power, it fuelled the expansion of empires and changed the course of civilisations. From ancient cowrie shells to the rise of the first banknotes and cryptocurrencies, this four-part series uncovers the role of money in history.

118231-002-A.title: The Fabulous History of Money (2/4) - The Age of Empires
118231-002-A.desc: From ancient Greece to modern-day America, this documentary series explores the role of money in history. Episode two: from the 4th century BC onwards, the great powers that were empires used money to service their expansion. This new era began with the adventure of one man, Alexander the Great, who plundered the immense treasures of Persia.

118231-003-A.title: The Fabulous History of Money (3/4) - The Chinese Way
118231-003-A.desc: Money has changed the course of civilisations across the ages. Episode three: In China, it all began in the tombs of great figures around five thousand years ago - cowries (small seashells) were placed there, and today they can be found in their thousands. These were the ancestors of Chinese coins, which first appeared in the 7th century BCE.

118231-004-A.title: The Fabulous History of Money (4/4) - From Coins to Bitcoin
118231-004-A.desc: How did Europe and America begin using paper money? In 1688 the Bill of Rights offered a new status to the Bank of England, according it the privilege of issuing banknotes backed by its hold and silver reserves.

118234-000-A.title: Forgotten Heritage of Arab Medicine
118234-000-A.desc: Could the knowledge of Arab scholars from the Middle Ages such as Al-Kindi, Al-Razi or Avicenna open up new perspectives for science in our times? From Fez to Rabat and Istanbul to Strasbourg, a team of scientists sets off on the trail of forgotten manuscripts of medieval Arabic medicine.

118240-000-A.title: Homo Plasticus - Plastic Inside Us
118240-000-A.desc: Scientists are exploring new leads to understand the effects of microplastics on our health: Disruption of the microbiota, saturation of the immune system, inflammation of the lungs and intestines, DNA damage and a risk factor for cancer - the scenario unfolding before our eyes points to a major public health issue. This unprecedented investigation takes us all over Europe to better understand what microplastic is doing to our bodies.

118254-000-A.title: Spain: From Dictatorship to Democracy - 50 Years Since Franco's Death
118254-000-A.desc: After Franco's death in November 1975, Spain embarked on the road to democracy. A look back at fifty years of political upheaval, social struggle and the building of democracy in a country on a quest for freedom.

118255-001-A.title: The Spanish Civil War (1/2) - The Long Road to Reconciliation
118255-001-A.desc: In two parts this documentary tells the painful story of the Spanish Civil War with descendants of Republicans and Francoists. July 1936. A military coup triggers a bloody civil war in Spain. After three years of fighting and 400,000 deaths, the war finally ends in the spring of 1939 with General Franco’s forces victorious. Heavy repression follows with the new dictatorship interning thousands of people in concentration camps.

118255-002-A.title: The Spanish Civil War (2/2) - The Long Road to Reconciliation
118255-002-A.desc: Spring 1939. After 1,000 days of fighting and more than 400,000 deaths, the war ended with Madrid taken by the Francoists and the Republic defeated. The post-war period was characterised by repression of the losing side and deep divisions often split families right down the middle.

118579-000-A.title: America: Library Wars
118579-000-A.desc: In a divided USA, what children read has become a burning issue and a culture war between progressive and conservative America. School libraries are the unlikely battleground for this war: Some want to ban authors they view as corrupting their children, others view this ban as reactionary censorship. This documentary focuses on the ideological fault lines of American society at the presidential election.

118607-000-A.title: Greenland - On the Trail of Polar Explorer Charcot
118607-000-A.desc: Guided by the trip made a century ago by French polar explorer Jean-Baptiste Charcot, two researchers from the Paris Natural History Museum go on an expedition to Greenland to discover how the arctic marine fauna is coping with climate change.

118618-000-A.title: Bernhard Grzimek and the Serengeti
118618-000-A.desc: German veterinarian, zoologist and television personality Bernhard Grzimek fought for decades for the preservation of the Serengeti, the vast savannah stretching across Kenya and Tanzania, while at the same time dismissing the rights of the indigenous people who lived there, the Maasai. Portrait of a passionate and controversial animal rights activist.

118623-000-A.title: Boredom - An Unexplored Feeling
118623-000-A.desc: Our modern world is characterised by productivity and speed in a high stimulus environment. Who experiences boredom today when smartphones and streaming platforms can fill any spare moment?  And yet boredom can be good for us: It feeds creativity and teaches us how to regulate our emotions from an early age. Researchers around the world are now trying to decipher this mysterious feeling.

118682-001-A.title: El Gran Teatro del Mundo - Madrid International Festival of Sacred Art
118682-001-A.desc: Madrid's International Festival of Sacred Art (FIAS) offers a uniquely diverse program at various venues across the Spanish capital every year. An opportunity to enjoy the work of high profile artists as well as new names, works and various other performances. El Gran Teatro del Mundo opens the festival with a French composer: Marc-Antoine Charpentier.

118682-002-A.title: Handel: German Arias - Madrid International Festival of Sacred Art
118682-002-A.desc: Madrid's International Festival of Sacred Art (FIAS) offers a uniquely diverse program at various venues across the Spanish capital every year. An opportunity to enjoy the work of high profile artists as well as new names, works and various other performances. Here, Soprano Ana Vieira Leite and the Concerto 1700 ensemble perform Handel's German Arias.

118682-003-A.title: Tenebræ Responsoria - Madrid International Festival of Sacred Art
118682-003-A.desc: Madrid's International Festival of Sacred Music (FIAS) offers a unique international program every year at various venues around the Spanish capital. The venue for this concert is the San Miguel basilica for a performance of Tomás Luis de Victoria's Tenebræ Responsoria by Cantoría, a young Spanish ensemble with an international following.

118865-008-A.title: The Book of My Life - With Yuval Noah Harari
118865-008-A.desc: "The big problem of science is that science is not a very good storyteller" says the Israeli philosopher and bestselling writer Yuval Noah Harari. Jagoda Marinić interviews one of the major thinkers of our time and finds out what books have inspired him throughout his life.

118865-009-A.title: The Book of My LIfe - With Francesca Melandri
118865-009-A.desc: "Great literature is about creating this miracle of empathy between the writer and the reader. And this miracle of empathy is the greatest political value of literature". Francesca Melandri is one of the best-known voices in contemporary Italian literature. With Jagoda Marinic, she talks about her free-spirited childhood and her life before she began writing books.

119015-000-A.title: The Cannibal of Milwaukee - "My Friend Dahmer" by Derf Backderf
119015-000-A.desc: After almost 20 years of work, Derf Backderf published his graphic novel "My Friend Dahmer" - an unadulterated biography about the high school years of his classmate Jeffrey Dahmer, who would become one of the most notorious serial killers in the USA. This documentary meets the author and finds out how he made this highly unusual comic book.

119022-000-A.title: The Tree of Authenticity
119022-000-A.desc: In Yangambi, Congo, the remains of a research centre for tropical agriculture bear witness to the colonial past. The documentary highlights the connection between colonisation and the climate crisis and in doing so, adopts an unusual perspective: that of the tree of authenticity, which plays a decisive role in climate regulation.

119024-000-A.title: Palimpsest - The Story of a Name
119024-000-A.desc: Canadian editor and director Mary Stephen, born in Hongkong to a Chinese family, tries to find out why she has an English family name. Her film is a journey of discovery into the past and a questioning of the desire to rewrite one’s history.

119027-001-F.title: Giacomo Puccini: Madama Butterfly - Festival d'Aix-en-Provence 2024
119027-001-F.desc: Seduced by a visiting American soldier, the geisha Cio-Cio-San, known as Madama Butterfly, awaits his return... In a production by Andrea Breth and under the musical direction of Daniele Rustioni, the Albanian soprano Ermonela Jaho brings Puccini's Japanese tragedy to life at the Théâtre de l'Archevêché in Aix-en-Provence.

119031-002-A.title: Tom Odell - Sounds Like Art - Musée Bourdelle, Paris
119031-002-A.desc: Surrounded by a choir of young London singers, British singer Tom Odell brings his bitter and melancholic songs into dialogue with the sculptures at the Bourdelle Museum in Paris.

119031-003-A.title: Asaf Avidan - Sounds like Art - Saarland Museum Modern Gallery
119031-003-A.desc: At the Saarland Modern Galerie in Saarbrücken, Germany, songs from Unfurl, Asaf Avidan's new album, blend with masterpieces from a contemporary art collection that is as socially conscious as it is inspiring.

119031-004-A.title: Sting - Sounds Like Art - Rijksmuseum Amsterdam
119031-004-A.desc: Legendary singer Sting performs in the beautiful surroundings of Amsterdam’s Rijkmuseum surrounded by paintings by Rembrandt, Vermeer and Judith Leyster, and reveals his love for the works of the old masters.

119033-000-A.title: Albéric Magnard: Guercœur - Opéra national du Rhin, Strasbourg
119033-000-A.desc: Guercœur, the knight and a freedom fighter, meets with an early death and ends up in heaven. But his soul is unable to rest and begs to be returned to earth. At the Opéra national du Rhin, Stéphane Degout plays the title role under the direction of Ingo Metzmacher, while Christof Loy’s staging explores the boundaries between the two worlds.

119423-000-A.title: Jimmy Somerville - Queer Rebel of British Pop
119423-000-A.desc: With his 1984 hit "Smalltown Boy", Jimmy Somerville hit the bigtime and became the voice of the gay movement in Britain hoping for freedom and equal rights in face of discrimination. As frontman of Bronski Beat then The Communards and finally as a solo artist, he became an icon of the 80s without ever denying his identity.

119461-000-A.title: Nils Frahm - Philharmonie de Paris
119461-000-A.desc: Nils Frahm unleashes the full genius of his neoclassical universe for an ecstatic audience at the Philharmonie de Paris. A concert built around tracks from Day, his fifteenth album released in 2024.

119482-000-A.title: The Taliban's Natural Resources
119482-000-A.desc: Afghanistan's mineral reserves, which are vital for the technology of the future and a carbon-free economy, are attracting interest from Russia, China and Iran. What impact will this windfall have on a population living under Taliban rule?

119501-000-A.title: New Treatments for Endometriosis
119501-000-A.desc: Around one in ten women suffers from endometriosis. The chronic disease can cause extreme pain and yet often goes undiagnosed for years. This documentary accompanies women who suffer from the misunderstood condition and finds out about the latest research on treatments.

119518-000-A.title: Putin and the Five Seas War
119518-000-A.desc: Vladimir Putin wants to extend Russian control over the five seas: The Caspian Sea, The Black Sea, The Baltic, the Azov Sea, and the White Sea, as part of a campaign to increase Russian influence and might in the world. This documentary takes an in-depth look at Moscow’s ‘Five Seas System’, a geopolitical strategy little known in the West, and how its implementation is leading to instability and war.

119595-001-A.title: Saying Goodbye to the Opera
119595-001-A.desc: Alice Renavand and Stéphane Bullion are ballet dancers who have reached the pinnacle of their careers as principal dancers (danseur étoile) of the Paris Opera. But their careers are fleeting, automatically coming to an end at the age of 42. How do they feel about their upcoming final performances And making their transition into a new life?

119928-000-A.title: Venice - The Golden Age - Glamour and Violence
119928-000-A.desc: In the fifteenth century the most Serene Republic of Venice was one of the richest cities in Europe through trade with the East. Through the fate of Doge Francesco Foscari and his son Jacopo, accused of high treason, this documentary tells the story of the golden age of Venice, a time of wonder and violence.

119958-005-A.title: Paul van Dyk - NatureOne 2024
119958-005-A.desc: Cult DJ and dance music producer Paul van Dyk has been a benchmark of trance music since the mid-1990s. In his remixes of productions from that decade, such as Humate's Love Simulation and his classic For an Angel, he blends melancholy melodies with ecstatic beats. In the space of ten years, he has helped to make progressive trance an essential genre. Paul van Dyk has been a mainstay of NatureOne's programming since the very beginning.

119958-011-A.title: Klaudia Gawlas - NatureOne 2024
119958-011-A.desc: At the age of 13, Klaudia Gawlas was already making music on her Commodore 64. She broke through in 2013 with the release of Papillon, which established her as a leader on the techno scene. The recent creation of her label Illusion Recordings marks a further milestone in her career. It's also proof that she's able to bring her music and visual aesthetic into line with her personal vision.

119958-017-A.title: ONYVAA - NatureOne 2024
119958-017-A.desc: ONYVAA's sets are always an exciting journey. They draw on the timeless sounds of Detroit and Chicago, as well as the acid techno of the early 1990s. A DJ able to mix hypnotic melodic lines with a variety of textures, while creating a sound filled with modular synthesizers, groovy basses, heavy drums, pads with poignant notes and samples of her own. ONYVAA releases on PLUS8, KNTXT and Passeport Records, her own label.

119958-019-A.title: Kölsch - NatureOne 2024
119958-019-A.desc: This Danish DJ has been playing in the electro big league for many years. IPSO, the label he founded, produces melodic house and techno tracks with a soulful edge. Kölsch's albums are packed with timeless tracks such as ‘Lorely’, ‘Opa’ and especially ‘Grey’, which has 5.5 million views on YouTube and almost 50 million listens on Spotify. I Talk To Water is his latest album.

119958-022-A.title: Markus Schulz - NatureOne 2024
119958-022-A.desc: Over the last ten years, Markus Schulz has taken progressive music, particularly trance and house, to new heights, creating a fusion sound all his own. In the 1990s, he got his start at rave parties in Arizona and quickly broke through internationally. His albums on the Armada and Coldharbour labels propelled him to the top of the American EDM scene. His tracks ‘The New World’ and ‘Destiny’ have even become electro anthems.

119958-023-A.title: Alignment - NatureOne 2024
119958-023-A.desc: Alignment describes his musical style as a journey into the depths of his inner self. His enigmatic sounds evoke a post-apocalyptic rave taking place indefinitely in a disused warehouse. He writes beautiful melodies with deep bass lines, kicks and resonant synth filters; renowned labels such as KNTXT and Suara are not immune.

120103-001-A.title: Tribute to Frank Cassenti - Jazz à Porquerolles 2024
120103-001-A.desc: Frank Cassenti, creator of Jazz à Porquerolles, passed away in December 2023. To pay tribute to him, the festival invites a top-flight line-up to the Var: With Naissam Jalal, Nguyên Lê, Marion Rampal and more.

120189-000-A.title: Air Play "Moon Safari" - Royal Albert Hall
120189-000-A.desc: AIR perform their iconic album Moon Safari at London's prestigious Royal Albert Hall. Return to the tail end of the 1990s with this exceptional concert, when the "French Touch" scene was conquering hearts and minds from the European continent to Hollywood.

120410-000-A.title: Climate Refugees - No Roof No Rights
120410-000-A.desc: As more and more people, forgotten by international law, flee rising sea levels and extreme weather events, the countries most vulnerable to climate change have no choice but to act. A focus on Bangladesh, Colombia and Fiji.

120440-000-A.title: The Great Smog of London
120440-000-A.desc: For five days in December 1952, a thick smog suffocated the British capital causing the death of thousands of Londoners. The health catastrophe lead to a realisation, in Britain and around the world, that air pollution was a serious threat to human health. This documentary reveals what happened, hour by hour, in one of the worst peacetime disasters of the 20th century.

120449-000-A.title: End War or Build Peace?
120449-000-A.desc: Through eyewitness testimonies and numerous archive images, this documentary delves into the negotiations that marked the history of diplomacy in the late 20th and early 21st centuries: the achievement of a real but fragile peace in Colombia and South Africa, the failure of an inclusive peace process in Afghanistan and the stalemate in the Israeli-Palestinian conflict. How can we end war and build peace?

120454-000-A.title: HVOB - Wiener Konzerthaus
120454-000-A.desc: Viennese production duo Anna Müller and Paul Wallner, aka HVOB, have made a name for themselves on the global electro scene with their albums and impressive live performances. On 20 April 2024, they gave a sold-out concert at Vienna's famous Konzerthaus.

120464-000-A.title: The Taylor Swift Effect
120464-000-A.desc: Taylor Swift has become the most famous woman in the world and the current most streamed recording artist. From her childhood in suburban Pennsylvania to Nashville, where she started her career as a country artist, to her current international fame as a preeminent pop star, Taylor Swift has galvanised an immensely loyal following with her subtly emotional songs and canny marketing and self-promotion.

120467-000-A.title: Madness - Princes of Ska
120467-000-A.desc: From the back rooms of Camden's pubs to the roof of Buckingham Palace at Queen Elizabeth’s Jubilee: the London band Madness enriched British pop music history with their cheeky style and spectacular performances. In this documentary, we look back on their illustrious career with interviews with band members, friends, and musc industry figures.

120498-001-A.title: Conrad Tao - Klavier-Festival Ruhr 2024
120498-001-A.desc: At once a piano recital and a wild ride through music history: Conrad Tao presents an astonishing selection of compositions. He alternates Robert Schumann and Sergei Rachmaninov with pieces by Billy Strayhorn, which he composed for Duke Ellington's big band.

120499-005-A.title: Cécile McLorin Salvant - Leverkusener Jazztage 2024
120499-005-A.desc: Cécile McLorin Salvant has been described as ‘the heiress of Billie Holiday’. When she sings, it's as if time and space disappear, with a modern-day voice reviving the soul of ‘Lady Day’. Winning award after award, this Franco-American singer is now one of the leading figures in jazz today.

120505-000-A.title: The Venetians - Vivoin Festival
120505-000-A.desc: They were pioneers, inspired and often forgotten: The Venetian composers of the Seicento are finally back in the spotlight in a vibrant programme performed by Camille Poul and the ensemble L'Assemblée, conducted by harpsichordist Marie van Rhijn.

120705-000-A.title: A Royal Overture - Festival international de Colmar
120705-000-A.desc: The latest edition of the Festival International de Colmar opens with an exceptional performance from the Orchestre Symphonique du Théâtre Royal de la Monnaie de Bruxelles, conducted by Alain Altinoglu. After Wagner's Prelude to Lohengrin, baritone Stéphane Degout sings Mahler's Songs of a Wandering Companion. The evening at Saint-Matthieu church closes with César Franck's Symphony in D minor, a jewel from the repertoire of the late 19th century.

120718-001-A.title: Arcade Fire - Bilbao BBK Live Festival 2024
120718-001-A.desc: Alternative icons Arcade Fire are known for their rousing performances, and this one, captured at the Bilbao BBK Live Festival, is no exception. A journey of pure poetic energy

120718-010-A.title: El Columpio Asesino - Bilbao BBK Live Festival 2024
120718-010-A.desc: El Columpio Asesino is known as one of Spain's biggest krautrock bands. In 2024, they embarked on a farewell tour that includes this performance at the BBK Live Festival.

120718-013-A.title: Zea Mays - Bilbao BBK Live Festival 2024
120718-013-A.desc: Local band Zea Mays unleash their progressive rock at the Bilbao BBK Live Festival for the release of their album Adore Kemena Kuraia.

120720-004-A.title: Anushka Chkheidze - Europavox Festival 2024
120720-004-A.desc: Anushka Chkheidze is representing Georgia at the Europavox Festival. Her ambassadorial role is set against a backdrop of electronic music.

120720-006-A.title: Elizabete Balčus - Europavox Festival 2024
120720-006-A.desc: An encounter of the third kind at Europavox! Latvian producer Elizabete Balčus introduces the festival to her psychedelic, futuristic dream pop.

120772-002-A.title: Ben Böhmer live - Sónar 2024
120772-002-A.desc: German producer and performer Ben Böhmer is one of the great architects of electronic music. He is known for his haunting sounds and deep, dreamlike beats. His new album Bloom comes out in September, and his world tour has brought him to the prestigious Sónar 2024 festival in Barcelona.

120772-004-A.title: Richie Hawtin: DEX EFX X0X - Sónar 2024
120772-004-A.desc: DJ and producer Richie Hawtin is one of the leading lights of electronic music, with a career spanning more than twenty-five years.  In his own words, "the whole art of techno is to make the machines disappear". This techno visionary is a regular at Barcelona's Sónar and returns this year with a new show, DEX EFX X0X.

120853-001-A.title: French Soldiers of the Third Reich (1/2)
120853-001-A.desc: Between 1941 and 1945 around 30,000 French citizens voluntarily joined the German army and engaged in terrible war crimes in central and eastern Europe. This documentary explores a little-known chapter in the story of the second world war and France’s collaboration with Nazi Germany.

120853-002-A.title: French Soldiers of the Third Reich (2/2)
120853-002-A.desc: Between 1941 and 1945 around 30,000 French citizens voluntarily joined the German army and fought shoulder to shoulder with the Nazis. Episode two: In November 1942, Germany takes control of the French zone libre as the allied armies make gains in North Africa. Pétain is now the head of a Nazi satellite state with no army or empire.

120879-008-A.title: Re: Rural Moldova - Between Russia and the EU
120879-008-A.desc: Upcoming elections in Moldova will determine its future. Armed with his camera, Victor Organ criss-crosses this country scarred by war and exodus. He is working to ensure that the inhabitants of isolated villages, and their stories, are not forgotten, through his photography.

120882-000-A.title: Tunisia: A Betrayed Revolution
120882-000-A.desc: The hope of a free republic in the heady days of Tunisia’s revolution in 2010 turned into despair as a new authoritarian regime took hold with the re-election of President Kais Saied in 2024. This documentary interviews supporters and opponents of Saied’s regime in a detailed overview of the last 15 years of tumultuous politics.

120884-000-A.title: El-Sisi: Egypt's New Pharoah
120884-000-A.desc: President El-Sisi has had built an immense new capital for Egypt out in the desert, part of his plan to build a new modern Egypt. But the Egyptian people are paying the price with the government racking up huge debts whilst poverty rates soar. This documentary looks at the explosive situation in Egypt faced with major problems at home and abroad.

120890-001-A.title: Chlär b2b Dax J - Stone Techno 2024
120890-001-A.desc: What drives Chlär is an unquenchable thirst for learning and progress. So it's hardly surprising that his productions are like attempts to awaken the primal, tribal instinct that lies dormant in all of us. Dax J, British DJ and producer, has gone from pirate radio to headlining the world's biggest techno institutions. All the while cultivating a resolutely singular style and an unshakeable attachment to his underground DNA.

120890-002-A.title: Oscar Mulero - Stone Techno 2024
120890-002-A.desc: Oscar Mulero's prolific career mirrors the evolution of EDM. A key figure in this wave, the DJ has never stopped incorporating new trends. The result: since the late 1980s, he's always kept up with the times.

120890-005-A.title: Grace Dahl - Stone Techno 2024
120890-005-A.desc: Amsterdam-based DJ and producer Grace Dahl has Finnish and Hungarian roots. The Vault Sessions resident delivers an eclectic techno selection, moving seamlessly from hypnotic loops to swinging grooves interspersed with powerful kicks and omnipresent percussion.

120890-006-A.title: Alarico - Stone Techno 2024
120890-006-A.desc: Over the last two years, this young Italian has matured like a fine Tuscan wine. After taking the scene by storm in 2021, Alarico has not rested on his laurels. Since then, he's put out one production after another, but never given up on his singular values and aesthetic.

120890-007-A.title: Yanamaste - Stone Techno 2024
120890-007-A.desc: Yanamaste is an extraordinary personality. Irakli Danelia is capable of captivating audiences with his instantly recognisable soundscapes and infectious beats. Originally from Batumi, Georgia, Yanamaste began his musical odyssey at an early age, quickly finding his calling in techno.

120890-008-A.title: Colin Benders - Stone Techno 2024
120890-008-A.desc: Colin Benders is all about connections. Ideas fly into his brain and he captures them at lightning speed to transpose them into compositions and creations. Over the last seven years, he has been fine-tuning a modular synthesiser so that he can fully exploit a multitude of sound elements.

120890-009-A.title: Adriana Lopez b2b Dasha Rush - Stone Techno 2024
120890-009-A.desc: Colombian Adriana Lopez, a Barcelona-based techno purist, has been inspiring the scene for over two decades with exceptional atmospheres at every appearance. Dasha Rush combines the forces of the universe, her fascination with astrophysics and the latent yearnings of the human soul to create emotionally charged techno, complemented by visual explorations and performance art.

120890-010-A.title: Annē b2b Sol Ortega - Stone Techno 2024
120890-010-A.desc: A native of Thessaloniki, where she spent her childhood, Annē began her musical career in 2012. Since then she has devoted herself body and soul to techno, working as both a DJ and producer. Originally from Buenos Aires, Sol Ortega is one of the few figures on the South American electro scene to have made a name for himself worldwide. Her sets, at the crossroads of house and techno, have made her a household name in her native Argentina.

120890-011-A.title: Elli Acula - Stone Techno 2024
120890-011-A.desc: Elli Acula's irresistible rise has taken everything in its path. With groovy techno, rave, breakbeat, electro and jungle sounds, her electrifying energy has won over regulars at major European clubs such as Berghain, Printworks and Fabric. And as if that wasn't enough, Elli Acula has even conquered the dancefloors of South America and Australia.

120891-018-A.title: VNV Nation - M'era Luna Festival 2024
120891-018-A.desc: Irishman Ronan Harris is the brain behind VNV Nation as well as the only permanent member of the group. His project was first launched in 1990, gradually laying the foundations for the pop of the future. VNV Nation (the three letters stand for ‘Victory Not Vengeance’) and its danceable trance has been a regular fixture in the German charts, with no less than eleven albums finding success there. His discography also includes an acclaimed collaboration with the Babelsberg film studio orchestra.

120894-000-A.title: Thomas Hengelbrock and Orchestre de chambre de Paris - Paris Pantheon
120894-000-A.desc: In homage to the composer who died a hundred years ago, on 4 November 1924, Thomas Hengelbrock conducts his celestial Requiem under the dome of the Panthéon in Paris. A musical event introduced by an orchestral version of Beethoven's String Quartet No. 15.

120911-000-A.title: The Unicorn: A Cultural History
120911-000-A.desc: One of the most famous mythical creatures has roamed our cultural history for centuries. Over the course of time, the unicorn has symbolised many things:  Healing, destruction, innocence, and even queerness. This documentary goes in search of the unicorn. Why does this creature fascinate us so?

121273-000-A.title: Iraq in the Crossfire
121273-000-A.desc: In 2014, to halt the onslaught of Islamic State, Iraq created the Popular Mobilisation Forces, majority Shiite militias, incorporated into the state military forces to protect the country in its desperate hour of need. These majority Shiite units, with links to Iran, have not disbanded and have enormous influence in the country, causing instability. Documentary filmmaker Anne Poiret investigates.

121274-000-A.title: Putin & Africa - Russian Influence in the Sahel
121274-000-A.desc: After military regimes in Niger, Mali and Burkina Faso drove out Western troops, Russia is using the resulting power vacuum to increase its influence in a region victim of terrorism and repressive regimes. This documentary reveals the extent to which Putin is expanding Russian influence on the African continent.

121278-000-A.title: Glaciers - Melting Giants
121278-000-A.desc: The Climate crisis is accelerating the melting of glaciers around the world, threatening access to fresh water for two billion people. This documentary is an in-depth investigation into the state of the globe’s glaciers. With the ice melting fast, what can be done to save them?

121379-000-A.title: Étretat Forever - Creating a Legend
121379-000-A.desc: Claude Monet made the steep cliffs of Étretat in Normandy world-famous. The charming seaside town has been a hotspot for artists since the 19th century. But with over a million tourists now visiting every year, local people, the built environment, and the cliffs themselves, are starting to feel the strain. This documentary looks at the history of Étretat and its representation in art, from humble fishing village to fashionable 'authentic' resort for Parisians, to today's victim of overtourism.

121382-000-A.title: Flower Power - Dutch Women Painters of the 17th Century
121382-000-A.desc: In the 17th century, advances in the natural sciences and the invention of the telescope led Dutch women painters to closely study nature and include reptiles and insects in their art. Women such as Maria Sibylla Merian, Alida Withoos and Maria Moninckx advanced botany and zoology with their highly detailed paintings. Almost forgotten, now their works hang in many important museums around the world.

121388-000-A.title: The Stranger Beside Me - Ann Rule and Ted Bundy
121388-000-A.desc: American writer Ann Rule’s best-selling first book was the chilling story of her strange friendship with Ted Bundy, a serial killer who terrorised the USA in the 1970s.

121414-000-A.title: At Your Service
121414-000-A.desc: Alma, Chaily, Elias, Sarah and Abdul are teenagers following a vocational course at a school for the hotel and catering industry. They did not all choose this profession but are all trying to make their way in the world. This documentary follows their apprenticeship as they set their course for a better future.

121417-004-A.title: St. Vincent - ARTE Concert Festival 2024
121417-004-A.desc: Annie Clark- AKA St. Vincent- is one of the most elusive figures in world rock. Yet here she is headlining the ARTE Concert Festival 2024 to mark the release of her album All Born Screaming.

121425-000-A.title: Babx & Adrien Mondot - Piano Piano
121425-000-A.desc: Piano Piano, devised by pianist Babx and multi-disciplinary artist Adrien Mondot, is a unique experience combining keyboards, juggling and light. A poetic journey that brings together two worlds.

121428-001-A.title: Dax J - Szczecin Recordings
121428-001-A.desc: Britain's Dax J is one of the biggest names on today's techno scene. His rave-infused style revisits classic 90s techno with energy and modernity. A regular at the world's biggest clubs and festivals, his sets combine speed, intensity and remarkable technical mastery.

121428-002-A.title: D.Dan - Szczecin Recordings
121428-002-A.desc: D.Dan's ascent to Berlin's techno temples like the Berghain and RSO is like a modern fairytale: A young producer and DJ with no career plan and no network rises to the top of the techno scene in record time. His music is resolutely funky, bursting with energy and resolutely hypnotic.

121428-003-A.title: Akua - Szczecin Recordings
121428-003-A.desc: This American artist with Ghanaian roots is part of a new generation of artists reviving the rebellious spirit of techno in their sets. Inspired by the synthetic funk that revolutionised the Detroit and Chicago techno and house scenes, Akua's sound is minimalist: Pure techno with a healthy dose of soul.

121428-004-A.title: Edvvin - Szczecin Recordings
121428-004-A.desc: Edvvin is emerging as one of the spearheads of the Polish techno scene. His energetic, catchy sets combine tribal techno and hardgroove, two classic styles that have come back into fashion which he revisits from a fresh, resolutely innovative perspective.

121428-005-A.title: An On Bast - Szczecin Recordings
121428-005-A.desc: Thanks to her improvised sets, in which she juggles modular synthesizers, effects boxes and drum machines with ease, An On Bast has made a name for herself well beyond Poland's borders. Her musical universe veers between melodic techno, ambient and experimental electro.

121428-006-A.title: Catz’N Dogz - Szczecin Recordings
121428-006-A.desc: Grzegorz and Wojciech have been friends since childhood and have been producing music under the name Catz'N Dogz for over twenty years. The two DJs, who hail from Szczecin in Poland, quickly conquered the house and tech house scene in the early 2000s, achieving undeniable success as evidenced by their international tours of the world's leading clubs.

121428-007-A.title: Cassy - Szczecin Recordings
121428-007-A.desc: A club and festival icon for the last two decades, vinyl fan Cassy is known for sweeping up audiences with her infectious enthusiasm. She has lived in Berlin, Paris, London and New York, and has been resident DJ at legendary clubs such as Panorama Bar in Berlin and Rex Club in Paris.

121428-008-A.title: Natalie Robinson - Szczecin Recordings
121428-008-A.desc: Natalie Robinson comes from the new Berlin school of house music, with a style mixing soul and 90s house. Her euphoric sets blend gospel, funk, soul, RnB and traditional Ghanaian music. In just a few years, she has established herself as a key figure on the Berlin house scene.

121428-009-A.title: fka.m4a - Szczecin Recordings
121428-009-A.desc: A British DJ based in Berlin, fka.m4a fuses italo-disco, high-NRG and leftfield techno, creating a new benchmark for electro sets. In 2024, he launched his own label, CONTRAST, and released his first EP, Summer Nostalgia. A style to be discovered at this unique Polish rave party.

121428-010-A.title: Ola Teks - Szczecin Recordings
121428-010-A.desc: DJ, promoter and resident of the Jasna 1 club in Warsaw, Ola Teks is also behind the Move Mózg project, which combines music and the visual arts. Her sets, marked by a healthy dose of eclecticism, explore various styles of techno combined with trance and classic house.

121437-000-A.title: PFAS - An Everyday Poison
121437-000-A.desc: PFAS, often called forever chemicals, have been in use in various industries for decades. These toxic substances, found in non-stick cooking equipment, waterproof clothing and fire-fighting foam are now everywhere on earth, and inside of the bodies of every human being. This documentary investigates how they got there, and what can be done about this worrying risk to humans and the planet.

121670-000-A.title: Zahia Ziouani and Mourad Merzouki - Les Nouveaux Mondes
121670-000-A.desc: Led by the conductor Zahia Ziouani and the choreographer Mourad Merzouki, a humanist concert combining classical music, contemporary creation, dance and world traditions.

122119-001-A.title: A Conversation with Marion Cotillard
122119-001-A.desc: How has French actress Marion Cotillard embodied characters from all walks of life with such passion and strength? Through an intense practice of her craft, driven by a secret desire to understand the human soul.

122119-031-A.title: A Conversation with Alfonso Cuarón
122119-031-A.desc: Mexican filmmaker Alfonso Cuaron never talks about his own films, but always about other directors’ works. Be it the great Japanese directors or masterworks of American cinema, his energy makes you want to see every film!

122151-000-A.title: Charles I : A King’s Downfall
122151-000-A.desc: 130 years before the French Revolution, Charles I, King of England, Ireland and Scotland is executed by his people. How did this act, deeply shocking for 17th century Europe, come to pass? This documentary sheds light on a major event in European history and the dramatic events leading up to and during the English Civil War.

122159-000-A.title: Mosquitos: A Dangerous Enemy
122159-000-A.desc: Tropical diseases such as dengue and malaria were long considered a distant problem for Europeans – today they have reached the continent. France reported a record number of locally transmitted chikungunya cases in 2025, whilst the West Nile virus is circulating in Germany. Climate change and global mobility are enabling deadly virus-carrying mosquitos to spread. This documentary looks at the problem and possible solutions.

122193-001-A.title: Famous Orchestras - The Vienna Philharmonic
122193-001-A.desc: The Wiener Philharmoniker is a very special orchestra hailing from the city that can perhaps claim to be the world capital of classical music. Steeped in tradition, and with a very specific sound, it is no surprise that eminent conductor Leonard Bernstein thought that the Vienna Philharmonic was the world’s best orchestra

122193-002-A.title: Famous Orchestras - The Berlin Philharmoniker
122193-002-A.desc: Discovering the world's most prestigious orchestras. In this episode: founded in 1882, the Berliner Philharmoniker initially consisted of around fifty musicians. Legendary conductors have led the ensemble including Hans von Bülow, Wilhelm Furtwängler,, Herbert von Karajan and the hugely popular Simon Rattle.

122207-000-A.title: Moby: 25 Years of Play - Sportpaleis Antwerp
122207-000-A.desc: Electro mastermind Moby celebrates the 25th anniversary of his cult album Play with a European tour. Here playing at the Antwerp Sportpaleis arena.

122224-000-A.title: Putin's Secret Weapons (1/2)
122224-000-A.desc: The Russian army never seems to run out of weapons. After four years of aggression against Ukraine, they still have significant stocks and have been able to develop new hypersonic weapons that could strike European capitals in minutes. How has the world’s most sanctioned country been able to do this?

122225-000-A.title: Putin's Secret Weapons (2/2) - The Power of New Alliances
122225-000-A.desc: From Iranian drones to North Korean missiles, Putin has managed to create new alliances in his confrontation with the West. This second episode focuses on the Russian president’s new pacts which are creating an anti-western alliance and redefining the balance of power.

122231-000-A.title: Breaking Social
122231-000-A.desc:
All societies are based on a social contract. In a meritocracy, hard work is supposed to be rewarded. But what happens when this social contract is broken?

 Breaking Social looks at the rule breakers: people who use tax havens to hoard wealth and reap profits without paying back to society. Looking at movements from around the world, including Chile and Malta, this thought-provoking documentary looks at global patterns of kleptocracy and how people are organising themselves to fight back against injustice.

122398-000-A.title: AI and the Art Market
122398-000-A.desc: Could an algorithm be able to authenticate masterpieces more reliably than human experts? And is it possible to create new painting from a long-dead master? This documentary explores the upheavals caused by the arrival of artificial intelligence in the art world.

122460-001-A.title: Battle Pro 2024 - International breakdance competition
122460-001-A.desc: The world's best breakdancers will be at the Théâtre du Châtelet for the 2024 edition of Battle Pro. Experience the competition as if you were there!

122691-000-A.title: Constantine the Great - Emperor of the New Rome
122691-000-A.desc: Emperor Constantine changed the Roman world like no other ruler of antiquity. This documentary follows the stages of his life and shows how he combined power and splendour with the spread of a new religion: Christianity.

122708-001-A.title: China - Making a Nation (1/2) - The Blue Sun
122708-001-A.desc: After the fall of the Qing dynasty in 1911, China faced an historic turning point: How could the country find its way in the modern world? This two-part documentary shows how for over a century, China has been building itself up to repel foreign influence and to create a strong nation.

122708-002-A.title: China - Making a Nation (2/2) - The Red Sun
122708-002-A.desc: At the end of the Second World War, the blue sun of Nationalist China seemed victorious. It had recovered all the foreign concessions on its territory and had become one of only five members of the United Nations Security Council. But with the communists of Mao Zedong still a threat, this was an illusion…

122718-000-A.title: Hungary: A Far-Right Testing Ground
122718-000-A.desc: Hungary’s Prime Minister, Viktor Orban, has been highly successful at home winning four elections in a row. His illiberal politics of hollowing out democratic institutions, attacking independent media and scapegoating minorities is widely celebrated by far-right commentators across the world. But it is in Europe where his influence is most present as Orban is now exporting his ideology to countries across the EU.

122719-000-A.title: Inside Gaza
122719-000-A.desc: Since the Hamas attack on 7 October 2023 and the Israeli reprisals that followed, international journalists and media have no access to the Gaza Strip. Agence France-Presse journalists, who were permanently stationed there, were among the last independent witnesses to the abuses committed in the hellscape that Gaza has become. This documentary shows what has happened inside the Palestinian territory over the last two years.

122735-000-A.title: The Girl From Ipanema - Astrud Gilberto: Queen of Bossa Nova
122735-000-A.desc: After singing vocals on the Girl from Ipanema in New York in 1963, Astrud Gilberto, who had never sung before, became an overnight sensation and a worldwide icon of the Brazilian Bossa Nova sound.

122738-000-A.title: The Matisse Chapel
122738-000-A.desc: The Matisse Chapel in Vence close to Nice in the South of France is the fruit of a special friendship between a young nurse and nun, Monique Bourgeois and the ageing artist Henri Matisse.  Considered by some to be a great religious building of the 20th century, it shocked both the Catholic Church and the avant-garde when it opened its doors in 1951. This documentary focuses on a special building and a special relationship.

122741-000-A.title: Nicolas Cage - Hollywood's Mad Genius
122741-000-A.desc: He’s a versatile actor who is forever experimenting with how to portray the human condition on the silver screen. Often drawn to unusual characters that stray into madness, Nicolas Cage has boldly blazed his own trail in Hollywood for the last four decades.

122744-000-A.title: Green Line
122744-000-A.desc:
Fida was born in Beirut in 1975, the year the Lebanese civil war broke out. As a child, she suffered less from the bombs that prevented her from going to school than from the need to comprehend the absurdity of the war. Why were people fighting each other on both sides of the green line that divided Beirut in two? 
Accompanied by filmmaker Sylvie Ballyot, Fida returns to Lebanon to answer this question for herself.

122763-000-A.title: Brad Mehldau Trio - Nancy Jazz Pulsation 2024
122763-000-A.desc: For Brad Mehldau's performance in Lorraine for the Nancy Jazz Pulsations festival, the piano virtuoso is accompanied by a double bassist and a drummer. A high-flying concert!

122767-000-A.title: Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds - The Wild God Tour, Paris
122767-000-A.desc: The triumphant return of Nick Cave and The Bad Seeds to France bring with them a full stage show brimming with elegant fury and mystical fervour.

123339-000-A.title: Lebanon: Anatomy of a War
123339-000-A.desc: To push back Hezbollah and make the paramilitary group unable to threaten Israel, the Israeli Defence Force have heavily bombarded Shia areas of Lebanon, killing many civilians. This documentary analyses the destructive strategy of the IDF in their war with Hezbollah.

123348-014-A.title: Eric Prydz - Sónar 2025
123348-014-A.desc:
Swedish progressive house genius Eric Prydz brings his brand new show to Sónar 2025. With unique visuals and laser effects, Prydz brings a whole new dimension to Barcelona's iconic festival.


Filmed on 14 June 2025 at Sónar, Barcelona.

123351-007-A.title: The Halo Effect - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-007-A.desc:
Halo Effect is one of the best death metal bands around right now. Sharp riffs, powerful growls, intense emotions: All elements that fascinate the audience. At Summer Breeze 2025, the Swedes deliver a show as solid as tempered steel.

Filmed on 13 August 2025 at Summer Breeze Open Air in Dinkelsbühl, Germany.

123351-008-A.title: Agnostic Front - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-008-A.desc: Despite being founded back in 1980, Agnostic Front remains at the forefront of hardcore punk. With bursts of energy, socially conscious lyrics, and spectacular shows, the band defends its vision of metal that is as aggressive as it is authentic. They deliver on that promise with this memorable evening on the Summer Breeze stage.

123351-025-A.title: Septicflesh - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-025-A.desc: Septic Flesh combines death metal with symphonic elements. A dark and poetic style that has become the trademark of this Greek band, known for its sophisticated songs and grandiose arrangements. At Summer Breeze 2025, they delivered a legendary show.

123351-028-A.title: Fit For A King - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-028-A.desc: Renowned for their high-energy live performances, raw power and powerful melodies, this American band has been captivating the metalcore scene since 2007. For their first appearance here at Summer Breeze, the musicians deliver an electrifying show combining passion and mastery.

123351-030-A.title: Obituary - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-030-A.desc: Obituary has been a veritable institution in death metal since 1984. With their raw sound providing the backdrop for John Tardy's inimitable voice, these Americans embody an authentic style without frills. A real highlight here at the Summer Breeze festival.

123351-031-A.title: Harakiri For The Sky - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-031-A.desc: Hailing from Austria, Harakari for the Sky combines the fury of post-black metal with a delicate, melancholic atmosphere in their compositions. Their melodic sensibility and introspective lyrics strike right for the heart. For their third appearance at Summer Breeze, this Viennese band deliver a performance as cathartic as unforgettable.

123351-035-A.title: Wind Rose - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-035-A.desc: Wind Rose from Pisa skilfully blend power metal with fantasy-inspired themes. Known for their catchy anthems such as ‘Diggy Diggy Hole’ and their powerful choruses, this group of Italians take the Summer Breeze audience on a whimsical musical adventure.

123351-037-A.title: Primordial - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-037-A.desc: Primordial returns to Summer Breeze after a nine-year absence. This Irish band are key figures in pagan metal and have a great onstage charisma, making each of their concerts an exceptional experience.

123351-038-A.title: Beast In Black - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-038-A.desc: Renowned for their power metal with epic melodies and sharp riffs, this band had already conquered Summer Breeze in 2022. This year, the Finns are back with anthems that are more powerful than ever.

123351-039-A.title: Kissin' Dynamite - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-039-A.desc: Taking freedom, fun and light-heartedness to be their mission, this German band is renowned for its catchy choruses and desire to brighten up any audience's day. Here at Summer Breeze they delivered some memorable rock anthems.

123351-040-A.title: Gaerea - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-040-A.desc: This Portuguese band embodies black metal oscillating between darkness and melancholy. Renowned for the intensity of their riffs, the group find a surprising diversity in their songs and are able to appeal to both long-time black metal fans and newcomers alike.

123351-045-A.title: Suffocation - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-045-A.desc: After an eight-year absence, Suffocation makes their grand return to Summer Breeze. These pioneers of brutal death metal stand out for their technical mastery, beastly ferocity and formidable precision. A performance combining vocal power with technical prowess.

123351-047-A.title: Destruction - Summer Breeze 2025
123351-047-A.desc: Destruction are true kings of thrash metal, a style they helped to forge. Aggressive riffs, rough vocals and intense charisma make them a global benchmark for the genre. At Summer Breeze, they deliver a performance reserved for the thrill seekers.

123375-007-A.title: Deborah de Luca - Zurich Streetparade 2025
123375-007-A.desc: From Naples to global stages, Deborah De Luca champions powerful, hypnotic techno. Intense and explosive, her sets get straight to the point, driven by absolute control and unwavering passion. At Street Parade 2025, her unique sounds wash over the shores of Lake Zurich and transform the streets into a seething rave.

123390-000-A.title: New Adult - The Boom in Romance Literature
123390-000-A.desc: Romance literature has a long history, but there is currently a boom with a new generation of writers and (mostly women) readers. This documentary looks behind the bright covers and dust jackets of New Adult romance novels and asks authors, readers and academics what is behind the phenomenon.

123404-000-A.title: A History of Elevator Music - From Muzak to Streaming
123404-000-A.desc: Invented in the 1930s in the USA to increase productivity in places of work, elevator music has become omnipresent in supermarkets and shopping centres the world over. This documentary tells the story of how this non-music, designed not to be listened to, has reached deep into our lives, without our realising.

123422-000-A.title: Shirley MacLaine - A Born Entertainer
123422-000-A.desc: A Broadway and Hollywood star from the Golden Age of American cinema to the present day, Shirley MacLaine, can sing, dance, and act with an incredible energy and talent. ARTE paints the portrait of a living legend.

123438-000-A.title: Shay - Paris Zenith
123438-000-A.desc: In November 2024, Shay brought a breathtaking show to the Paris Zénith. The performance of Acte III stands up easily to any of the biggest American stars, driven by tracks from the album Pourvu qu'il pleuve.

123594-003-A.title: Cristian Măcelaru conducts Ravel - With Beatrice Rana
123594-003-A.desc: 2025 marks 150 years since the birth of Maurice Ravel. To honour the musical legacy of this immensely influential composer, Cristian Măcelaru and the Orchestre National de France join forces with Beatrice Rana.

123594-005-A.title: Mikko Franck - With Jean-Yves Thibaudet
123594-005-A.desc: After ten years of loyal service at the helm of the Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France, Mikko Franck is flying off to new musical horizons. Before bidding farewell, the conductor and the ensemble offer us a final concert, featuring pianist Jean-Yves Thibaudet.

123594-006-A.title: Riccardo Minasi - With Emmanuel Pahud
123594-006-A.desc: Conductor Riccardo Minasi, flutist Emmanuel Pahud and the Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra join forces for a concert dedicated to Mozart, Schubert and Mendelssohn.

123594-008-A.title: Pascal Rophé conducts Berio, Mahler and Gísladóttir - With Stéphane Degout
123594-008-A.desc: Under the baton of Pascal Rophé, the Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra performs Luciano Berio's Sinfonia and Mahler's Fünf frühe Lieder, after introducing us to Bára Gísladóttir's sea sons seasons.

123662-000-A.title: Concert for Notre-Dame - La Tempête | Sabine Devieilhe | Olivier Latry
123662-000-A.desc: Simon-Pierre Bestion celebrates the musical and cultural heritage of Paris' Notre-Dame with the La Tempête ensemble and a host of guests like Sabine Devieilhe and Olivier Latry, among others.

123951-000-A.title: Dangerous Apps - How They Trade Your Data
123951-000-A.desc: Presented to users as confidential and anonymous, data collected by smartphone apps are in reality sold on by traders in a vast global marketplace. The Data Broker Files investigation by a team of German journalists reveals a highly lucrative and unregulated system that is an infringment on our privacy and a clear and present danger.

123999-000-A.title: Edward Abbey - Naturally Subversive
123999-000-A.desc: The writer and environmental activist Edward Abbey was one of the most imaginative minds of US counterculture. A so-called anarchist poet, he inspired generations of activists and artists. Abbey's cult novel "The Monkey Wrench Gang" is considered a manifesto for the green movement today. This documentary asks: Do we need radicalism or a willingness to compromise to save the planet?

124003-000-A.title: Ensemble Intercontemporain & Friends - With Jean-Guihen Queyras and Pierre-Laurent Aimard
124003-000-A.desc: The Ensemble Intercontemporain celebrates Pierre Boulez's centenary at the Philharmonie de Paris. The concert brings together Jean-Guihen Queyras and Pierre-Laurent Aimard, also featuring work by Charlotte Bray.

124004-002-A.title: Yann Tiersen - Piano Day 2025
124004-002-A.desc: For ARTE Concert's Piano Day Yann Tiersen strips back to solo piano, alone in front of his keyboard. A gripping performance taking cues from his album Rathlin from a Distance / The Liquid Hour.

124008-000-A.title: Hommage to Oum Kalthoum - Paris Philharmonic
124008-000-A.desc: Conducted by Alaa Abdelsalam and accompanied by singers Rehab Omar and Eman Abdel-Ghani, the Cairo Opera Arab Music Orchestra celebrates Oum Kalthoum.

124011-005-A.title: Luiza | L' Antidote | Buttshakers - Journeys Through Music
124011-005-A.desc: Journeys Through Music once again offers up an incredibly eclectic evening, taking us on a journey from Luiza's Franco-Brazilian pop to L'Antidote's oriental jazz and the soul of the Buttshakers.

124011-009-A.title: Imarhan, Lagon Nwar and Tribeqa - Journeys Through Music
124011-009-A.desc: This edition of Concerts Volants puts Africa in the spotlight. On the programme: Tuareg blues by Imarhan and two quartets drawing inspiration from West Africa, Lagon Nwar and Tribeqa.

124120-000-A.title: Syria's New Rulers
124120-000-A.desc: After the collapse of the Assad regime in December 2024, the interim president Ahmed al-Sharaa took over promising to heal Syria’s wounds and preserve ‘civil peace’. But has the warlord of rebel group Hayat Tahrir al-Cham (HTC), who fought under the pseudonym of Abu Mohammed Al-Joulan really left his jihadist past behind him? And will he build a state based on the rule of law that includes all parts of Syrian society?

124397-000-A.title: Caribou - Paris Zenith
124397-000-A.desc: Canadian producer Dan Snaith brings his Caribou project to the Paris Zénith on an exploration of his entire discography, combining the big tunes of the past with sweet treats from his latest album Honey.

124405-000-A.title: Being Gay in Kurdistan
124405-000-A.desc: Diako Yazdani, a political refugee in France, travels to Iraqi Kurdistan where, accompanied by Kojin, a 23-year-old gay friend, he confronts his pious family, his friends and other members of the Kurdish community with their prejudices about homosexuality.

124442-000-A.title: Hoaxes of Science
124442-000-A.desc: Rigorous use of the scientific method is the hallmark of good science. But frauds and hoaxes are not uncommon. Whether it be manipulated data, stolen findings or entirely fabricated studies, individuals exploit weaknesses in the system which undermine trust in research. How can the scientific community protect itself against fraud within its own ranks?

124447-000-A.title: Arundhati Roy - A Rebel Writer
124447-000-A.desc: With "The God of Small Things", Arundhati Roy won international acclaim as well as the Booker Prize in 1997. She became India's best-known author and an uncompromising voice against injustice and in support of human rights. This documentary is a portrait of the writer, essayist and activist and an exploration of her relationship with her mother, the subject of her recent memoir.

124449-000-A.title: Full Support
124449-000-A.desc: In the changing room of a lingerie boutique in Tel Aviv, women talk about how they feel about their bodies and their femininity. These intimate conversations reveal the way men and wider society look at them.

124773-001-A.title: The Split: Barcelona (1/2)
124773-001-A.desc: The characters from the brilliant series 'The Split', which centres on a family of London-based divorce lawyers, head to Catalonia for a two-part epilogue that explores, as always, the themes of love and passing on a legacy. Part One: Hannah attends her daughter’s wedding, where she happens to bump into a former lover.

124773-002-A.title: The Split: Barcelona (2/2)
124773-002-A.desc: The characters from the brilliant series 'The Split' head to Catalonia for a two-part epilogue centred on love – as always – and passing on a legacy. Part Two: whilst Liv’s marriage hangs in the balance, Hannah and her ex-lover must deal with a divorce case.

124774-000-A.title: Zelensky: A Decisive Year?
124774-000-A.desc: This documentary follows Volodymyr Zelensky through a year that takes him to his political, personal and moral limits: In 2025, Ukraine is hit by incessant attacks, the army is exhausted and society is fraying at the seams. Between military setbacks, accusations of corruption and risky diplomatic negotiations, the Ukrainian president struggles… Zelensky has never been under so much pressure. Will he prevail?

124800-000-A.title: Gustave Charpentier: Louise - Festival d'Aix-en-Provence
124800-000-A.desc:
Escaping from her stifling working-class family, the young Louise joins her penniless lover Julien in Paris. But disillusionment will soon set in... Christof Loy’s new reading of Gustave Charpentier’s opera is brought to life by soprano Elsa Dreisig.

During the presenter Saskia de Ville’s introduction, third-person subtitles allow non-French-speaking viewers to follow her presentation. De Ville contextualises the opera and discusses its significance with the artistic team.

124801-000-A.title: Rituel: Boulez & Millepied - With Orchestre de Paris L.A Dance Project
124801-000-A.desc: Benjamin Millepied and the L.A. Dance Project join forces with Esa-Pekka Salonen and the Orchestre de Paris to celebrate Pierre Boulez through dance. A performance to mark the composer's centenary, followed by works by Stravinsky and Bartók.

124802-000-A.title: Mikko Franck - Festival d'Aix-en-Provence 2025
124802-000-A.desc: Accompanied by the Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra and pianist Beatrice Rana, Mikko Franck conducts Shostakovich's Symphony No. 10 and Tchaikovsky's Concerto No. 1.

124809-000-A.title: Marvin Gaye - Visionary of Soul
124809-000-A.desc: The son of a Detroit pastor, Marvin Gaye impressed from the 1960s onwards with his sensual voice and songwriting craft, as well as his political lyrics during the civil rights era. This documentary paints the picture of a tormented soul and one of the United States' greatest musicians.

124812-000-A.title: Max Ernst - The Surrealist and the Fascist Monster
124812-000-A.desc: Max Ernst (1891-1976) is considered a pioneer of surrealist painting; his enigmatic, magical worlds delight and disturb the viewer: To mark the 50th anniversary of his death, this documentary tells his fascinating story from his early life in Germany up to his exile in the USA in the 1940s.

124823-000-A.title: Samara Joy - Philharmonie de Paris
124823-000-A.desc: At just 25, Samara Joy is already one of the greatest voices in jazz. On a world tour in France in support of her album Portrait, the American demonstrates all facets of her talent at the Philharmonie de Paris.

124826-000-A.title: Opera Gala - Hanover Klassik Open Air 2025
124826-000-A.desc: At the Hanover Klassik Open Air Festival, opera takes to the open air and welcomes audiences of all kinds. The 2025 edition opens its doors on 22 August 2025, with a programme featuring a best-of selection of great arias by Tchaikovsky, Borodin and Puccini performed by the NDR Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Stanislav Kochanovsky, featuring soprano Oksana Dyka, baritone Boris Pinkhasovich and tenor Liparit Avetisyan.

125537-000-A.title: Disunited Nations - The UN and the Middle East
125537-000-A.desc:
In March 2024, Francesca Albanese, the UN Special Rapporteur on the Occupied Palestinian territories, denounced genocide in Gaza. Following in her footsteps, this documentary plunges into the heart of the UN’s crisis, confronted by its inability to prevent the massacre of civilians.

The UN was newly created when the Partition Plan for Palestine was decided in 1947. Will the organisation collapse over the Palestine issue?

125549-000-A.title: Sahara Prison
125549-000-A.desc: On 25 November 2011, Johan Gustafsson set off from his native Sweden on an adventure of a lifetime: a motorbike trip across Africa. But his dream journey turned into a nightmare when he was kidnapped by Al-Qaeda, along with a South African and a Dutchman. What followed was a terrible ordeal of over five years of captivity in the Sahara desert.

125633-001-A.title: Patrick Melrose (1/5) - Bad News
125633-001-A.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past. An elegant mixture of humour and tragedy, carried by the multi-faceted Benedict Cumberbatch. Episode one: On the day he learns that his father has died, Patrick is otherwise disposed…

125633-002-A.title: Patrick Melrose (2/5) - Never Mind
125633-002-A.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past, starring Benedict Cumberbatch. Episode two: Patrick returns to London in bad spirits and the memory of a holiday spent with his parents resurfaces.

125633-003-A.title: Patrick Melrose (3/5) - Some Hope
125633-003-A.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past, starring Benedict Cumberbatch. Episode three: Patrick tries to banish drugs and alcohol from his life. At a party, the depravity of his surroundings is revealed.

125633-004-A.title: Patrick Melrose (4/5) - Mother's Milk
125633-004-A.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past, starring Benedict Cumberbatch. Fourth episode: Patrick, married with children, is on holiday with his sick mother.

125633-005-A.title: Patrick Melrose (5/5) - At Last
125633-005-A.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past, starring Benedict Cumberbatch. Final episode: at Eleanor's funeral, Patrick confronts his demons for the last time.

125634-011-A.title: Save Me Too - Season 2 (5/6)
125634-011-A.desc: Convinced that his daughter has been kidnapped by a paedophile ring, Nelly leads his own investigation to find her. A thriller created and starring Lennie James (The Walking Dead). Episode 5: Grace is unable to reach Nelly and asks the help of an old acquaintance to get out of the city.

125653-000-A.title: "L'Opera Seria" - Teatro alla Scala Milan
125653-000-A.desc: In search of an opera for his theatre, an impresario finds himself grappling with a group of egotists and jealous individuals. Based on a score by Florian Leopold Gassmann, this caustic satire of the 18th-century opera world is directed by Laurent Pelly. Starring Julie Fuchs as the prima donna.

125654-000-A.title: What Are We Going to Do With You?
125654-000-A.desc: Between playing, colouring in and singing, children in a Paris primary school learn about the impact of political decisions on their everyday lives and their future. As a result, children's language reveals a surprising sensitivity to social issues at an early age. The documentary sheds light on the future of the education system.

125655-000-A.title: Gaël Faye - Paris Louvre
125655-000-A.desc: The power of flow versus the power of waves: At the Louvre, in front of Géricault's gigantic canvas, Gaël Faye reinterprets his songs in subtle arrangements for piano and strings. A mesmerising event.

125691-000-A.title: Outgrow the System
125691-000-A.desc: "Change the system, not the climate" is a common demand in the climate movement. But what kind of system do we actually want? In the midst of humanity's worst crisis and biggest challenge, there are pioneers ready to revolutionise our economy. This film explores their ideas and delves deep into what a sustainable economy could look like.

125786-000-A.title: Jamie XX - Paris Adidas Arena
125786-000-A.desc: British producer Jamie xx takes the Adidas Arena on an epic electronic journey. A kaleidoscopic trip, punctuated by jewels from his second album proper In Waves.

125900-000-A.title: Tom Hanks - An Ordinary Hero
125900-000-A.desc: Through hard work and focus, Tom Hanks became one of Hollywood's most famous actors and a role model for many people as an ordinary hero and the archetypal American. With extensive interviews, this documentary paints a picture of one of America’s most popular and most bankable actors.

126095-000-A.title: Trump's America - The Great Divide
126095-000-A.desc: Just two and a half hours drive from Washington D.C, Piedmont is a world away. Whilst in the democrat-leaning capital Trump’s second term in office is viewed with consternation and fear, local people living in Appalachian Piedmont are hopeful for economic renewal with the re-elected president. This documentary follows the highs and lows of politics in everyday life in a divided country.

126174-000-A.title: Martin Scorsese - My Life in Music
126174-000-A.desc: For Martin Scorsese, music is a vital component in his films - almost as important as the actors themselves. The American filmmaker works with great composers to create the soundtracks for his films but also uses pre-existing music which is equally important for him. In this documentary, he also talks about the role music played when he was growing up in New York's Little Italy.

126177-001-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (1/18)
126177-001-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode 1: trapped for twenty-five years in the Black Lodge, Agent Cooper receives a new message from the giant...

126177-002-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (2/18)
126177-002-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode two: Cooper's evil double tries to escape the Black Lodge, while good Dale tries to get out.

126177-003-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (3/18)
126177-003-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Third episode: Agent Cooper finds himself in an unfamiliar world; his evil doppelganger is overcome by vertigo...

126177-004-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (4/18)
126177-004-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode four: Agent Cooper gets lucky at the casino and gradually slips into the life of his doppelganger Dougie.

126177-005-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (5/18)
126177-005-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode five: Cooper discovers where his double works; the Pentagon opens an investigation.

126177-006-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (6/18)
126177-006-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode six: Dougie/Dale is taken home by a policeman; the one-armed man reappears ...

126177-007-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (7/18)
126177-007-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode seven: Hawk examines pages torn from Laura Palmer's diary; Lieutenant Knox from the Pentagon makes a startling discovery.

126177-008-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (8/18)
126177-008-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode eight: Ray Monroe and Mr C. escape, but nothing goes according to plan.

126177-009-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (9/18)
126177-009-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode nine: Mr C. has survived the attack and now seeks revenge; the police make a discovery about Dougie Jones.

126177-010-A.title: Twin Peaks : The Return (10/18)
126177-010-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter.
