Episode ten: Richard Horne tries to escape from the police after his accident; the Mitchum brothers track down Dougie Jones...

126177-011-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (11/18)
126177-011-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode eleven: Bill Hastings leads the FBI team to the place where he met Major Briggs...

126177-012-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (12/18)
126177-012-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode twelve: the FBI's special cases team expands, while Laura Palmer's mother senses a threat approaching...

126177-013-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (13/18)
126177-013-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode thirteen: Dougie Jones narrowly escapes a deathly situation; Mr C. settles his score with Ray Monroe ...

126177-014-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (14/18)
126177-014-A.desc: 25 years after the end of the second season, David Lynch and Mark Frost continue the mystery of Twin Peaks in a multi-layered and fascinating third chapter. Episode fourteen: Gordon Cole dreams of Monica Bellucci, Diane reunites with her half-sister and the sheriff's team makes a discovery.

126177-015-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (15/18)
126177-015-A.desc: FBI agent Dale Cooper continues his investigation into the murder of Laura Palmer, uncovering the secrets of Twin Peaks' inhabitants. Episode fifteen: Mr C. talks to Phillip Jeffries and the Log Lady says goodbye...

126177-016-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (16/18)
126177-016-A.desc: FBI agent Dale Cooper continues his investigation into the murder of Laura Palmer, uncovering the secrets of Twin Peaks' inhabitants. Episode sixteen: Mr C. takes Richard Horne into the desert, Dale Cooper wakes up from his coma and Diane reveals her true nature...

126177-017-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (17/18)
126177-017-A.desc: FBI agent Dale Cooper continues his investigation into the murder of Laura Palmer, uncovering the secrets of Twin Peaks' inhabitants. Episode seventeen: Gordon Cole makes revelations; the two Coopers arrive in Twin Peaks...

126177-018-A.title: Twin Peaks: The Return (18/18)
126177-018-A.desc: FBI agent Dale Cooper continues his investigation into the murder of Laura Palmer, uncovering the secrets of Twin Peaks' inhabitants. Final episode: Dale Cooper crosses a border with Diane, then spends the night with her in a motel. The next morning, he wakes up alone…

126188-000-A.title: Far-Right Terrorism (2/2)
126188-000-A.desc: In Europe and the United States, attacks on minorities and democratic institutions are on the increase. Part two of our documentary on far right terrorism: An international ideology is rising that venerates far-right mass murderers as saints.

126232-000-A.title: Jason Mraz - Folies Bergère, Paris
126232-000-A.desc: Back in Europe for a new tour, American singer-songwriter Jason Mraz stopped off at the Folies Bergère in Paris for an intimate acoustic concert, where his soulful voice and catchy melodies delighted the audience.

126235-000-A.title: Zaho de Sagazan Symphonique - The Lyon Auditorium 2025
126235-000-A.desc: This rising star of French music revisits her album ‘La symphonie des éclairs’ with musicians from the Orchestre National de Lyon. A special rearrangement for a unique musical journey of hypnotic intensity.

126245-000-A.title: The Zola Experience
126245-000-A.desc: For her adaptation of Zola’s classic novel ‘L’Assommoir’ for the stage, Anne Barbot casts her new neighbour, an unemployed actress. Reality and fiction become blurred in a celebration of theatrical and cinematic creativity.

126458-000-A.title: Beatrice Rana - Lucerne Festival 2025
126458-000-A.desc: The Lucerne Festival Orchestra and its music director Riccardo Chailly dedicate an evening to Sergei Rachmaninoff, with Italian pianist Beatrice Rana shining in the highly virtuosic variations of the Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini. Also on the programme is the mysterious Symphony No. 1 and The Rock, an early work that already gives a glimpse of the Russian composer's musical sensibility.

126462-006-A.title: Venga Venga - Europavox Sessions 2025
126462-006-A.desc: Venga Venga is all about the generosity and extravagance of Brazil. This fabulous duo unveil themselves to Europavox festival-goers with a colourful performance.

126464-048-A.title: Sex Pistols & Frank Carter - Hellfest 2025
126464-048-A.desc: Bringing Frank Carter on as front man, the Sex Pistols successfully bridge the gap between the old school and the future. This concert at Hellfest marks a historic moment in the history of punk.

126467-000-A.title: Laurie Anderson - Saint Joan of Arc Basilica, Paris
126467-000-A.desc: In the inspiring setting of a deconsecrated Parisian church, iconic experimental artist Laurie Anderson performs a new private concert. A timeless, spellbinding and elegant interlude.

126469-005-A.title: Alle Farben - Nature One 2025
126469-005-A.desc:
‘Nature One is definitely one of my favourite festivals. To you, who have been celebrating electronic music for 30 years, happy birthday!’

Frans Zimmer, alias Alle Farben, has been giving the crowds gathered in front of the OpenAirFloor a number of epic moments over the years. With 20 years of experience on the circuit, he is one of the best when it comes to deep tech house and festival anthems. With over 2 billion streams and 15 top 10 hits (including 5 number 1 hits in Europe), Alle Farben is also one of Germany's most successful exports.

Filmed on 2 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-006-A.title: A.N.I. - Nature One 2025
126469-006-A.desc:
This Berlin-based DJ and producer is taking the techno scene by storm after making a name for herself as a classical pianist and sound engineer. Her passion for hard techno and hard trance has led her to create a unique sound that has gone viral on social media. Her DJ mix sessions recorded in her flat propelled her to the forefront of the scene in 2024.
Filmed on 1 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-007-A.title: Charly Lownoise - Nature One 2025
126469-007-A.desc:
Whether performing as part of the duo Mental Theo in the 1990s or solo today, Charly Lownoise brings generations together with his high-energy music and unique vibes. This legend of happy hardcore and Nature One is the author of iconic tracks such as ‘Wonderful Days’, which have long enjoyed cult status.

Filmed on 1 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-008-A.title: Chris Liebing b2b Luke Slater - Nature One 2025
126469-008-A.desc:
Chris Liebing, a native of Gießen, Germany, is a true pioneer of electronic music. For some thirty years, he has left his mark on the electro scene as a label owner, podcast creator, DJ and producer. His musical range spans from the raw energy of 1990s techno to the minimalist accents of the early 2000s. British artist Luke Slater has developed a unique musical vision at the forefront of the British techno scene, attracting attention since the 1990s with his dynamic and powerful sound. Two styles, two legends, one epic performance.

Filmed on 2 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-009-A.title: CVMB (Marco Bailey b2b Cristian Varela) - Nature One 2025
126469-009-A.desc:
These two techno legends and veteran DJs join forces for a set at Century Circus. On the menu: Pressure, pulse and pure passion. Cristian Varela joined the Nature One family in 1999, followed by Marco Bailey in 2000. With their many years of experience, they deliver an epic set to Nature One festival-goers.

Filmed on 1 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-010-A.title: Dominik Eulberg - NatureOne 2025
126469-010-A.desc: Dominik Eulberg likes the Nature One festival ‘because it was born and developed out of the deepest idealism’, he said in an ARD report. Alongside his career as a DJ, he's also a nature specialist and author known for his biodiversity programmes. He says that walking in the forest is a source of inspiration, because ‘you never know what's waiting for you behind the next bush’.

126469-012-A.title: Klaudia Gawlas - Nature One 2025
126469-012-A.desc:
‘For me, Century Circus is pure magic, a stage unlike any other in the world. I get goosebumps every time I walk in,’ Klaudia Gawlas told FAZEmag. At the age of 13, she was already creating music on her Commodore 64 computer. In 2013, she achieved success with ‘Papillon’. Today, she continues her career on her label Illusion Records and at Filth on Acid. In 2024, FAZEmag magazine named her DJ of the Year for the sixth time!

Filmed on 2 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-014-A.title: Paul van Dyk - Nature One 2025
126469-014-A.desc: ‘Let's dance until the stars rain down on this former military base. See you on the dance floor!’ That's right, techno legend Paul van Dyk has spoken. So head to Nature One to check out his set at the anniversary edition of Germany's oldest outdoor electronic music festival.

126469-017-A.title: WestBam - Nature One 2025
126469-017-A.desc:
WestBam has been one of the leading figures in electronic music since the 1980s. An avant-garde and innovative personality on the German techno and house scene, he is not only a musician but also a visionary who has permanently transformed German club culture and propelled techno into the mainstream. In addition to his numerous productions, he has also written a book and inspired generations of musicians.

Filmed on 2 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126469-018-A.title: Plastik Funk - NatureOne 2025
126469-018-A.desc: With over 100 million streams and the support of heavyweights like David Guetta and Don Diablo- not to mention appearances at some of the world's biggest festivals- Plastik Funk has already made waves on the international scene. Last year, he celebrated summer on the Open Air Floor with his hits ‘Groove Tonight’, 'Sideways' and ‘Sweet Harmony’. For the 2025 edition of the festival, he's got more tricks up his sleeve.

126469-021-A.title: Moguai - Nature One 2025
126469-021-A.desc: Sets from Moguai over the years have seen him climb the ranks within NatureOne. 2023 saw him release ‘The Power The Change’ with Lili Pistorius, which became the official anthem of the festival. Today, he divides his time between Berlin and Los Angeles, but his music is everywhere, particularly with his weekly radio show PUNX Up The Volume, which broadcasts the hottest hits from the DJ scene across 40 countries.

126469-022-A.title: Mha Iri - Nature One 2025
126469-022-A.desc:
A rising star and already a key figure on the techno scene, Mha Iri has made her mark with a style that combines big-room energy and emotionally rich vocals. Born in Edinburgh and shaped by Scottish underground culture, she found her calling in Melbourne, where she quickly established herself as a DJ and party promoter. Since 2018, the artist has been forging her musical identity and gaining notoriety thanks to numerous releases on leading labels such as Drumcode, Filth on Acid and Trick. From Tomorrowland to Hï Ibiza, the artist delights EDM fans with a cocktail of powerful sounds and emotion.

Filmed on 3 August 2025 at the Nature One festival in Kastellaun, Germany.

126473-000-A.title: Elīna Garanča - Lucerne Festival 2025
126473-000-A.desc: Since its founding in 2003, the Lucerne Festival Orchestra has established itself as a benchmark in the interpretation of Mahler. The composer's Symphony No. 10 and his Rückert Lieder, performed by Elīna Garanča and conducted by Riccardo Chailly will open the 2025 Lucerne Festival with a tribute to Pierre Boulez, another figure of this renowned festival.

126927-000-A.title: Trump's America - Power, Change, Resistance
126927-000-A.desc: With his ultra-conservative policies, US President Donald Trump is disrupting the global order as well as US institutions and society. Filmed less than one year after the start of his second mandate, this documentary analyses the concrete impact of his policies on the American people.

126987-000-A.title: This is Orson Welles
126987-000-A.desc: Considered one of the greatest and most influential filmmakers of all time, Orson Welles made his mark on 20th century cinema like no other. This documentary investigates Welles’ personal life and long career with interviews with his eldest daughter Chris and friends such as Peter Bogdanovich as well as filmmakers including Martin Scorsese.

127016-001-A.title: America Who Are You? (1/2) - A History of the American Mindset
127016-001-A.desc: To mark the 250th anniversary of US independence, this documentary examines the values upon which the American nation is founded – a nation now in the midst of Trump’s second presidential term – and is structured around four main themes: the ideal of freedom, the prominence of religion, the American dream and love of country.

127016-002-A.title: America Who Are You? (2/2) - A History of the American Mindset
127016-002-A.desc: Linked to the principle of freedom and the concept of divine blessing, the figure of the self-made man contributes to the development of American-style capitalism: the state must guarantee fundamental rights, but must not interfere in the economy.

127030-000-A.title: Iggy Pop - Spandau Citadel, Berlin 2025
127030-000-A.desc: Fifty years after his first steps on the streets of Berlin, the city that symbolises his artistic rebirth alongside David Bowie, Iggy Pop sets the stage alight at the Spandau Citadel. It's an opportunity to confirm that, at over 70 years of age, the charismatic former leader of The Stooges remains the undisputed godfather of punk.

127032-000-A.title: Tribute to David Bowie - Heroes Never Die
127032-000-A.desc: The Molotovs, Anna Calvi feat. Isobel Waller-Bridge, Jeanne Added, Peter Doherty and Carl Barât, The Divine Comedy... In eight intense covers, Bowie's voice resonates through singular artists who evoke his immense influence on their music.

127034-000-A.title: Hilma Af Klint - Pioneer of Abstract Art
127034-000-A.desc: As a major retrospective of her work takes place in Paris from May 2026, this documentary looks at the fascinating life and work of Hilma af Klint (1862-1944). A relative unknown, the Swedish painter was a pioneer of abstract and modern art, pre-dating Kandinsky and Mondrian with many of her mystical paintings only discovered decades after her death.

127035-000-A.title: Renoir in Love
127035-000-A.desc: Pierre-Auguste Renoir (1841-1919) is one of the most popular impressionist painters, famous for much-loved paintings such as “The Luncheon of the Boating Party”. But critics have sometimes been less impressed by an artist who could be considered superficial. This documentary sheds light on Renoir’s paintings. Could they be more modern and sophisticated than we imagine?

127036-000-A.title: Rodin and Michelangelo - The Song of Statues
127036-000-A.desc: Rodin and Michelangelo were both celebrated sculptors of their ages. This documentary brings together the works of the French and Italian sculptors and reveals the creative processes of the two artists.

127039-000-A.title: Red River
127039-000-A.desc: Since the end of the armed conflict between the Colombian state and FARC, the natural beauty of the Cano Cristales, a Colombian river also known as the River of Five Colours, is under threat from tourism and private sector exploitation. Guillermo Quintero's film shows the paradox of peace, which has brought new problems to a remote region of the Amazon.

127055-000-A.title: Vienna Opera Gala - With Elina Garança, Sonya Yoncheva, Benjamin Bernheim and Jonas Kaufmann
127055-000-A.desc: The Vienna State Opera celebrates the 80th anniversary of the Second Austrian Republic in the Burggarten gardens and offers a preview of the 2025–2026 season. The programme features the prestigious voices of Elīna Garanča, Jonas Kaufmann and Benjamin Bernheim, among others, with the Opera Orchestra conducted by Bertrand de Billy.

127056-001-A.title: Woods of Birnam - Come to the Woods Open Air Festival 2025
127056-001-A.desc: Woods of Birnam brought the Come to the Woods Festival in Dresden to a rousing close. The finale was all gleaming synth rock, with the charisma of frontman Christian Friedel shining through.

127065-001-A.title: Yom & Théo and Valentin Ceccaldi - Jazz à Porquerolles 2025
127065-001-A.desc: At Jazz à Porquerolles, Yom and brothers Théo and Valentin Ceccaldi join forces on a project as audacious as it is meditative: Le Rythme du Silence.

127065-002-A.title: Ballake Sissoko & Piers Faccini - Jazz à Porquerolles 2025
127065-002-A.desc: On the Jazz à Porquerolles stage, Ballaké Sissoko's kora blends with the delicate folk of Piers Faccini. It's a subtle and spellbinding union, sealed by a four-handed album: Our Calling.

127065-003-A.title: Sona Jobarteh - Jazz à Porquerolles 2025
127065-003-A.desc: Singer, multi-instrumentalist and composer Sona Jobarteh brings the sound of her kora to the jazz festival in Porquerolles.

127065-004-A.title: Jowee Omicil - Jazz à Porquerolles 2025
127065-004-A.desc: Jowee Omicil casts a spell over the Jazz à Porquerolles festival with a spiritual and festive maelstrom combining jazz, gospel, Afro-Haitian rhythms and hip-hop.

127069-000-A.title: Kae Tempest - Echoes with Jehnny Beth
127069-000-A.desc: A major figure in British spoken word, Kae Tempest performs tracks from his new album, ‘Self Titled’. He delivers a sharp flow over electric melodies that are both soaring and raw, revealing dark, committed, uncompromising poetry – an X-ray of contemporary England.

127070-001-A.title: Isaiah Collier - TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival 2025
127070-001-A.desc: Isaiah Collier is one of the most exciting young American saxophonists of the moment. Meet this phenomenon at the 2025 TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival.

127070-002-A.title: Marcus Miller - TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival 2025
127070-002-A.desc: The celebrated New York bassist Marcus Miller, steeped in jazz and soul, delivers a generous performance for the TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival audience.

127070-003-A.title: Sullivan Fortner Trio - TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival 2025
127070-003-A.desc: Pianist Sullivan Fortner appears in a trio at the TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival accompanied by double bassist Tyrone Allen and drummer Kayvon Gordon. Together they introduce us to Southern Nights, a musical ode to New Orleans.

127070-004-A.title: Kamasi Washington - TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival 2025
127070-004-A.desc: Brilliant iconoclast Kamasi Washington is one of the most astonishing saxophonists of his generation. Witness a demonstration of his mastery in the enchanting setting of the TSF Jazz Chantilly Festival.

127359-000-A.title: Alborosie & Shengen Clan - Summerjam Festival 2025
127359-000-A.desc:
Reggae artist Alborosie and his group Shengen Clan make a memorable performance at German gathering, Summerjam Festival 2025.

Filmed 4 July at Summerjam Festival, Cologne.

127360-000-A.title: Famous Orchestras - Orchestre National de France
127360-000-A.desc: Created in 1934 as a national radio orchestra, the Orchestre National de France brings music into French people’s lives from its home concert halls in Paris. Cristian Macelaru currently conducts the prestigious ensemble but many maestros have led the orchestra over the years, such as Sergiu Celibidache, Leonard Bernstein, Lorin Maazel, Seiji Ozawa, Kurt Masur and Daniele Gatti.

127361-000-A.title: Famous Orchestras - Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra Amsterdam
127361-000-A.desc: Famous Orchestras brings you inside the most prestigious orchestras in the world. In this episode: The Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra is a highly acclaimed ensemble which plays in the special acoustics of their home hall the Concertgebouw, opened in 1888. Conductor Klaus Mäkelä is always striving for that special sound which has made the orchestra’s reputation.

127396-000-A.title: Trump: No Limit
127396-000-A.desc: Donald Trump has pursued an aggressively protectionist approach to trade since his return to the White House, imposing large tariffs on imports from many countries and using the threat of high tariffs to bully countries into acting how he would like. This documentary looks at the workings of Trump’s imperialist ambitions through economic pressure.

127428-000-A.title: The Breach
127428-000-A.desc: Hélène Régnier leaves her troubled husband Charles after he attacks their son. But her wealthy in-laws are an obstacle to her freedom and happiness. With Stéphane Audran, Jean-Pierre Cassel and Jean-Claude Drouot.

127499-000-A.title: Paula Modersohn-Becker - No Compromises
127499-000-A.desc: Today German artist Paula Modersohn-Becker (1876-1907) is famous worldwide. But during her lifetime, her unconventional painting irked her contemporaries as she radically broke taboos, anticipating both Expressionism and Modernism. This documentary portrays an artist who unwaveringly pursued her vision for art, ahead of her time and without compromises.

127507-002-A.title: Wu Lyf - ARTE Concert Festival 2025
127507-002-A.desc: Wu Lyf's raw rock is back! To celebrate the Manchester band's comeback in style, head to La Gaîté Lyrique and the 2025 edition of the ARTE Concert Festival.

127507-003-A.title: Mac DeMarco - ARTE Concert Festival 2025
127507-003-A.desc: With his trademark cap firmly planted on his head, Mac DeMarco gives an intimate concert to the audience at La Gaîté Lyrique. The Canadian indie rock star presents Guitar, his sixth album.

127507-005-A.title: Sudan Archives - ARTE Concert Festival 2025
127507-005-A.desc: The android violinist Sudan Archives is coming to the ARTE Concert Festival! With her album THE BPM, featuring R&B beats and avant-garde sounds, the American artist takes the violin and the Gaîté Lyrique to uncharted territory.

127507-006-A.title: Baxter Dury - ARTE Concert Festival 2025
127507-006-A.desc: British dandy Baxter Dury performs some of his greatest tongue-in-cheek hits at the 2025 ARTE Concert Festival, as well as a few tracks from his chain-pub referencing ninth album, Allbarone.

127513-001-A.title: SHDW b2b Stojche - Stone Techno 2025
127513-001-A.desc: Founder of the Mutual Rytm label, SHDW is one of the leading figures on the German techno scene. And his influence is far from confined to Germany. With a solo career still in its infancy he's already setting the tone. A trailblazer showing the way forward.

127513-002-A.title: Ogazón - Stone Techno 2025
127513-002-A.desc: In recent years, Berlin-based DJ Ogazón has made a name for herself around the world. With unerring flair, she delivers stripped-down techno that is as hypnotic as it is percussive, with forays into dub. At the Stone Techno Festival 2025, she provides a captivating and euphoric soundscape for the audience.

127513-003-A.title: Marrøn - Stone Techno 2025
127513-003-A.desc:
Resident DJ at Eerste Communie, MARRØN is one of the most prominent figures on the current techno scene. His previous appearances at Stone Techno left festival-goers with unforgettable memories. 2025 is an opportunity for him to demonstrate once again that he is unrivalled in creating hypnotic, groovy odysseys where percussion reigns supreme.
Filmed on 11 July 2025 during the Stone Techno festival at Zollverein, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

127513-005-A.title: Nastia - Stone Techno 2025
127513-005-A.desc: Founder of the Nechto label, Nastia is one of the most influential figures on the international techno scene. A fan of intense, raw and atmospheric sounds, she has made her mark far beyond her native Ukraine. At the Stone Techno Festival 2025, she transports the audience into her hypnotic world.

127513-007-A.title: Cinthie - Stone Techno 2025
127513-007-A.desc: Cinthie is known for her pure house music, a harmonious blend of classic and modern elements. Founder of the 803 Crystal Grooves label and the Elevate record shop, she has an unerring flair for both editorial and creative work. With her, the Stone Techno dance floor is immersed in a unique atmosphere.

127513-008-A.title: Stef Mendesidis - Stone Techno 2025
127513-008-A.desc: With releases on Klockworks, ARTS and R&S Records, Stef Mendesidis has caused a sensation around the world. His signature sound is a clever balance of distortion, groove and harmonic layers that crystallise in sets that couldn't be more energetic. 2025 marks his debut at the Stone Techno Festival. To say that this first appearance was eagerly awaited would be an understatement.

127513-009-A.title: Ben Klock - Stone Techno 2025
127513-009-A.desc: Berghain resident Ben Klock is one of the pillars of Berlin's techno scene, known for his legendary tracks that have influenced an entire generation of ravers. At the Stone Techno 2025 festival, he delivers one of his finely crafted sets, combining deep, rolling basslines with hypnotic grooves.

127513-010-A.title: Deetron b2b Tasha - Stone Techno 2025
127513-010-A.desc: Born in Switzerland, DJ and producer Deetron has made his mark on the electro scene with his highly personal fusion of techno elements and the most cutting-edge house music. With a career spanning two decades, he wields the art of mixing with rare fluidity. The result is tracks with a thousand and one layers of rich complexity.

127513-011-A.title: KiNK b2b Raredub - Stone Techno 2025
127513-011-A.desc: KiNK is one of the most innovative and talented figures on the electro scene. This Bulgarian artist is renowned for his mix of house and techno blended with improvisation, bringing to life soundscapes that are as powerful as they are spontaneous. After his acclaimed debut at Stone Techno in 2022, this year marks his big comeback to Essen.

127633-005-A.title: Landmvrks - Les Eurockéennes de Belfort 2025
127633-005-A.desc: Formed in Marseille in 2014, Landmvrks have quickly established themselves as one of the spearheads of the European metalcore scene. Blending powerful riffs, ultra-effective breakdowns and catchy choruses, their live show is explosive, their delivery heartfelt.

127719-004-A.title: L’Impératrice - Bilbao BBK Live Festival 2025
127719-004-A.desc: The six members of L'Impératrice get Bilbao BBK Live dancing with their swayful pop. A trans-Pyrenean meeting to introduce Spanish audiences to Pulsar, their third album.

127936-000-A.title: Ingeborg Bachmann - Rebel Poet
127936-000-A.desc: On 25 June, the literary world celebrates the 100th anniversary of poet Ingeborg Bachmann’s birth. This documentary tells the story of how Bachmann became one of the most important writers of the 20th century in the German-speaking world, and investigates her untimely and mysterious death in 1973 in Rome.

127952-000-A.title: Birgitt Haas Must Be Killed
127952-000-A.desc: An unremarkable unemployed man (Jean Rochefort) is manipulated by a French secret service agent (Philippe Noiret) to serve as cover for the assassination of a former German terrorist (Lisa Kreuzer). Featuring a strong trio of actors, this spy film also tells an unexpected love story.

128035-000-A.title: Dolly - The Cloning Revolution
128035-000-A.desc: What medical prospects does cloning hold, three decades after the birth of Dolly the sheep? This documentary examines the bioethical issues surrounding a technique that both fascinates and causes concern.

128086-000-A.title: The History of Hair in Art
128086-000-A.desc: What does hair symbolise in the visual arts? From the 15th century to the present day, here is a fascinating retrospective that sheds light on issues relating to these depictions, with valuable contributions from a number of contemporary artists specialising in the subject.

128099-000-A.title: Restless Spirit - Carl Maria von Weber
128099-000-A.desc: World-famous for his opera The Marksman (Der Freischütz), considered one of the pillars of the German Romantic repertoire, Carl Maria von Weber reinvigorated musical composition in the early 19th century. This documentary is a portrait of an inventive and free-spirited figure of classical music.

128120-002-A.title: Julia Leshneva & Franco Fagioli - Bayreuth Baroque 2025
128120-002-A.desc: Soprano Julia Lezhneva and countertenor Franco Fagioli present an exceptional evening. The programme features arias and duets by the great masters of the Baroque: Purcell, Corelli, Handel, Graun and Porpora, Haydn and Farinelli's Italian master, but also Handel's great rival. Passionate arias, high-pitched vocalises, virtuosity and sonic exuberance: A real treat!

128120-005-A.title: Malena Ernman: Terra Mater - Bayreuth Baroque 2025
128120-005-A.desc: In their concert Terra Mater, leading figures on the early music scene Christina Pluhar and her ensemble L'Arpeggiata celebrate Mother Nature with Swedish mezzo-soprano Malena Ernman. Vocal and instrumental art intertwine with delight until they merge, evoking not only the exuberance of Baroque nature, but also its fragility. A conceptual concert rich in associations.

128178-001-A.title: Adèle Castillon - Cabaret Vert 2025
128178-001-A.desc: With her second album Crèvecoeur, Adèle Castillon takes her electropop sound to new heights within the French-speaking pop scene. See what the buzz is all about here at Cabaret Vert.

128178-003-A.title: SDM - Cabaret Vert 2025
128178-003-A.desc: With third album A la Vie à la Mort SDM has gone from heavyweight to giant of French-language rap. Witness this transformation live at Cabaret Vert.

128178-005-A.title: Trym - Cabaret Vert 2025
128178-005-A.desc: Harddance, Hardtrance, Techno, Acid... Whatever label you put on it, Trym's music is geared towards getting you dancing. Check out his performance on the Zanzibar stage at Cabaret Vert.

128191-001-A.title: Camila Arriva Grupo - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-001-A.desc: "Vivan las mujeres!" The message is unambiguous: Camila Arriva Grupo is all about women. These five Argentinian musicians shake up traditional gender roles with their new approach to tango. The men are out, the women are in. A far cry from the traditional image of the tango artform.

128191-002-A.title: Nik West - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-002-A.desc: Bardetreffen headliner Nik West brings her trademark punchy bass playing and magnetic on-stage energy to the festival, no equal when it comes to working up her audience. Prince, Quincy Jones and Santana have all invited her on stage during their concerts: A musician with the energy of a jet engine!

128191-004-A.title: Ana Frango El’étrico - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-004-A.desc: Singer, guitarist and producer Ana Frango Elétrico is an emblem of the Brazilian indie scene and has made juggling numerous influences her trademark: So much so that her approach has been described as a ‘groovy time machine’. A blend of bossa nova, funk, disco, lounge music and city pop, witness her music electrify the main stage at Nuremberg's Bardentreffen.

128191-006-A.title: Dobet Gnahoré - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-006-A.desc: In a career spanning more than twenty years, Dobet Gnahoré's timeless pan-African music has won numerous awards. This recognition and her legendary choreography has made her the queen of Afro-pop and a powerful icon who embodies her native continent on the international stage.

128191-007-A.title: Tarta Relena - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-007-A.desc: The name Tarta Relena conjures up images of a traditional Catalan pastry. In concert, their sensual, crystal-clear voices oscillate effortlessly between futuristic sounds and traditional Catalan harmonies; a refined experimental music that has the flavour of a mystical experience.

128191-008-A.title: Balkan Paradise Orchestra - Bardentreffen 2025
128191-008-A.desc: Since it was founded in Barcelona in 2015 by ten female wind musicians, the Balkan Paradise Orchestra has established itself in a field traditionally reserved for men. These ten Spanish artists deliver a refreshing performance combining traditional Balkan fanfare and dance music and have already toured at festivals all over the world. Here they thrill the audience at Nuremberg's Bardentreffen in 2025.

128476-001-A.title: ARTE Mix ø Trabendo 2025 - Live event with Cassius, DJ Falcon, Etienne de Crécy and Tatyana Jane
128476-001-A.desc: Once again this year, ARTE Mix ø Trabendo brings together an XXL line-up of heavyweights and rising stars to kick off the new season of electro events on ARTE Concert: Etienne de Crécy, Cassius, DJ Falcon, Dylan Dylan, Nathalie Duchene, and Tatyana Jane. More than enough to keep you dancing until dawn!

128600-001-A.title: Marco da Silva Ferreira: F*cking Future - Lyon Biennale de la Danse 2025
128600-001-A.desc: Portuguese choreographer Marco da Silva Ferreira explores the relationship between dance and patriarchy in F*cking Future, a show presented at the Lyon Dance Biennale.

128600-002-A.title: Aina Alegre: FUGACES - Lyon Biennale de la Danse 2025
128600-002-A.desc: At the Lyon Dance Biennale, Aina Alegre, the Grenoble National Choreographic Centre and STUDIO FICTIF celebrate the legacy of Carmen Amaya, who revolutionised flamenco in the 1930s.

128669-000-A.title: Chin Up!
128669-000-A.desc: Pregnant with her first child, Emma learns she has breast cancer. In her debut feature, filmmaker Sólveig Anspach, who passed away in 2015, depicts the struggles she was facing in the twilight of her life. A deeply moving and authentic story, brought to life with profound humanity by Karin Viard.

128786-000-A.title: Sabotage - Resistance in Auschwitz
128786-000-A.desc: In January 1945, four women were hanged in the Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp. They were members of a resistance group that had helped to prepare an uprising in October 1944. Sabotage tells their dramatic story from the perspective of Anna Wajcblum, whose older sister was involved.

128792-000-A.title: Wildfires: New Ways to Fight Them
128792-000-A.desc: With climate change increasing the risk of wildfires in Europe every summer, strategies to prevent them are becoming ever more important. This documentary meets experts from across the continent who are bringing new approaches to firefighting.

128794-000-A.title: Vanessa Wagner & Friends - Paris Sur Mesure
128794-000-A.desc: Pianist Vanessa Wagner joins forces with circus artist Chloé Moglia, choreographer Kader Attou, dancers from the Accrorap company and set designers from Collectif Scale to bring Philip Glass's Piano Etudes to life.

128799-000-A.title: Queens of the Stone Age - Baloise Session 2025
128799-000-A.desc: After electrifying the Paris rock scene with their ‘Alive In The Catacombs’ concert in 2024, Queens of the Stone Age are coming to Switzerland. Josh Homme's band revealed a new side to themselves in the catacomb tunnels and will be presenting this rare project at the Baloise Session, one of seven European dates.

128839-000-A.title: Bach: St John's Passion - NDR Vocal Ensemble & Concerto Köln
128839-000-A.desc:
Johann Sebastian Bach's St John Passion depicts the trial of Jesus Christ in impressive musical images. The Evangelist acts as narrator as the story is told in a sequence of arias, chorales and solo parts. Intense dramatic moments alternate with quiet, meditative passages.
The NDR vocal ensemble perform with Concerto Köln conducted by Klass Stock, and tenor Sebastian Kohlhepp as the Evangelist.
Performed on 28 March 2026 at Hamburg’s Elbphilharmonie.

128856-000-A.title: Tabita Berglund and Julian Steckel - Sibelius, Shostakovich, Thorvaldsdottir
128856-000-A.desc: Norwegian conductor Tabita Berglund leads the Frankfurt Radio Symphony Orchestra in Anna Thorvaldsdottir's Archora, Wagner's Prelude to Tristan and Isolde, and Sibelius' Seventh Symphony. In the first half, Julian Steckel performs Shostakovich's Cello Concerto No. 1.

128858-004-A.title: Carlos Piñana & Ensemble - The Journey of Instruments
128858-004-A.desc: Our musical journey in the footsteps of the oud takes us all the way to Andalusia at the historic crossroads of Arab, Jewish and Christian cultures. Spanish guitarist Carlos Piñana blends traditional flamenco with modern virtuosity in his music.

128862-001-A.title: Alma Naidu - Levekusener Jazztage 2025
128862-001-A.desc: At the Leverkusen Jazz Festival, Alma Naidu performs on the piano- her favourite instrument- accompanied by a string quartet.

128862-002-A.title: RAH & The Ruffcats - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-002-A.desc: Rapturous Apollo Helios, aka R.A.H., has established himself as one of the most notable rappers, songwriters and singers in the German capital. He draws inspiration from his Nigerian roots and Afro-descendant music, ranging from hip hop to soul and Afrobeat. In Leverkusen, he takes to the stage alongside the Ruffcats.

128862-003-A.title: Keziah Jones - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-003-A.desc: In 1992, his first album Blufunk Is a Fact propelled him onto the international scene, carried by the global hit ‘Rhythm Is Love’. Over the course of his six studio albums, he added memorable tracks such as ‘Where Is Life’, ‘A Million Miles From Home’ and ‘Beautiful Emilie’ to his repertoire.

128862-004-A.title: Kokoroko - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-004-A.desc: Kokoroko was formed in 2014 by trumpeter and bugle player Sheila Maurice-Grey. During a trip to Kenya, the musician realised that the Afrobeat she grew up with in England had little in common with the sounds of the genre that became popular in Africa in the 1970s. This revelation shaped her musical approach.

128862-007-A.title: Jazz Sabbath - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-007-A.desc: Three British jazz musicians who rearrange Black Sabbath songs and cheekily claim to have written the heavy metal pioneers' hits: It's Jazz Sabbath!

128862-008-A.title: Vincent Peirani & Émile Parisien Quintet - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-008-A.desc: With ‘Living Being IV: Time Reflexion’, Vincent Peirani and his quintet have created an album deeply rooted in a reflection on our times. During the first lockdown, the accordionist took advantage of this enforced break to reflect on the impact of time on our existence and our view of the world.

128862-012-A.title: Lawrence - Leverkusener Jazztage 2025
128862-012-A.desc: This American soul-pop group, led by siblings Clyde and Gracie Lawrence, has appeared on Jimmy Fallon and Kelly Clarkson's television shows. They have shared the stage with the Rolling Stones, the Jonas Brothers and Vulfpeck, and have performed at major festivals such as Coachella and Bonnaroo.

128868-000-A.title: Putin's Shadow Army in Europe
128868-000-A.desc: Arson, sabotage and destabilisation operations: since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has stepped up its covert activities on European soil. This documentary provides an overview of the tactics employed by Putin’s shadow army in this hybrid confrontation.

128881-000-A.title: Return to Raqqa
128881-000-A.desc: It was one of the most high-profile kidnappings carried out by Islamic State: Nineteen journalists and humanitarian workers were captured in 2013 in Syria at the height of the civil war.  The story is told by Marc Marginedas, one of the journalists who came back alive.

128915-000-A.title: Alexandre Desplat - From Paris to Hollywood
128915-000-A.desc: At the helm of the Orchestre de Paris, the leading figure in film music conducts a selection of his Hollywood compositions with obvious pleasure, from The Tree of Life to Godzilla, to Wes Anderson's films.

129034-003-A.title: Gizmo Varillas - Reeperbahn Festival Collide 2025
129034-003-A.desc: Spanish-British musician Gizmo Varillas combines flamenco, folk, indie and global sounds to create his unique sound. His songs tell of loss, hope and the courage to make a new start.

129034-004-A.title: The Cavemen. - Reeperbahn Festival Collide 2025
129034-004-A.desc: Kingsley Okorie and Benjamin James, two brothers from Nigeria, combine nostalgia and contempory styles. With influences from highlife, Afrobeat, jazz and soul, they create their very own genre: "Highlife Fusion".

129335-000-A.title: Private Confessions
129335-000-A.desc: A woman confesses to her uncle the failure of her marriage and the adulterous affair she feels guilty about. Adapted by Liv Ullmann from a script by Ingmar Bergman for Swedish television, this drama offers a unique and cruel perspective on infidelity.

129378-000-A.title: Giorgio Moroder & Donna Summer - "I Feel Love"
129378-000-A.desc: A look back at the meeting in Munich in 1977 between composer and producer Giorgio Moroder and singer Donna Summer, which was to result, amongst other global hits, in the extraordinary “I Feel Love”.

129383-000-A.title: John Neumeier: The Nutcracker - Hamburg Ballet
129383-000-A.desc: Created in 1971, John Neumeier's ballet set to Tchaikovsky's music is now a classic performed all over the world. In his reinterpretation of The Nutcracker, the American choreographer transposes young Marie's initiation into the world of dance under the tutelage of ballet master Drosselmeier.

129386-000-A.title: Tchaikovsky: The Maid of Orleans - Dutch National Opera - Amsterdam
129386-000-A.desc: Charged with high treason, Joan of Arc must face her accusers. Dmitri Tcherniakov offers a contemporary retelling  of Tchaikovsky's least performed opera in the closed setting of a courtroom, with soprano Elena Stikhina in the title role.

129388-001-A.title: Friska Viljor - Rolling Stone Beach 2025
129388-001-A.desc: The duo Friska Viljor distils melancholic indie pop that makes even broken hearts want to dance. At the 2025 edition of the Rolling Stone Beach festival, these authentic and passionate artists will fill the beach with waves of melody, euphoria and well-being.

129388-002-A.title: We Are Scientists - Rolling Stone Beach 2025
129388-002-A.desc: Since the 2000s, the band We Are Scientists has been shaping the indie scene with hooks that are as sharp as they are ironic. At Rolling Stone Beach 2025, Keith Murray and Chris Cain deliver sunny indie rock, bursting with contagious energy and nods to pop culture, which immediately sets the Baltic Sea alight.

129388-003-A.title: Chuck Prophet & His Cumbia Shoes - Rolling Stone Beach 2025
129388-003-A.desc: The former guitarist of Green on Red now blends rock, Americana and Latin rhythms. With his band His Cumbia Shoes, he brings an irresistible mix of heartfelt lyrics and catchy funk, imbued with Californian sunshine, to Rolling Stone Beach 2025.

129388-006-A.title: Mogwai - Rolling Stone Beach 2025
129388-006-A.desc: For over 25 years, Mogwai has embodied both instrumental power and pure emotion. At Rolling Stone Beach 2025, the Scots unleash their hypnotic post-rock waves on Weissenhäuser beach. A concert to delight all the senses.

129423-000-A.title: The Songs of Joni Mitchel - With Yaël Naïm, Charlie Winston, Kate Staples...
129423-000-A.desc: Kate Stables, Jesca Hoop and Lail Arad perform the music of Joni Mitchell with a host of great musicians at the Casino de Paris in a celebration of the songwriting and political commitment of the great folk musician. With Yaël Naïm, Charlie Winston, and Delgrés.

129851-000-A.title: The Cinema Posters of Laurent Durieux
129851-000-A.desc: In demand by the great names of Hollywood, Belgian illustrator Laurent Durieux makes iconic cinema posters which capture the essence of famous films. This documentary extensively interviews the artist and looks closely at the subtle framing and hidden depths of his artworks for films such as Hitchcock’s “The Birds” and “Psycho” and Coppola’s “The Godfather” and “Apocalypse Now”.

129940-001-A.title: Zaz - Sounds Like Art - Mo Museum Vilnius
129940-001-A.desc: Zaz, famous for her song ‘je veux’, brings her infectious optimism to the MO Museum in Vilnius in the cold Lithuanian winter. The French singer-songwriter performs inside the museum amongst a collection of Lithuanian artworks dating from the 1960s to the present day.

129940-002-A.title: José González - Sounds Like Art - Moderna Museet, Stockholm
129940-002-A.desc: Sounds Like Art presents José González at the Moderna Museet in Stockholm. González plays a mixture of reflective new songs and classic fan favourites surrounded by the artworks of Carsten Höller, Maya Deren and Karol Radziszewski. González talks about his family's roots in political exile and his hopes for our common future.

129946-000-A.title: Antonia Bembo: Ercole Amante - Paris Opera
129946-000-A.desc: Hercules has fallen in love with his son's lover and refuses to accept her rejection... Venetian composer Antonia Bembo’s opera is a baroque jewel from a little-known female composer, written in France in 1707. This production, the first from the Paris Opera, is brought to the stage by Netia Jones and conducted by Leonardo Garcia-Alarcón.

129953-000-A.title: Clara Cuvé - Club Price Charles, Berlín
129953-000-A.desc: Melancholic, raw and full of energy. Clara Cuvé and ARTE invite you to a night dedicated to the heartbeat of Berlin: techno! At the Prince Charles in Kreuzberg, where bass meets concrete and sweat drips from the ceiling, the past and present of techno merge into a roaring whole.

130043-000-A.title: Jean-Michel Jarre - Live from Seville
130043-000-A.desc: Jean-Michel Jarre unveils an immersive concert-show in the heart of Seville's sumptuous Plaza de España, transformed into an exceptional stage setting for one evening.

130222-008-A.title: The Cinema of John Williams - Indiana Jones, E.T., Star Wars and more
130222-008-A.desc: The Radio France Philharmonic Orchestra, Bastien Stil and Nathan Mierdl transport the Radio France Auditorium into the spellbinding world of John Williams. From Jurassic Park to Harry Potter, rediscover legendary melodies from Hollywood cinema.

130321-000-A.title: Carolyn Carlson: Midnight Souls - Avignon - Palais des Papes
130321-000-A.desc: In the magnificent setting of the Popes’ palace in Avignon, dancers Hugo Marchand and Caroline Osmont perform a choreography by Carolyn Carlson with music by Philip Glass and René Aubry.

130440-000-A.title: Debt Fever
130440-000-A.desc: 0.005% of Swedes own 64% of the national GDP. Decades of deliberate economic decisions have led to these spectacular inequalities, and Sweden is far from being the only country with these disparities in wealth. Negative borrowing rates, inflation held at 2% at all costs, a spiralling property market... how did the Swedish economy go mad?

130478-001-A.title: Concert for Europe 2026 - With the Berliner Philharmoniker
130478-001-A.desc: The Berlin Philharmonic with Kirill Petrenko perform their annual concert for Europe, this time from the baroque magnificence of the Esterházy Palace in Austria. On the programme are works by Haydn, Stravinsky, Tchaikovsky and Beethoven.

130499-000-A.title: Frozen People Festival - Oulu 2026
130499-000-A.desc: Frozen People is a unique festival of electronic music and northern art on the frozen sea next to the Finnish city of Oulu. As part of Oulu2026 European Capital of Culture, the event brings together international and Finnish DJs Sanna Mun, Paula Koski, Schacke and Winter Games in truly arctic conditions. Welcome to the world’s iciest dancefloor!

130500-000-A.title: Stanislav Vavřínek conducts Brahms and Dvořák - La Folle Journée 2026
130500-000-A.desc: The Hradec Králové Philharmonic , an orchestra which specialises in Central European music, gives its debut at La Folle Journée festival in Nantes, France. The Czech ensemble performs music by Johannes Brahms and Antonin Dvořák, conducted by Stanislav Vavřínek.

130501-001-A.title: Classes and coaching #1 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-001-A.desc: The Prix de Lausanne has begun! Discover all the competitors  in the year’s international ballet competition for young dancers in the first day of classes and coaching.

130501-002-A.title: Classes and Coaching #2 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-002-A.desc: The first afternoon at the 54th Prix de Lausanne focuses on the 17-18 year-old candidates and the ballet classes.

130501-003-A.title: Classes and Coaching #3 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-003-A.desc: An early start for the participants in the Prix de Lausanne 2026, the programme includes lessons in classical dance for the girls in Group A (15-16 years) and coaching in contemporary variations for the boys in Group B (17-18 years).

130501-004-A.title: Classes and Coaching #4 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-004-A.desc: An afternoon with the girls of group B (17-18 years) and their classical and contemporary dance classes. This is followed by the boys of group A (15-16 years) in rehearsals for their contemporary variations.

130501-005-A.title: Classes and Coaching #5 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-005-A.desc: This third morning is all about classical dance: classical lessons for all girl groups and coaching of classical variations for girls A (15-16 years) and boys B (17-18 years).

130501-006-A.title: Classes and Coaching #6 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-006-A.desc: Classes and coaching sessions, classical and contemporary, girls and boys: This third afternoon session from the Théâtre de Beaulieu brings together all the diversity and excitement of the Prix de Lausanne 2026.

130501-007-A.title: Classes and Coaching #7 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-007-A.desc: On this last morning before the selections, the focus is on classical ballet lessons for all the boys and contemporary dance lessons for the girls in Group A (15-16 years). A last opportunity to work on details before things get serious.

130501-008-A.title: Classes and Coaching #8 - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-008-A.desc: The selections for the 54th Prix de Lausanne are right around the corner. Follow the girls in Groups A and B (aged 15-18) and the boys in Group B (aged 17-18) in their final classes and coaching sessions.

130501-009-A.title: Junior Selection - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-009-A.desc: After four days of classes and coaching, it's time for the younger candidates in the 54th Prix de Lausanne to take part in the selection rounds. Who among them will impress the jury?

130501-010-A.title: Senior Selections - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-010-A.desc: After four days of classes and coaching, it’s time for the older candidates (17-18) to face selection. Who will impress the jury and make it into the final?

130501-011-A.title: Finals - Prix de Lausanne 2026
130501-011-A.desc: After five intensive days of classes, coaching and selection, the last day of the 54th Prix de Lausanne means one thing: the final. Who will win the prestigious prizes on offer?

130502-000-A.title: Richard Bona - With the Frankfurt Radio Big Band
130502-000-A.desc: Richard Bona was born in Cameroon. He was 10 when he cobbled together his first bass out of a crate and bicycle brake cables. Now based in New York, he has won over audiences with his multi-instrumentalism and his singing in several languages. The Frankfurt Radio Big Band is conducted by Michael Philip Mossman.

130503-000-A.title: Sergey Khachatryan Performs Khachaturian - Alain Altinoglu & the Frankfurt Radio Symphony Orchestra
130503-000-A.desc:
Sergey Khachatryan and the Frankfurt Radio Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Alain Altinoglu, perform Aram Khatchatourian's Violin Concerto. Also on the programme, Igor Stravinsky's 'Rite of Spring'.



 Programme:
 Aram Khatchaturian - Adagio from Spartacus und Phrygia
 Aram Khatchaturian – Violin Concerto
 Igor Stravinsky – The Rite of Spring

130504-003-A.title: La Maestra 2026 - Semi-final - Part 1
130504-003-A.desc:
La Maestra is an international competition open to women conductors at the Philharmonie de Paris with the Paris Mozart Orchestra.
This year 16 candidates from all over the world compete in front of a jury led by acclaimed Ukrainian conductor Oksana Lyniv.  
In the semi finals, each candidate has 40 minutes to practice with the orchestra before performing two pieces: the accompaniment to a concerto and a passage from an opera.

130504-004-A.title: La Maestra 2026 - Semi-final - Part 2
130504-004-A.desc:
La Maestra is an international competition open to women conductors at the Philharmonie de Paris with the Paris Mozart Orchestra. This year 16 candidates from all over the world compete in front of a jury led by acclaimed Ukrainian conductor Oksana Lyniv.  
In the semi finals, each candidate has 40 minutes to practice with the orchestra before performing two pieces: the accompaniment to a concerto and a scene from an opera.

130504-005-A.title: La Maestra 2026 - The Final - Part 1
130504-005-A.desc: La Maestra is an international competition open to women conductors at the Philharmonie de Paris with the Paris Mozart Orchestra.The last four contenders have reached the final. Each conductor must present a work from the classical repertoire and a contemporary piece.

130504-006-A.title: La Maestra 2026 - The Final - Part 2
130504-006-A.desc:
With only 4.3% of orchestras having women conductors in 2019, La Maestra was launched to change that. This international competition at the Philharmonie de Paris with the Paris Mozart Orchestra gives the next generation of women conductors a chance to shine.
This year 16 candidates from all over the world compete in front of a jury led by acclaimed Ukrainian conductor Oksana Lyniv.  
The four finalists must interpret works by Stravinsky and Ravel.

130507-000-A.title: Tamino - Private Session
130507-000-A.desc: Filmed in the intimate surroundings of a Paris flat, Tamino impresses the audience with his beautiful and melancholic songs.

130508-000-A.title: Wagner, Saint-Saens and Debussy - Daniele Gatti & Gautier Capuçon
130508-000-A.desc: French and German romanticism are on the programme for the Staatskapelle Dresden orchestra with Wagner’s music inviting us to contemplate spirituality and ecstasy, Debussy’s evocative impressions of nature, and Saint-Saëns  sensual cello concerto with Gautier Capuçon as soloist.

130670-001-A.title: Spanish Fiesta - With Yutaka Sado
130670-001-A.desc: To celebrate the New Year, the Orchestre National de France with conductor Yutaka Sado  offer a journey to Spain with works by Rimsky-Korsakov, Bizet, and María Rodrigo.

130680-000-A.title: The Kooks - Columbia Theater, Berlin
130680-000-A.desc:
Every year ARTE organises a concert during the Berlin Film Festival. This year British indie band The kooks are invited to perform at the Columbia Theater, a converted cinema. They play old favourites and tracks from the seventh studio album, Never/Know.
Filmed on 15 February 2026 at the Columbia Theater, Berlin.

130681-000-A.title: Nuclear Deterrent - A Strategy for Europe
130681-000-A.desc: Until recently, Europe felt safe under the US nuclear umbrella. But since Russia invaded Ukraine and Donald Trump was re-elected, transatlantic relations have deteriorated. Experts are calling for Europe to focus more on its own nuclear deterrent. But what options do nations on the continent have?

130682-000-A.title: Belly of the Beast
130682-000-A.desc: When an unlikely duo discovers a pattern of illegal sterilizations in women’s prisons, they wage a near impossible battle against the Department of Corrections. Filmed over seven years with extraordinary access and intimate accounts from currently and formerly incarcerated people, Belly of the Beast exposes modern-day eugenics and reproductive injustice in California prisons.

131033-002-A.title: Sakari Orama conducts Saariaho and Mahler - With Verneri Pohjola
131033-002-A.desc: The Orchestre Philharmonique de Radio France and Finnish conductor Sakari Oramo celebrate the memory of composer Kaija Saariaho with a performance of one of her last works as well as Mahler's Symphony No. 5.

131033-004-A.title: Cristian Măcelaru Conducts Beethoven and Brahms - With Frank Peter Zimmermann
131033-004-A.desc: The Orchestre National de France, conducted by Cristian Măcelaru, perform Brahms Symphony No. 1 and Beethoven’s Violin Concerto with Frank Peter Zimmermann as soloist.

131034-000-A.title: Bar Avni Conducts Ravel, Milhaud & Bizet - With Ava Bahari
131034-000-A.desc: Two years after her triumph at the "La Maestra" conducting competition, Bar Avni is back on the podium: at the Philharmonie de Paris, she gives a concert of the highest calibre with the Orchestre de Paris and violinist Ava Bahari.

131035-000-A.title: Humo - A Flamenco Ballet by Rafaela Carrasco
131035-000-A.desc: Filmed at Seville’s Teatro de la Maestranza in Seville, Humo, (smoke) combines dance and song in a tribute to the 19th century cigar makers who also inspired the character of Carmen from the famous opera. Choreographer Rafaela Carrasco creates portraits that reveal diverse lives that often contradict clichés of the past and present.

131059-000-A.title: Eternal Disco - The Greatest Hits
131059-000-A.desc: ABBA, Michael Jackson, Boney M., Chic, Edwin Starr, Lio and Gloria Gaynor: the biggest stars of the 1970s and 1980s took turns appearing on the German TV programme “Musikladen”. Here’s a selection of these cult moments to let loose to classic disco hits!

131182-000-A.title: Etienne de Crécy - L'Ososphère 2026
131182-000-A.desc: A major figure in house music over the last 30 years, Etienne de Crécy is always reinvented his sound. For this very special performance, the French DJ gives a set to a joyous crowd from the main hall of Strasbourg’s central station.

131183-000-A.title: Mysteries of Lisbon
131183-000-A.desc:
Mysteries of Lisbon draws the viewer into a perpetual whirlwind of adventures and misadventures, coincidences and revelations, emotions and violent passions, acts of revenge, and thwarted or illicit loves, in a turbulent journey across Portugal, France, Italy, and Brazil.


In this Lisbon of intrigues and hidden identities, we encounter a gallery of characters who shape the destiny of Pedro da Silva, an orphan and boarding student at a religious school: Father Dinis, a former libertine aristocrat turned vigilante; a countess consumed by jealousy and thirsting for revenge; a bloodthirsty pirate who becomes a prosperous businessman.
All move through the history of the nineteenth century, accompanying our protagonist’s search for identity.

131394-000-A.title: Paris Sur Mesure - Saburo Teshigawara, Sayaka Shoji & Rihoko Sato at Champlâtreux Castle
131394-000-A.desc: For Paris Sur Mesure, the outstanding Japanese choreographer Saburo Teshigawara stages a performance at the 18th century Château de Champlâtreux with dancer Rihoko Sato and violinist Sayaka Shoji.

131503-000-A.title: Nureyev - A Ballet by Kirill Serebrennikov and Yuri Possokhov
131503-000-A.desc: An evocation of the life of one of the twentieth century’s most famous and talented dancers. Brought to the stage by Kirill Serebrennikov and choreographed by Yuri Possokhov, the Staatsballett production is the first creation of the work outside Russia.

131534-000-A.title: Ecce bombo
131534-000-A.desc: The film that marked Nanni Moretti’s breakthrough captures a generation of young Italians in the 1970s, caught between political ideals and existential boredom. It's a typical Moretti: political themes and personal stories are interwoven with subtle humour and (self-)irony, as he explores the peculiarities of human nature.

131537-000-A.title: Home Sweet Home
131537-000-A.desc: A retirement home in Brussels is run with military discipline; the residents are treated like naughty children and it eventually leads to a revolt. At the centre of it all is Jules, a spirited seventy-year-old who refuses to conform. His defiance ignites the rebellious spirit of several other elderly residents, whose mischief escalates into a wild uprising.

131538-000-A.title: Hush-a-Bye Baby
131538-000-A.desc: In a small town in Northern Ireland, a teenage schoolgirl becomes pregnant unexpectedly and faces a deep inner conflict due to the rigid moral standards of those around her. Hush a bye Baby tackles the controversial issue of pregnancy outside marriage and abortion in Northern Ireland during the turbulent 1980s through the love story of two teenagers.

131539-000-A.title: Pigskin
131539-000-A.desc:
An intimate journey, imbued with wonder, pop culture and a sense of lightness, yet underpinned by a secret, tormented sense of existential insecurity, oscillating between a wild collection of images and an understanding of reality. PIGSKIN was hailed as a cinematic event on its release: A film that constantly challenges conventions, innovates and offers a unique and fresh formal approach. It explores the passage of time, the daily lives of couples and the excitement of childhood.


Philippe Katerine's personal film is made up of twelve video sequences in which the singer- not yet a star- breathes new life into the world with a jubilant sense of freedom. A self-portrait that has achieved cult status.

131540-000-A.title: Brad Mehldau - With the Frankfurt Radio Big Band
131540-000-A.desc: Brad Mehldau is one of the most important jazz pianists of our time. The Frankfurt Radio Big Band and its composer in residence Darcy James Argue present some of Mehldau's pieces for the ensemble at Hamburg's Elbphilharmonie.

131543-000-A.title: The Divine Comedy - Cirque Royal, Brussels
131543-000-A.desc: The Divine Comedy bring all the suave elegance and humour of their orchestral pop to the Cirque Royal in Brussels. With frontman Neil Hannon at the top of his game, they introduce us to their thirteenth album: Rainy Sunday Afternoon.

131547-000-A.title: Last Man Standing - Suge Knight and The Murders of Biggie and Tupac
131547-000-A.desc: Suge Knight, the former CEO of legendary rap label Death Row Records is currently serving a 28-year prison sentence for manslaughter. This documentary looks at how Los Angeles gang culture came to dominate how the label was run and how it was this world that cost the lives of two of the biggest names in US rap: Tupac Shakur and Biggie Smalls.

131550-001-A.title: Brahms: Piano Concerto No. 1 - Yulianna Avdeeva in Vienna
131550-001-A.desc: Johannes Brahms spent the last 25 years of his life not far from where the Wiener Konzerthaus now stands. On 21 June 2026, the Vienna Symphony Orchestra and the young Russian pianist Yulianna Avdeeva perform the composer’s Piano Concerto No. 1 in D minor, Op. 15, under the baton of principal conductor Petr Popelka. Part of europiano, ARTE's special day celebrating the piano concerto across Europe.

132068-000-A.title: The Macaluso Sisters
132068-000-A.desc: Maria, Pinuccia, Lia, Katia and Antonella are five sisters living together in an apartment in Palermo. They make a living by renting out doves for ceremonies. A normal day at the beach turns tragic when Antonella has an accident. Her death turns the relationship between her sisters upside down for the rest of their lives.

132072-000-A.title: Smile at Last
132072-000-A.desc: Mari lives in a children’s home. Her mother is dead and her father is an alcoholic. These are the reasons why 16 year-old Mari was sent to the home, just like the other children and teenagers who live there. Mari cannot cope with their rough behaviour and cruel games and attempts to run away, but fails. Tauri, the son of a wealthy father, steps in to offer her his protection.

132074-000-A.title: Just Like Home
132074-000-A.desc: After living abroad for ten years, András returns to Hungary and tries to reconnect with Anna, his great love from the past, but she greets him coldly. Cut off from his friends and his job, he can no longer find a place in the life that once belonged to him. His bond with a rebellious, lonely young girl becomes a way for him to measure his own sense of rootlessness.

132076-000-A.title: Riddance
132076-000-A.desc: Jutka works as a labourer in a textile factory. At a party she meets András, a college student from a well-to-do family and out of shame she pretends to be his equal. When she realizes she has fallen in love with him, she decides to tell him the truth. But meeting his family prompts her to lie again in an attempt to appear different from who she really is.

132141-001-A.title: China Moses - Jazz sous les Pommiers 2026
132141-001-A.desc: The charismatic American singer China Moses is back after an eight-year hiatus. At the Jazz sous les Pommiers festival, she performs new material from her seventh album It's Complicated...

132141-002-A.title: Shai Maestro Quartet - Jazz sous les Pommiers 2026
132141-002-A.desc: Jazz pianist Shai Maestro presents his album The Guesthouse to festival-goers at Jazz sous les Pommiers. An expressive performance in a quartet which subtly combines piano, keyboard, double bass and drums.

132141-003-A.title: Marc Ribot Quartet - Jazz sous les Pommiers 2026
132141-003-A.desc:
American guitarist and composer Marc Ribot gives a rare performance in Europe at the Jazz sous les Pommiers Festival with his ensemble: Marc Ribot Quartet: Hurry Red Telephone.

132141-004-A.title: Albin de la Simone, Jozef Dumoulin & Fabrice Moreau - Jazz sous les Pommiers 2026
132141-004-A.desc: Together with Jozef Dumoulin and Fabrice Moreau, Albin de la Simone ventures into a jazzy reinterpretation of his repertoire of French song at the Jazz sous les Pommiers festival in Normandy.

132143-001-A.title: Carl Craig B2B Seth Troxler - Nuits Sonores 2026
132143-001-A.desc: Lyon’s Nuit Sonores festival welcomes two living legends of Detroit techno to the party: Carl Craig, a towering figure in electronic music since the 1980s and Seth Troxler, one of the world’s most popular DJs according to Resident Advisor.

132143-002-A.title: Anetha B2B Vel - Nuits Sonores 2026
132143-002-A.desc: Nuits Sonores festival features a French premiere: a unique B2B set with DJs Anetha and Vel. The long-time partners in crime let loose on a festival known for its enthusiastic crowds should make for a legendary set.

132143-003-A.title: Nathan Fake - Nuits Sonores 2026
132143-003-A.desc: Nathan Fake returns to the Nuits Sonores festival after the release of the British electronic musician’s seventh studio album, Evaporator.

132143-004-A.title: Skream & Benga - Nuits Sonores 2026
132143-004-A.desc: The comeback of Skream & Benga marks the return of one of the most influential duos in electronic music. The two giants bring the house down at Nuits Sonores, giving festivalgoers the best of British dubstep.

132143-005-A.title: DJ_Dave - Nuits Sonores 2026
132143-005-A.desc: Dancing to the machine: Welcome to the algorave where live coding supplies the beats. American DJ_Dave gives a master class in the discipline at the Nuits Sonores festival.

132145-000-A.title: Only The Poets - Leipzig, 2026
132145-000-A.desc: 2026 is the year for Only the Poets. Hailing from Reading, UK, their debut studio album And I’d Do It Again has taken the charts by storm. Here they perform in Leipzig at the LitPop Party.

132363-000-A.title: Iran: The Internal War
132363-000-A.desc: From the January 2026 uprising, which was brutally suppressed, to the resumption of US air strikes on Iran, this documentary – filmed in secret – gives a voice to a people caught in the crossfire.
