132692-046-A.title: Napalm Death - Hellfest 2026
132692-046-A.desc: Once a year, the idyllic little French town of Clisson is transformed into the European capital of metal. This is the real deal. Get ready to be blown away by a tidal wave of heavy metal!

132692-050-A.title: Bring Me The Horizon - Hellfest 2026
132692-050-A.desc: Bring Me The Horizon have come of age and are more motivated than ever, the British band bring their power metalcore to the main stage at Hellfest.

132694-000-A.title: Kim Gordon - Le Trianon, Paris
132694-000-A.desc: Rock icon Kim Gordon unleashes the unbridled energy of her brand new album Play Meat at the Trianon in Paris . An explosive mixture of fast-paced music, socially critical lyrics and black humour.

132897-003-A.title: europiano - The Most Beautiful Piano Concertos from Across Europe
132897-003-A.desc: Join us on an inspiring journey across Europe and through the central repertoire of the piano concerto. On 21 June 2026 we travel from city to city where leading international pianists play some of the most beautiful piano concertos accompanied by renowned orchestras led by acclaimed conductors.

133114-000-A.title: Les hautes solitudes
133114-000-A.desc:
“The idea was to create outtakes from a film that didn't exist, starring Jean Seberg. So I conceived "Les Hautes Solitudes" as something very raw in its portrayal of her face.”
- Philippe Garrel

133190-000-A.title: Budapest Festival Orchestra - Kissinger Sommer Opening Concert
133190-000-A.desc: It was during one of the rare happy periods of his life that Schumann composed his third symphony and a palpable joy can be heard in this masterpiece. In contrast Mozart's Piano Concerto No. 24 is, like many of the composer's minor-key pieces, imbued with a profound melancholy. Accomplished pianist Elisabeth Leonskaja is soloist with the Budapest Festival Orchestra conducted by its founder Iván Fischer.

RC-014120.title: Escape to a Hut in the Pyrenees - 360° GEO
RC-014120.desc: For the past fourteen years, Brice has taken his herd of cows to graze in vast pastures in the mountains, five months out of the year. He loves to run to the point of exhaustion in the Pyrenees, across the steep slopes, ridges and rocks at 3000 metres above sea level. It's a spartan, lonely existence, but for Brice, it is the definition of happiness.

RC-015349.title: Palimpsest - The Story of a Name
RC-015349.desc: Canadian editor and director Mary Stephen, born in Hongkong to a Chinese family, tries to find out why she has an English family name. Her film is a journey of discovery into the past and a questioning of the desire to rewrite one’s history.

RC-015903.title: Gaël Faye - Paris Louvre
RC-015903.desc: The power of flow versus the power of waves: At the Louvre, in front of Géricault's gigantic canvas, Gaël Faye reinterprets his songs in subtle arrangements for piano and strings. A mesmerising event.

RC-016485.title: Gustave Charpentier: Louise - Festival d'Aix-en-Provence
RC-016485.desc:
Escaping from her stifling working-class family, the young Louise joins her penniless lover Julien in Paris. But disillusionment will soon set in... Christof Loy’s new reading of Gustave Charpentier’s opera is brought to life by soprano Elsa Dreisig.

During the presenter Saskia de Ville’s introduction, third-person subtitles allow non-French-speaking viewers to follow her presentation. De Ville contextualises the opera and discusses its significance with the artistic team.

RC-016709.title: Gizmo Varillas - Reeperbahn Festival Collide 2025
RC-016709.desc: Spanish-British musician Gizmo Varillas combines flamenco, folk, indie and global sounds to create his unique sound. His songs tell of loss, hope and the courage to make a new start.

RC-016979.title: Zelensky: A Decisive Year?
RC-016979.desc: This documentary follows Volodymyr Zelensky through a year that takes him to his political, personal and moral limits: In 2025, Ukraine is hit by incessant attacks, the army is exhausted and society is fraying at the seams. Between military setbacks, accusations of corruption and risky diplomatic negotiations, the Ukrainian president struggles… Zelensky has never been under so much pressure. Will he prevail?

RC-017162.title: The Butcher
RC-017162.desc: Hélène, a schoolteacher, meets Paul, a butcher and veteran of the French colonial wars. In their rural community, young women are turning up dead. A thriller by Claude Chabrol, starring Jean Yanne and Stéphane Audran.

RC-017627.title: Richard Bona - With the Frankfurt Radio Big Band
RC-017627.desc: Richard Bona was born in Cameroon. He was 10 when he cobbled together his first bass out of a crate and bicycle brake cables. Now based in New York, he has won over audiences with his multi-instrumentalism and his singing in several languages. The Frankfurt Radio Big Band is conducted by Michael Philip Mossman.

RC-018561.title: Kae Tempest - Echoes with Jehnny Beth
RC-018561.desc: A major figure in British spoken word, Kae Tempest performs tracks from his new album, ‘Self Titled’. He delivers a sharp flow over electric melodies that are both soaring and raw, revealing dark, committed, uncompromising poetry – an X-ray of contemporary England.

RC-019389.title: Tenebræ Responsoria - Madrid International Festival of Sacred Art
RC-019389.desc: Madrid's International Festival of Sacred Music (FIAS) offers a unique international program every year at various venues around the Spanish capital. The venue for this concert is the San Miguel basilica for a performance of Tomás Luis de Victoria's Tenebræ Responsoria by Cantoría, a young Spanish ensemble with an international following.

RC-019390.title: Spanish Fiesta - With Yutaka Sado
RC-019390.desc: To celebrate the New Year, the Orchestre National de France with conductor Yutaka Sado  offer a journey to Spain with works by Rimsky-Korsakov, Bizet, and María Rodrigo.

RC-019796.title: Raye & Guests - Montreux Jazz Festival 2026
RC-019796.desc: Marking the opening of the 60th edition of the Montreux Jazz Festival, RAYE invites a host of international pop stars to join her on stage for an evening of music.

RC-020068.title: Paris Sur Mesure - Saburo Teshigawara, Sayaka Shoji & Rihoko Sato at Champlâtreux Castle
RC-020068.desc: For Paris Sur Mesure, the outstanding Japanese choreographer Saburo Teshigawara stages a performance at the 18th century Château de Champlâtreux with dancer Rihoko Sato and violinist Sayaka Shoji.

RC-020443.title: Wagner, Saint-Saens and Debussy - Daniele Gatti & Gautier Capuçon
RC-020443.desc: French and German romanticism are on the programme for the Staatskapelle Dresden orchestra with Wagner’s music inviting us to contemplate spirituality and ecstasy, Debussy’s evocative impressions of nature, and Saint-Saëns  sensual cello concerto with Gautier Capuçon as soloist.

RC-021158.title: Anetha B2B Vel - Nuits Sonores 2026
RC-021158.desc: Nuits Sonores festival features a French premiere: a unique B2B set with DJs Anetha and Vel. The long-time partners in crime let loose on a festival known for its enthusiastic crowds should make for a legendary set.

RC-021759.title: The Split (1/6) - Season 1
RC-021759.desc: A brilliant London divorce lawyer begins to have doubts about her own marriage. A multifaceted look at the world of divorce and a sensitive family portrait from the creator of ‘The Hour’. Episode 1: Hannah resists the advances of a former lover and, together with her sisters, faces her father’s return.

RC-021976.title: Paris Sur Mesure - Saburo Teshigawara, Sayaka Shoji & Rihoko Sato at Champlâtreux Castle
RC-021976.desc: For Paris Sur Mesure, the outstanding Japanese choreographer Saburo Teshigawara stages a performance at the 18th century Château de Champlâtreux with dancer Rihoko Sato and violinist Sayaka Shoji.

RC-022090.title: Humo - A Flamenco Ballet by Rafaela Carrasco
RC-022090.desc: Filmed at Seville’s Teatro de la Maestranza in Seville, Humo, (smoke) combines dance and song in a tribute to the 19th century cigar makers who also inspired the character of Carmen from the famous opera. Choreographer Rafaela Carrasco creates portraits that reveal diverse lives that often contradict clichés of the past and present.

RC-022826.title: Ogazón - Stone Techno 2025
RC-022826.desc: In recent years, Berlin-based DJ Ogazón has made a name for herself around the world. With unerring flair, she delivers stripped-down techno that is as hypnotic as it is percussive, with forays into dub. At the Stone Techno Festival 2025, she provides a captivating and euphoric soundscape for the audience.

RC-023040.title: The Book of My LIfe - With Francesca Melandri
RC-023040.desc: "Great literature is about creating this miracle of empathy between the writer and the reader. And this miracle of empathy is the greatest political value of literature". Francesca Melandri is one of the best-known voices in contemporary Italian literature. With Jagoda Marinic, she talks about her free-spirited childhood and her life before she began writing books.

RC-023541.title: Bar Avni Conducts Ravel, Milhaud & Bizet - With Ava Bahari
RC-023541.desc: Two years after her triumph at the "La Maestra" conducting competition, Bar Avni is back on the podium: at the Philharmonie de Paris, she gives a concert of the highest calibre with the Orchestre de Paris and violinist Ava Bahari.

RC-023743.title: Ship of the Dead
RC-023743.desc: Philip Gale, a young American sailor, signs onto the Yorikke, a decrepit freighter crewed by outcasts. What begins as a quest for freedom becomes a brutal ordeal of hard labour, inhumane treatment, and grim revelations – especially when a disturbing secret is found in the ship’s cargo. A gritty seafaring tale that launched Horst Buchholz’s path to Hollywood.

RC-024039.title: Alexandre Desplat - From Paris to Hollywood
RC-024039.desc: At the helm of the Orchestre de Paris, the leading figure in film music conducts a selection of his Hollywood compositions with obvious pleasure, from The Tree of Life to Godzilla, to Wes Anderson's films.

RC-024263.title: The Battle of Chile (1/3) - The Insurrection of the Bourgeoisie
RC-024263.desc:
Chilean president Salvador Allende wanted to modernise his country and fight poverty. But conservative groups organised a series of wildcat strikes and the White House put pressure on Chile's economy. When they realised that they could not eliminate the president by legal means, they prepared a coup d'état. 

This bold three-part work of cinema vérité captures the many voices of Chilean society in a riveting account of the fall of the Allende government in 1973.

RC-024282.title: Sex Slaves Of The Catholic Church
RC-024282.desc: Church and sexual abuse of minors has long been an issue. But in addition to the paedophilia scandals, other revelations are shaking the Catholic Church: allegations from across the world of nuns being sexually abused by priests. In February 2019, Pope Francis acknowledged the abuse for the first time. This documentary gives an insight into an issue in the Catholic Church that has remained largely unreported.

RC-024713.title: The Long War - Iran - Israel - USA - The Origins of the Conflict
RC-024713.desc: How did Iran become the sworn enemy of Israel and the United States, despite being allies of Iran’s Shah in the 1970s? A look at shifting geopolitics and war in a troubled region.

RC-024861.title: Ploughing Tilling Fighting: A History of Peasants - The Beginning (1/4)
RC-024861.desc: Many Europeans are probably descended from medieval peasants, but we know remarkably little about the lives of the peasantry. Often derided and misunderstood, this four-part documentary tells the story of these people at the bottom of the social scale from the early Middle Ages onwards.

RC-024968.title: Zaz - Sounds Like Art - Mo Museum Vilnius
RC-024968.desc: Zaz, famous for her song ‘je veux’, brings her infectious optimism to the MO Museum in Vilnius in the cold Lithuanian winter. The French singer-songwriter performs inside the museum amongst a collection of Lithuanian artworks dating from the 1960s to the present day.

RC-025017.title: Zaz - Sounds Like Art - Mo Museum Vilnius
RC-025017.desc: Zaz, famous for her song ‘je veux’, brings her infectious optimism to the MO Museum in Vilnius in the cold Lithuanian winter. The French singer-songwriter performs inside the museum amongst a collection of Lithuanian artworks dating from the 1960s to the present day.

RC-025079.title: Brad Mehldau - With the Frankfurt Radio Big Band
RC-025079.desc: Brad Mehldau is one of the most important jazz pianists of our time. The Frankfurt Radio Big Band and its composer in residence Darcy James Argue present some of Mehldau's pieces for the ensemble at Hamburg's Elbphilharmonie.

RC-025612.title: Iron Curtain: Tales of Soviet Occupation (1/3) - Satellite States
RC-025612.desc: How did the Soviet Union impose its communist ideology on central and eastern Europe after the Second World War? From 1945 until the building of the Berlin Wall in 1961, citizens of Poland, Hungary, East Germany and Czechoslovakia tell their stories of how their countries were occupied and transformed into communist states.

RC-025786.title: 14: Diaries of the Great War - The Abyss (1/8)
RC-025786.desc: We follow 14-year-old Marina Yurlova who joins her father, Cossack Colonel Yurlov, on a train to the front. Artist Käthe Kollwitz and her husband must accept their 17-year-old son’s decision to enlist. The Austrian Karl Kasser is sent to the front despite an injury and ten-year-old Yves Congar from Sedan expects France to win quickly. He feels safe in his family until German troops arrive on the doorstep.

RC-025927.title: Three Women from Mariupol
RC-025927.desc: Like her mother and daughter, director Svitlana Lishchynska grew up in Mariupol, a city in eastern Ukraine that has had cultural links with Russia for centuries. Three generations of women are confronted with the upheaval of war in a film about shifting identities in Ukraine.

RC-026042.title: China Moses - Jazz sous les Pommiers 2026
RC-026042.desc: The charismatic American singer China Moses is back after an eight-year hiatus. At the Jazz sous les Pommiers festival, she performs new material from her seventh album It's Complicated...

RC-026203.title: Dax J - Szczecin Recordings
RC-026203.desc: Britain's Dax J is one of the biggest names on today's techno scene. His rave-infused style revisits classic 90s techno with energy and modernity. A regular at the world's biggest clubs and festivals, his sets combine speed, intensity and remarkable technical mastery.

RC-026932.title: Mysterious Treasures of Nigeria - Nok Terracotta Sculptures
RC-026932.desc: Archaeologists in Nigeria study the remains of little-understood civilisations. In this episode: the Nok civilisation produced fascinating terracotta figurines, rarely found in their original archaeological setting.

RC-027197.title: Patrick Melrose (1/5) - Bad News
RC-027197.desc: After the death of his father, an aristocrat with an excessive lifestyle decides to face up to his problems and the traumas of his past. An elegant mixture of humour and tragedy, carried by the multi-faceted Benedict Cumberbatch. Episode one: On the day he learns that his father has died, Patrick is otherwise disposed…

RC-027201.title: European Emigration: Forgotten Stories (1/3) - Into A New World
RC-027201.desc: Between 1618 and 1748, thousands of German-speaking people fled to North America and Eastern Europe to escape famine, disease and religious persecution. A huge refugee camp was established in London. European rulers began to compete for new settlers, making refugees a political weapon.

RC-027232.title: Putin's Secret Weapons (1/2)
RC-027232.desc: The Russian army never seems to run out of weapons. After four years of aggression against Ukraine, they still have significant stocks and have been able to develop new hypersonic weapons that could strike European capitals in minutes. How has the world’s most sanctioned country been able to do this?

RC-027320.title: The Fabulous History of Money (1/4) - A Revolutionary Idea
RC-027320.desc: Gold, silver, bronze or paper: money tells the story of 2,700 years of human history. An essential tool for trade and a symbol of power, it fuelled the expansion of empires and changed the course of civilisations. From ancient cowrie shells to the rise of the first banknotes and cryptocurrencies, this four-part series uncovers the role of money in history.

RC-027513.title: Twin Peaks - Season 1 (1/8) - Pilot
RC-027513.desc: David Lynch's masterpiece of television is a unique drama that takes us beneath the surface of a small American town. In this feature-length pilot episode: The community of Twin Peaks is shocked by the discovery of the body of Laura Palmer, a popular high school student.

RC-027575.title: China - Making a Nation (1/2) - The Blue Sun
RC-027575.desc: After the fall of the Qing dynasty in 1911, China faced an historic turning point: How could the country find its way in the modern world? This two-part documentary shows how for over a century, China has been building itself up to repel foreign influence and to create a strong nation.

RC-027645.title: José González - Sounds Like Art - Moderna Museet, Stockholm
RC-027645.desc: Sounds Like Art presents José González at the Moderna Museet in Stockholm. González plays a mixture of reflective new songs and classic fan favourites surrounded by the artworks of Carsten Höller, Maya Deren and Karol Radziszewski. González talks about his family's roots in political exile and his hopes for our common future.

RC-027683.title: Far-Right Terrorism (1/2)
RC-027683.desc: Often underestimated, far-right terrorism is a real and present threat in Europe and the world. This investigation goes deep into the world of extreme groups that could be organising major attacks.

RC-027698.title: French Soldiers of the Third Reich (1/2)
RC-027698.desc: Between 1941 and 1945 around 30,000 French citizens voluntarily joined the German army and engaged in terrible war crimes in central and eastern Europe. This documentary explores a little-known chapter in the story of the second world war and France’s collaboration with Nazi Germany.

RC-027727.title: Marilyn Monroe: Fame at any Price (1/3)
RC-027727.desc: Norma Jeane Baker grew up in difficult circumstances. Her mother and grandmother suffer from depression; her father only existed as a photograph. When an early marriage does not bring the security she craves, her only consolation is the dream of a career in the movies…

RC-027733.title: How Our Brain Tricks Us (1/2) - Me and My Brain
RC-027733.desc: Our brain uses mental shortcuts so that we can filter the countless stimuli in our environment and react to them. However, this strength of the brain is also its weakness. Using optical illusions and magic tricks, Albert Moukheiber demonstrates in the first episode of the two-part documentary that what we think is real is not always real.

RC-027806.title: Famous Orchestras - The Vienna Philharmonic
RC-027806.desc: The Wiener Philharmoniker is a very special orchestra hailing from the city that can perhaps claim to be the world capital of classical music. Steeped in tradition, and with a very specific sound, it is no surprise that eminent conductor Leonard Bernstein thought that the Vienna Philharmonic was the world’s best orchestra

RC-027892.title: Hush-a-Bye Baby
RC-027892.desc: In a small town in Northern Ireland, a teenage schoolgirl becomes pregnant unexpectedly and faces a deep inner conflict due to the rigid moral standards of those around her. Hush a bye Baby tackles the controversial issue of pregnancy outside marriage and abortion in Northern Ireland during the turbulent 1980s through the love story of two teenagers.

RC-027930.title: America Who Are You? (1/2) - A History of the American Mindset
RC-027930.desc: To mark the 250th anniversary of US independence, this documentary examines the values upon which the American nation is founded – a nation now in the midst of Trump’s second presidential term – and is structured around four main themes: the ideal of freedom, the prominence of religion, the American dream and love of country.
